chapters.title: Chapter 137: News
chapters.body:
Chapter 137: News

Chapter 137: News

Early dawn, Arsen Foster woke up in his single apartment to the crisp sounds of chirping birds.

The sun shone on his face through the windows, bringing an air of warmth to the cool morning.

“Phew!”

Arsen gently pushed open the window and exhaled the sleep built up in him overnight and stretched lazily afterward.

The man who was born with a silver spoon found a surprising amount of leisure and excitement that he had been missing for a while in a plain little apartment like this one.

Before properly getting up, Arsen could not resist the urge to look at his arms again – this was the first thing he did every morning.

He saw two well-built, delicate, and clean arms, their fingers were slender and full of strength with hardly a trace of wrinkles on them.

Nobody would have believed you if you said that this was a pair of arms that belonged to a sixty-five-year-old man.

Arson could clearly remember that just three months ago, these arms were filled with marks left behind by the passage of time despite him attentively taking care of them over the years.

It was as if the sands of time flowed backward on these arms of his.

Arsen excitedly hopped down the bed and twisted his body in all sorts of ways. He felt endless energy surging out of his body, a stark contrast to his previous experience where the slightest movement made his body scream with soreness.

After feeling the youthfulness surging across his body, Arsen hummed to the tune of a rock song from the ’70s while he stepped into the bathroom.

A Chinese man in his mid-forties appeared in the bathroom mirror. The figure had a head of long brown hair, sharp eyes like a falcon, and well-defined features resembling a sculpture.

Arsen could not contain a soft chuckle when he saw his long lost youthful appearance in the mirror.

His soft chuckle slowly grew louder and louder, soon, he had broken into a crazy fit of laughter which echoed across the walls of the apartment...

Half an hour later, Arsen dressed himself in a white tracksuit and headed downstairs all dressed up for the day.

This place was the Blackwatch Biotechnology Research Center. The place Arsen stayed in was the apartments provided by the research center for visitors.

Arsen was one of the financial oligarchs who had received a direct invitation from Chen Chen. He was a direct member of the Foster family who was none other than the younger brother of the head of the family, Norman Foster.

As usual, Arsen got up around seven in the morning and went to the cafeteria for his breakfast. After that, he went to the research building for his daily anti-aging treatment.

Chen Chen was probably the only person who could get a bunch of royalty who held the reins of the world to get up and head to the cafeteria for breakfast on their own.

Even if the cafeteria was only opened to the professors in the research teams, it still failed to meet the standards of these individuals.

Still, these financial oligarchs were hardly bothered by this. How bad could it be? They were regaining their youth after all.

The moment Arsen stepped into the cafeteria, several people could not stop themselves from craning their necks toward him. Furthermore, several ladies lit up immediately.

The mid-forties was the period where men’s mature charm was at its peak. Back in his days, Arsen’s looks and charm were widely acknowledged across the Britain royalty circle. He had countless passionate relationships with numerous ladies.

“Good morning, ladies. Gentlemen.”

Arsen politely greeted the people before sitting down at an unoccupied table.

“Mr. Arsen, what would you like to have for breakfast today?” a Chinese waiter approached and asked politely.

“I’ll have a basket of dumplings, a bowl of soy milk, and two dough sticks, thank you.” Arsen flipped through the menu and ordered several Chinese cuisines. He also spoke in Chinese when he ordered, making an impression on the crowd.

“One moment.” The waiter immediately began preparing.

“Arsen, you’re eating Chinese again,” the red-bearded Tom sitting on the next table commented candidly.

“They might not be the healthiest, but they taste splendid. I recommend you give it a try.”

Arsen said with a smile, “The heavens grant us the chance to regain our youth. In that case, it’s about time to make up for some past regrets, wouldn’t you say so buddy?”

“Hmmph, I still haven’t settled the score from back then with you either.” Tom shot Arsen a look and made no further comments.

When the waiter carefully brought the food to Arsen, he ceremoniously wrapped a scarf in front of his chest as usual, even though he was eating Chinese cuisine. After that, he picked up a dumpling with a fork and took a soft bite.

The thick aroma of meat instantly swarmed his mouth. Arsen had to blow on his red-hot lips and panted at the heat.

“Excuse me, may I sit here?”

As Arsen was gritting his teeth from the heat, he heard a familiar voice and immediately looked up. He met the eyes of a young Chinese man standing by the opposite side of the table.

Arsen could not be any more familiar with this person. He was the brains behind the “Mankind Anti-aging Project” and the one who single-handedly brought the research center to reality. Even though they were the ones paying, not a single soul in the Disciple Council wanted to get on the young man’s bad side.

“So, it’s Mr. Chen.”

Arsen smiled awkwardly and made an inviting gesture. “Come on, take a seat, the seat is unoccupied.”

“Thank you.”

Chen Chen sat opposite Arsen and snapped his finger, a waiter quickly approached. “Boss, what would you like to order for breakfast?”

Chen Chen thought for a moment before raising his finger and speaking in Chinese, “I’ll have a bowl of Dandan noodle[1], two lard cakes, and lobster fried with green onions. Make sure to not put in seasoning powder.”

“You’re Chinese but you’re not a fan of seasoning powder?” Arsen asked with a sense of intrigue.

“In moderation, I suppose.”

Chen Chen nodded. “The sodium glutamate in seasoning powder breaks down into glutamate. Consumed in excess, the solution catalyzed by enzymes is transformed into an inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain.”

Chen Chen pointed at his head. “This inhibitory neurotransmitter will put your nerve functions in an inhibited state, affecting your thoughts...”

“I see!”

Even though he did not understand a single word, Arsen smiled and nodded. “I never thought that seasoning powder could be this harmful, I suppose I should cut down on them in the future.”

After everyone finished their meal, they headed to the research center building together.

“I haven’t congratulated everyone here yet.”

While the crowd was chatting among themselves in a low voice, Chen Chen spoke suddenly, “Everyone, we’ll be conducting the final tests today. If no problems arise, then we can officially announce that the ‘Age-reversal treatment’ is an unqualified success.”

The group took a brief moment to process what Chen Chen just said before the joy started to surface in their eyes.

Despite Chen Chen’s best attempts at providing the best facilities possible, it did not change the fact that they were in Africa which was most lacking in natural resources. There was not much by way of entertainment or anything else, they were at their limit at this point.

This was only one of many reasons. What gave them the most pleasure was in knowing that being allowed to leave meant that the “Age-reversal treatment” they had undergone was a success.

These old men who were on the eve of their lives had extended their lives by thirty years!

This was not a life sustained by lying on a sickbed, hooked up to all sorts of devices. This was a proper chance to live again, abandoning their old and corroding body with a brand new, youthful shell!

Their eyes glowed brightly at the suggestion. This was a glowing eagerness for what the future had in stock for them...

“On top of that, I have one more good news to share with you all.”

Chen Chen paused briefly before continuing. “I know that some of you have concerns that you’ll continue to be plagued by Alzheimer’s disease despite regaining a youthful body. However, from now today onward, there’s no longer a need to worry about such disease.”

“We’ve heard about this as well.”

Tom Wilson turned to him. “I heard you discovered a therapy procedure that was able to contain the worsening effects of Alzheimer’s disease?”

“Your information network is truly impressive.”

Chen Chen answered with a swift smile, “I’ve only announced this in the Chinese Academy of Sciences and haven’t even published it in Nature. And yet, you already found out about it. However, that’s not the good news I was referring to.”

“It’s not?” Tom was surprised.

“The good news I’m talking about concerns a breakthrough in Alzheimer’s disease reversal treatment project research. In three years, we’ll be able to develop a specialized drug to completely cure Alzheimer’s disease.”

Chen Chen’s remark was met with astonishment. “Are you sure you’re not messing with us here? I thought even the cause of the disease is still largely unknown?”

“According to our latest findings, Alzheimer’s disease is a form of deterioration of the human body functionalities which in turn leads to internal deterioration. This is a disease caused by a chain reaction.”

Chen Chen approached the entrance of the research building before concluding. “Therefore, the ‘Age-reversal treatment’ also achieves an effect of suppressing Alzheimer’s disease to a certain degree. As for the true method of curing it, I will not be disclosing it for now. I just hope that everyone is ready for it...

“In three years at most, a new line of drugs will be born to bring an end to the plague of the century!”

[1] A Chinese dish from Sichuan

chapters.title: Chapter 138: Premiere
chapters.body:
Chapter 138: Premiere

Chapter 138: Premiere

They had their doubts about Chen Chen’s final claim.

Ever since the dawn of mankind, there had been two things that countless men would willingly spend their whole lives pursuing.

One was power, the second was resources.

However, there was no such thing as definite power in the world. Therefore, the worship for power eventually took on the form of a “community” strength.

With the roots of early society taking shape, the concept of communal strength evolved until the concept of “authority” was birthed.

Resources, on the other hand, were indispensable for human beings to ensure both their survival and continuous reproduction in ancient times. This included animal pelts, stone tools, prey, and even women to a certain extent.

Under harsh conditions, you stand a chance to live a better life than others and reproduce if you obtain more resources.

As the structure of human society grew ever complex, the embodiment of resources slowly morphed into abstract subjects such as money. Still, its essence remained the same.

This was to say that authority and money were the two things that modern human society seek above all else.

With authority, one would be able to establish one’s position over millions. With money, one would be able to easily obtain things that regular people could only dream of.

Whether it was wealth or authority, upon reaching a certain stage, one would be able to be exposed to the corresponding circles and obtain secrets that regular people would never discover in their entire lifetimes.

These secrets concerned the security and interests of the political system and were only known to a selection of people for a certain period.

This included matters of politics, economy, military affairs, finances, technology, and many other information that the Federation could not risk disclosing.

Take finances for example. By knowing ahead of time of a failure of a certain project of a certain company, one should be able to determine that the company’s stock should experience a drop in a few days.

Or in the case of technology, by knowing of a successfully deployed product ahead of time, preparations could be made to anticipate the changes the new gadget could cause in the market.

Having access to these pieces of information offered a substantial advantage whether it was for profit accumulation or risk aversion.

From the perspective of the ones on top who were in charge of this confidential information, regular investors were merely livestock ripe for slaughter.

At this moment, what Chen Chen was doing was breaking the information to these royal families ahead of time. It was a secret that could not be obtained by any other parties in the Federation.

The moment AD-001 made its debut, it was the acetylcholinesterase inhibitor drugs or NMDA receptor antagonist drugs on the market. Perhaps it could be international pharmaceutical companies such as Novartis, Johnson & Johnson, Biogen, Eli Lilly, Pfizer, Roche, Merck, and so on...

More than two decades of patented technology and investments totaling above 600 billion U.S. dollars was going to turn into nothing but scrap papers.

When that happened, what sort of ground-breaking revolution was going to occur in the international pharmaceutical market?

There was a good reason Chen Chen chose to disclose this information. He was going to use the resources available to him to section out a network for himself.

Should they believe what he told them, they should know to act accordingly upon being approached by pharmaceutical markets for investment to avoid losing everything three years later.

It was not a big deal even if they chose to not pay mind to it. By the time everything unfolds three years later, these people present here should be able to verify the validity of the information they had received ahead of time. With that, it should boost the prestige of God’s Forbidden Zone when they disclose similar information again.

Soon enough, God’s Forbidden Zone would become a unique network for relaying useful information. This was the reason Chen Chen had disclosed insider’s information to them...

Back to the present.

Several days later, the first group of family members of the Disciple Council concluded their successful treatment. The flames of life had been reignited for this group of elder members averaging at above sixty-five-years-old as they had returned to their state thirty years ago.

They had experienced a similar recovery in terms of physical fitness as well. Their physical capabilities had returned to the state of their younger self. It was practically the same as regular thirty-year-old adults.

It was as if the passage of time had been reversed.

After escorting them to the plane one by one, Chen Chen recognized that this marked the end of the scouting phase for the eleven families.

Next up, he was going to have to prepare to welcome the head of family and core members of the respective families.

This was also a sign of the eleven families officially taking the side of God’s Forbidden Zone. There should be no signs of conflict for a short period to come.

Chen Chen was not too worried about the potential leak of the existence of God’s Forbidden Zone.

First of all, with Little X monitoring the Internet, it was extremely easy to spot any activities directed at him.

Secondly, so what if there was some minor leak somewhere along the lines? This was not some sort of terrorist organization after all. At most, the outside world would see it as several organizations providing mutual aid, similar to a freemasonry system. The entire world knew of the practice of freemasonry anyway, so what?

Besides, Chen Chen also had the relevant qualification certification for conducting clinical trials. No matter how you look at it, Blacklight was a perfectly legitimate biotechnology company. There was no way someone could say that qualification certification for clinical trials issued by Namibia was not standard enough.

As long as the deeper secrets remained hidden, nobody could threaten Chen Chen. The worst that could happen was some international criticism that could be easily silenced by enlisting the help of the navy.

Over the last few days, the movie Chen Chen shot had also officially passed North America and the Mainland’s screening process. They would be premiering across the two continents and it should take some time for the other continents.

However, the reception was a little controversial.

Deadly Crisis: A Shocking Scheme garnered a rating of 40% on the tomatometer on North America’s renowned film critic site, Rotten Tomatoes. This meant that out of all the professional critics reviewing it, sixty-percent of them gave it a net negative review.

The popcorn meter on the other hand was rated by audience voting. The average score for Chen Chen’s movie was 2.5 out of 5.

As for IMDB, they gave the movie a rating of 6.0 which was a borderline passing mark.

“Getlost: I can’t believe this is a Hollywood movie. Apart from the all-star cast, acceptable cinematography, and the all-too-familiar car chase scene, its plot was complete garbage!”

“PSYCHO: Oh God, this movie is about an idiot who’s being hunted down by an even more idiotic force. It’s like both sides are competing to see who can be the bigger idiot. Voila, the idiot took the crown. Safe to say that this is the worst movie I’ve watched in my entire lifetime!”

“Kill: I think it’s alright. The female protagonist is hot, the scenes are explosive. With all these big-name cast, it was worth the ticket price!”

“Carpenter Qian: So, the story hasn’t even concluded right? Where did the villain come from? Was the director sleepwalking when he made the movie?”

“Douban Reviews: This here is a good old Hollywood classic, I’m going to give it five stars... What? This is shot by a Chinese man? Hehe, that explains why it’s so bad, I’ll give it a one-star to balance things out.”

Most people went to the theaters for the all-star cast and ended up dumbfounded by the actual movie itself. In the first week, the total box office of Deadly Crisis: A Shocking Scheme was only 10 million U.S. dollars. 7 million in North America and 3 million in the Mainland.

“Looks like it’s a botched investment.”

Chen Chen helplessly sighed upon reading the many criticisms on review sites. He added to the pile of comments himself:

“Chenchen: Dumb director.”

chapters.title: Chapter 139: New Weapon Gs Sword
chapters.body:
Chapter 139: New Weapon Gs Sword

Chapter 139: New Weapon GS Sword

As expected, the business magnates were astounded upon receiving their family members who had completed their treatment.

These old and frail men in the late stages of their lives had suddenly had the passages of time wound backward on them. How could they have the strength of their youthful prime restored by just spending four months in Africa?

There was not much by way of hesitation this time. Fifty members across eleven families had made reservations for the second batch of treatment. The Muhammad family of the Middle East continent accounted for the majority of it.

Most of them were collateral members, so they had to pay for the treatment of God’s Forbidden Zone at the price of ten million dollars for ten years of lifespan.

This meant that collateral members had to make a one-time payment of ten million dollars. Over the next decade, apart from helping them restore their youth, God’s Forbidden Zone would also be cultivating a cloned body of the payer and maintaining it. Should the payer be struck with any illness, they would be cured subsequently. If the cloned body remained unused after a decade had passed, God’s Forbidden Zone would get rid of it.

It was free of charge for the direct members of the Disciple Council.

Due to large family structures like Muhammad, there was a need for another disclaimer in God’s Forbidden Zone. There could be no more than thirty direct members receiving treatment and any additional member was subject to payment similar to the collateral members.

The only person allowed to directly liaise with God’s Forbidden Zone was the head of the family who held the highest authority in the family. Other family members or related members did not have the privilege of conducting business with God’s Forbidden Zone.

As one of the twelve disciples, these families could receive one hundred thousand information credit upon becoming a disciple. These information credits could be used for purchasing certain resources in God’s Forbidden Zone or they could use it to obtain certain secret information or issue missions.

The ones who would be carrying out the published missions could be other members of God’s Forbidden Zone or even the God’s Forbidden Zone organization itself.

...

Several days later, there was a conversation coming from Chen Chen’s office.

Chen Chen was chatting calmly with a gray, elderly voice on the other end.

“Don’t worry, our God’s Forbidden Zone adheres to a strict set of code. How else can we hope to attract the next group of business tycoons if we don’t hold up this level of credibility?”

“As for the matter concerning your son, I know what to do, be rest assured.”

Chen Chen sank into the rotating chair and dealt with matters on the phone while toying around with a black object measuring about thirty centimeters long.

The elongated object seemed to resemble a knife handle. There was an oblate armguard on one end of it and a convex section built for grip in the middle. The only thing missing was a blade at its top.

There was a faint gray button located in the area of the armguard. It was positioned at an angle so that someone reaching for it could conveniently press it with their thumb.

“Of course, Sir Roman, I look forward to your arrival...”

“Of course, most welcome.”

Toward the end of their conversation, Chen Chen silently hung up the phone while he held the handle and pressed the gray button.

“Chink!”

A long narrow blade popped out from the inside of the handle following a swift ring.

What was even more amusing was that the body of the blade was coated in stark black, it was as if it was painted with a matte black surface.

Appearance-wise, it looked like an Asian samurai sword, except with a high technology element built into it. The length of the sword could be adjusted flexibly, ranging from thirty centimeters at its shortest, up to two meters.

Gantz Sword.

This was another piece of equipment Chen Chen had extracted from Gantz. It was convenient for carrying around and could easily launch its blade during combat. It was the perfect weapon fit for carrying and eradicating his enemies whenever necessary.

The principle of the sword was similar to the PK Armor from Predator, both of them were a form of nano-arrangement structure. In addition, when the sword faced an opposing force which velocity exceeded 100N, it would automatically generate a three-kilohertz high-frequency oscillation, instantly cutting obstacles in its path.

With so many high-tech built-in features, a Gantz Sword weighed up to 3.8kg.

Chen Chen continued messing around with the Gantz Sword in his palms and gave it a conventional name – GS Sword, completing a full set along with the GS Combat Suit.

“Sir Godfather, Prince Steel of the Muhammad family have informed us that the Symphony of the Sea cruise ship they took has been authorized by Namibia to dock. They’ll be arriving at the Walvis Bay harbor in two hours.”

Little X’s crisp voice came from the speakers.

“Got it, have Qian Wenhuan get his crew ready to escort them at Walvis Bay.”

Chen Chen caressed the blade while commenting absent-mindedly, “Make sure those old men don’t slip and fall or something. Those are walking dollar bills.”

“But they also extended an invitation for you to board the cruise ship for an extravagant, luxurious trip experience,” Little X added.

“Luxurious trip? To where?” this caught Chen Chen’s attention, he asked without raising his head.

“Didn’t I inform you back then, the Muhammad family already invited all the other members of the Disciple Council to board the cruise ship before coming here to meet us. Also, when it comes to cruise ships, there is no need for a destination because being on the cruise itself is a prime pastime.”

“Boarding a cruise is a form of pastime? Now, this is something I’m unaware of.” Chen Chen shook his head with a blank look on his face.

Little X went on explaining patiently, “Cruise ships are a form of tourism vehicle that focuses on a sightseeing experience, there’s no need for a fixed route. You can stop in various countries and experience the local culture or indulge in the entertainment provided on the ship. There are swimming pools, bars, casinos, and even a theater to boot. Ensuring enjoyment of the highest quality even during the journey!”

“I got it, it’s something like Titanic right?” Chen Chen snapped his finger.

“That’s right!”

Little X replied, “Only the cruise ship today is far larger than Titanic. In the case of the Symphony of the Seas reserved by the Muhammad family, it’s 362 meters long, 65.7 meters wide, and 73 meters tall. It weighs 228 thousand tons in total. Titanic is merely one-fourth of its size.”

Chen Chen seemed to be finally intrigued, “If the cruise ship is this big, surely it must require a lot of energy to operate?”

“Yes, Symphony of the Seas has six Leroy Somer LSA62 generators installed, totaling its power generation to up to 100,000 kilowatts,” Little X answered, “It’s the equivalent of the Walvis Bay power plant, enough to power a small city on their own!”

Unexpectedly, Chen Chen rose decisively upon hearing this, “Inform Qian Wenhuan to put together a convoy to head to Walvis Bay. Little X, I need you to survey the largest shipyards in the world and compile an analytic report.”

“What would you like to analyze?” Little X asked.

“Price, time, power generation... I want to customize a cruise ship as well!”

“Understood.”

Following Chen Chen’s orders, Cheng Cao quickly arrived at the ground floor of the research center driving a Wrangler. By the time Chen Chen took the USB drive from the main control room and headed outside, Cheng Cao and Qian Wenhuan were already waiting for him by the entrance.

“Boss.”

“Boss!”

The two men bowed their heads slightly and headed outside with Chen Chen. Qian Wenhuan asked, “Boss, we’re going to...”

“The second batch of people from those families have arrived, we’re going to meet them and also take a tour on their cruise ship.”

Chen Chen asked as he continued walking, “Wenhuan, you’re always traveling, have you been on a cruise ship before?”

“I’ve heard of them, but I’ve never really boarded one before.”

Qian Wenhuan smiled awkwardly. “Those things are for people who have money and have the time for leisure. People who pursue efficiency have no time for it and would rather take the plane instead.”

Chen Chen nodded in agreement. It was true, even if you could afford to take a cruise ship, it was far more efficient to take the plane unless your intention in the first place was just to travel.

Chen Chen lowered his head and got into the car. They were then headed for the harbor.

Right behind them was a fleet of vehicles for escorting their esteemed guests. A long line formed of dozens of cars made for a grand presentation as they headed toward Walvis Bay.

chapters.title: Chapter 140: Reception
chapters.body:
Chapter 140: Reception

Chapter 140: Reception

As the second-largest city in Namibia and the largest coastal city in the country, Walvis Bay played a role similar to Shangdu’s role in the Mainland.

Walvis Bay was also the only deep-water port in a 3,000 kilometers span of coastline between Lobito, Angola, and Cape Town, Rainbow Country.

In 2014, the Mainland contracted an $800 million U.S. dollar land reclamation project in Namibia around the region of Walvis Bay and built a modern deepwater port.

This project was not officially completed until October of 2019.

The new port included a container port of 800 TEU per hectare that was capable of handling loads up to 100,000 tons. Its specification also included a 372-meter-long cruise terminal and a 410,000-square-meter land storage yard. Corresponding supporting facilities such as railways, highways, water, electricity, and communications facilities, along with housing construction were established as well.

This was to say that without this port, there was no way that Walvis Bay could dock a cruise ship like the Symphony of the Seas.

At just three o’clock at noon, a fleet of vehicles made their grand appearance at the port of Walvis Bay, attracting the attention of countless residents from afar.

Due to the construction of the nuclear power plant, the day-to-day activities at the port were extremely lively. There were always cargo ships traveling from afar docking at the port one after another. After being unloaded, their cargos were transported by countless heavy load trucks and sent to the nuclear power plant construction site.

This was the reason Chen Chen had to construct the nuclear power plant somewhere nearby Walvis Bay.

In addition to being the only deepwater port in Namibia, Walvis Bay was also connected to the railroad. The farther the nuclear power plant was from Walvis Bay, the higher the transportation cost of materials, and vice versa.

By the time Chen Chen’s fleet appeared at the Walvis Bay customs, the supreme officer had already been waiting for him there. Chen Chen greeted the officer with a firm handshake and promptly informed the officer of the reason for his arrival.

The other party knew better than to give Chen Chen any trouble, they quickly sorted out all the formal procedures for Chen Chen. They even took care of the formal procedures for the other family members who had yet to arrive in Namibia. The only thing they needed was their signatures.

After nearly half an hour, they spotted a small white dot appearing at the horizon far away. Its appearance was accompanied by a distant whistle, it appeared to be a cruise ship.

As the cruise ship drew closer, many people present at the scene were floored by the sheer size of the cruise ship.

“It’s the Symphony of the Seas!”

It was one of the giant cruise ships that could count itself among the ranks of the top five in the Federation. It held a prestigious reputation in the marine-related industry. The crowd began to murmur and discuss upon hearing someone’s comments about the ship.

The cruise ship began to draw closer with every passing second. What followed after was a spectacular visual. It was like facing a twenty-four stories tall building that was continuously drawing closer like a roaring mountain!

As the crowd was feeling the weight of the enormous beast, there was another dull roar, a deep and heavy rumble. It was like the bellowing of a sea monster residing under the depths of the ocean.

It took another half an hour before the titan cruise ship docked itself by the harbor.

As the passenger elevator was lowered, a group of aged but graceful and noble-looking Caucasian crowd began making their descent from above.

There were a total of fifty-eight people who came to the research center for the age-reversal treatment, nearly three times more than the previous batch. The group of gray-haired elders passed through the customs on the grounds of traveling and participation in international meetings. They then exchanged pleasantries with Chen Chen before entering the escort vehicles Chen Chen had prepared for them and headed for the research center.

Chen Chen, on the other hand, boarded the cruise ship with Qian Wenhuan and Cheng Cao.

“Please come with me, sirs.”

The person tasked with welcoming Chen Chen was an ashen-haired man who gave off an impression of being a butler. “I’m the advisor of Prince Steel, His Highness has been waiting for you for some time.”

“Thank you.”

Chen Chen nodded. “I heard that His Highness reserved the entire cruise ship?”

“That’s right, Prince Steele put in 20 million U.S. dollars for full ownership of the Symphony of the Seas for a month. Apart from the 2175 crewmates on board, there are only 500 passengers. Around 100 of them accounted for the other esteemed guests of other families, the rest are all their accompanying followers and friends.”

Chen Chen nodded and silently marveled at the extravagant lifestyle of Middle Eastern culture. 20 million U.S. dollars for a month was around 1.3 billion Renminbi, exceeding the annual target Old Wang had set.

“The primary motive for reserving this cruise ship is because His Highness is very fond of the entertainment provided on this cruise.”

The ashen-haired elder marveled with pride. “There are seven activity sections on the Symphony of the Seas. This includes the central park, the Parkway Happy City, the Royal Avenue shopping street, dozens of swimming pools of various sizes, a spa, and a fitness center.”

As they chatted, Chen Chen officially stepped into the cruise. It appeared to be pre-planned for Chen Chen to enter from the tail end of the ship. The moment he boarded the ship, he was greeted by the scenery of a mini garden.

It made sense to call it mini since the total area of the square was only a few thousand square meters. The garden part of the description came from the wide variety of plantations decorating the area. There were various bars and restaurants on both sides, Chen Chen even caught a glimpse of a steakhouse and an Italian restaurant.

As they continued advancing, Chen Chen saw more bars, gyms, basketball courts, spa centers, and a theater that could host up to two thousand people...

Even though this was just a small preview, Chen Chen was completely floored by the sheer magnitude of the cruise ship and how well facilitated it was.

After walking for roughly ten minutes, Chen Chen finally arrived at the destination. It was the largest banquet hall in the Symphony of the Seas.

At the moment, there were a large number of people gathered in the banquet hall to welcome Chen Chen’s arrival, it was a great show of gesture. Unlike the previous spectacle where it was mostly aged members, the majority of them were the younger men and women of the respective families and relatives.

There were more than a hundred people present.

“Welcome, Mr. Chen.”

The one leading in front was a man from the Sand Continent. He had a white turban wrapped around him and had a massive beard. Chen Chen had seen him on the television in the past, he was the brother of the king of the Sand Continent, Prince Steele.

Unlike the other continents, the Sand Continent still upheld an autocratic monarchy system. Strictly speaking, he held the highest authority of all people present at the scene.

“Your Highness, I’m flattered by your grand reception.”

Chen Chen bowed slightly as a show of respect, Prince Steele approached him and kissed Chen Chen’s cheek as they have always done in their culture.

What followed after was a lengthy introduction where Prince Steele introduced Chen Chen to dozens of esteemed families present. It was only after the introductions had concluded that they officially began the banquet of the night.

The banquet was hosted in the form of a buffet. Dishes from all over the world were presented including hot pot, barbecue, seafood, Japanese food, pizza, and so on.

Right beside them was a world-class band weaving together musical chords and symphonies, delivering a magnificent musical performance. Chen Chen had a chance to see for himself the refinement which the true royal classes indulged in.

The people came and went, forming small circles among themselves and making conversation. Countless business opportunities were birthed this way amid their many discussions, businesses where common people would never see, hear nor get a single cent of.

Chen Chen let Qian Wenhuan and Cheng Cao be on their own, Qian Wenhuan had already found someone to mingle with. Cheng Cao, on the other hand, kept to himself at the edges of the buffet while munching down his steak.

After mingling around with the crowd, Chen Chen left the banquet hall and went to the deck outside the venue.

Without even sensing the passing of time, midnight had already come upon the cruise ship.

The Symphony of the Seas had long set sail into the vast blue sea again shortly after Chen Chen had boarded it. The night sky dyed in indigo drowned itself into the dark green ocean, there was no telling where the horizon which separated the two started and ended.

“The moon of the sea shines ever the same across all the different worlds...”

Chen Chen recited a familiar ancient poem.

“Good poem, good poem.”

There were approaching footsteps coming from behind. Shortly after, a tall and broad figure appeared by Chen Chen’s side and leaned on the railing just like how Chen Chen did. “I’ve always found myself captivated by the ancient poetry and culture of the Mainland.”

“Mr. Leo, you’ve heard of this one before too?” Chen Chen tilted his head and asked.

This was not the first time Chen Chen had met this man. This was the very man the Foster family sent the very first time Chen Chen sent the invitation to the eleven families.

Leo Foster. The only heir of Norman Foster, who was at the helm of the Foster family.

chapters.title: Chapter 141: Patricide And Filicide
chapters.body:
Chapter 141: Patricide And Filicide

Chapter 141: Patricide and Filicide

“I’ve always greatly admired Chinese culture.”

Leo nodded, glanced back silently, then said, “By understanding the culture of Mainland China and comparing it to our Western culture, one can always find a lot in common.”

“Such as?” Chen Chen looked out at the sea and did not turn back as he asked.

“Such as... The culture of killing one’s children.”

Leo shot a look at Chen Chen. “You must know of The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars, right?”

Chen Chen nodded.

“There’s a story in The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars that I find very interesting.”

Leo smiled. “In a catastrophic year, a filial son named Guo Ju decided to bury his son alive to have one less mouth to feed, so that his mother won’t go hungry. This was because he reasoned that, if his son died, he could have another child again, but if his mother died, she can’t be brought back to life.”

“Makes sense...” Chen Chen nodded.

“???”

Leo was stunned, then he laughed. “Chen, you’re too funny... This story is too simple. Let me tell you another one.

“In ancient times, there was a boy named Shun who was very capable, but his father didn’t like him and often beat him viciously for no reason. But Shun never retaliated. When he was beaten lightly, he endured it obediently; when he was beaten heavily, he would flee to the wilderness, crying alone bitterly.

“Later on, Shun’s father became worse. Once, he made Shun repair the roof, but when Shun climbed up, he took away the ladder and set the house on fire. Finally, Shun had to use the bamboo hat he was wearing as a parachute and jump down, escaping death...”

At this point, Leo observed Chen Chen’s expression again, but Chen Chen’s face was still stoic as he looked into the distance indifferently.

Frowning to himself, Leo continued saying, “Two days later, Shun’s father made him dig a well. Shun went, honest and obedient. When he reached the bottom of the well, his father hurriedly piled on the dirt, trying to bury Shun alive.

“Fortunately, Shun was vigilant and knew his father was up to no good. So he had already dug a secret tunnel in the sidewall of the well as soon as he reached the bottom. In this manner, he survived again...”

“Phew!”

After he finished, Leo finally could not refrain from asking, “Chen, don’t you think that Shun’s father is utterly abhorrent?”

“I guess.” Chen Chen nodded. “If I were Shun, I’d be very sad.”

“Yes, I think so too.” When he saw Chen Chen’s reaction, Leo was secretly pleased. Before he could say anything else, the captain’s voice boomed through the speaker of the cruise ship.

“Dear ladies and gentlemen, the Symphony of the Seas will leave Namibia’s territorial waters in half an hour and arrive at the high seas of the Atlantic.

“I repeat, the Symphony of the Seas will leave Namibia’s territorial waters in half an hour...”

This announcement rang out three times.

At once, the people who were still at the banquet erupted in a cheer. Many people directly left the banquet and headed to the deck below.

“We’ll be on the high seas soon.”

Leo looked delighted. At the same time, three people walked out of the banquet hall and came to both of them. “Chen Chen, Leo, how about we go together?”

Among these three, one of them was Davis, heir to the Wilson family while the other two were the heirs of the Alexander and Edwards families.

“Of course.” Leo turned to look at Chen Chen in invitation. “Chen, are you up for a round at the casino?”

“The casino?” Chen Chen inquired.

“Yup, and not just the casino. To celebrate your arrival, Mr. Chen Chen, the few of us have specially prepared something special. I believe it’ll be worth your while!”

The three others at the side also started to egg him on.

“I’ll be honored.” Seeing that the four of them were inviting him, Chen Chen nodded. “I also wish to see what sort of special program it is.”

Before boarding the ship, Chen Chen had already revised some information about cruise ships. Most of these cruise ships were so popular because of their casinos.

According to international law, the territories on aircraft, ships, and other vehicles belonged to the country of said vehicle. In other words, this cruise ship belonged to a particular country. If a criminal case were to occur on this ship, it would be investigated and managed by that country.

Therefore, as long as this country did not prohibit gambling, cruise ships of this country could operate casinos onboard.

Of course, if this ship entered the waters of a country where gambling was prohibited, the ship must immediately close the casino, which could only be opened when it left the country’s waters once more.

Before he left with the group, Chen Chen shot a final glance at the distance.

At an unknown time, the moonlight had faded.

A dark cloud slowly drifted across the sky, spreading outward with its thick, silent darkness, covering the pure bright moon.

It seemed that a storm was fast approaching...

...

As there was still a half-hour’s voyage to the high seas, in this time, Chen Chen was invited by the heirs of the four families to the opera house.

There were only a handful of people in the theater that could accommodate a thousand people. Still, the scene on stage was as grand as ever, with the group of actors performing with great effort.

Chen Chen thought that this opera was interesting too. It told the story of how Zeus became the king of gods in Greek mythology.

According to legend, in the realm of gods, Uranus, the god of sky, imprisoned his children underground. As a result, his son Cronus rose with the wind and used a sickle to castrate his father, thus replacing the rule of the god of sky.

However, the god of sky cursed Cronus before he died, saying that Cronus would be killed by his own children, just like Uranus was.

Thus, Cronus made the decision to devour every single child of his that was born.

That was what he ended up doing. When his first five offspring were born, they were swallowed into his stomach before they could even open their eyes.

This happened until the birth of the sixth baby, named Zeus. The mother, Rhea, hid Zeus, swaddled a stone in cloth, and offered that to Cronus, saying that that was their child.

Therefore, Cronus swallowed the stone in one gulp...

Zeus survived by luck. As he grew up, he began to understand his origins and decided to take revenge.

First, he spiked his father’s wine with emetics. The father vomited after drinking the wine, regurgitating his five siblings.

Among these five siblings were the well-known king of the sea, Poseidon, and the king of the underworld, Hades.

Ultimately, the brothers worked together to defeat Cronus and imprison him in the deepest level of hell while Zeus became the king of the gods.

“What do you think?”

After the opera ended, Leo asked with a smile, “Chen, have you realized that our Western patricide and your Eastern filicide shared the same message despite delivering it differently? As a person grows, they must overthrow the will imposed upon them by the previous generation. Only then would they achieve full personhood and become their true self.”

“It’s still a little different.”

Chen Chen shook his head and disagreed. “This is in fact a cultural difference. In China, we’re good at farming. The older one gets, the more experience one would accumulate in farming. Therefore, as society grows, we end up with a situation where the older you get, the more senior you are, the greater your right to speak.

“It’s different from Greek civilization. Greek land is barren and unsuitable for farming, but it can produce olive oil, wine, and wool. So the Greeks went to sea to do business, selling their goods to other countries in exchange for food.

“However, the sea is clearly the world of young people. Riding the wind to go out to sea and fighting the waves is something that old people can’t do. Therefore, Greek culture worships young people more, and so the father-killing myth appeared.”

Chen Chen analyzed. “As such, this is the ideological difference between the two civilizations. And in truth, our filicide in China isn’t as serious as the patricide complex in the West.”

“...”

Everyone exchanged glances. Their initially exuberant spirits had dropped into a death-like silence.

“Oh right, didn’t you say that there was something else planned?”

Chen Chen suddenly changed the topic and put on an interested face. “Can we go now?”

“It should be here soon. Let’s go to the casino lobby first...”

On one side, Davis broke the silence. “We’ll do a quick round there, then head to the boxing match!”

chapters.title: Chapter 142: Blackjack
chapters.body:
Chapter 142: Blackjack

Chapter 142: Blackjack

“Boxing match?” Chen Chen asked curiously.

Leo quickly came to his senses and smiled as he explained, “You must’ve watched UFC before. Unlike UFC, this type of boxing match has no rules. Arms, legs, and even teeth can be used as a weapon.”

“Right, many people like watching UFC, but to really stir up the beast in you and pump up your adrenalin, it has to be boxing without rules!”

On one side, Davis broke out into a bloodthirsty grin. “There are no medics, physical examinations, doping tests, not even referees. The difference between victory and defeat is that one side will be standing while the other side has fallen.”

“I’ve never seen that kind of fighting. What are we waiting for then?” Chen Chen was already walking off.

Leo who lagged slightly behind stared at Chen Chen’s back with a dark glint in his eyes. After that, he exchanged a glance with Davis.

Davis nodded and the four of them swiftly followed behind.

Upon reaching the casino, Chen Chen found that the doors, which had been shut tight, were fully opened. Not only that, but there was also a large throng of people inside. Most of the passengers on the cruise had gathered here.

Before he went in, he could already sense the excitable atmosphere surging out from inside.

“Looks like the one we’ve invited isn’t here yet,” Leo said ruefully. “So, Chen, wanna play a few rounds of ‘Blackjack’ with us?”

“What’s ‘Blackjack’?” Chen Chen asked offhandedly.

“Oh, my dear Chen, you don’t even know what ‘Blackjack’ is?”

Leo did not know whether to laugh or cry, so he pulled Chen Chen by the sleeve and went in. “Come, dear Chen, let us give you a proper lesson!”

With that, he dragged Chen Chen into the casino.

It was not until Chen Chen walked into the casino that he understood why so many successful people with billions of dollars would be addicted to this sort of place, even losing control as they gambled frantically and finally burned through everything.

The interior of the casino was magnificent and sumptuous with thick, dizzying carpets, splendid golden inscriptions on the walls, and a gigantic chandelier suspended over the huge hall. It was like paradise on Earth.

However, beneath the brilliant, glittering fixtures, there was a psychological suggestion that anything was possible —

The windows here did not show the world outside, so one would not sense the passage of time;

There was no mirror that would show one’s reflection, so people would not notice their faces distorted with greed;

Moreover, the flawless service here, along with the casino chips that obscured the fact that money was slipping through one’s fingers, caused people to abandon all reasons. They could only sink endlessly lower...

The livelier the atmosphere, the more the place was filled with a “hysterical” aura.

As he walked through the aisle at the center of the casino, Chen Chen had already seen several gamblers with bloodshot eyes. They pushed their chips forward recklessly, panting, having lost their rationality.

It took humans millions of years to develop reason and gamblers abandoned it in only three minutes.

Before Prince Steele had invited the eleven plutocrats, he had said that he would allow these powerful families to invite their relatives and friends on board as well. This was why there were so many people on the ship.

Most of these were second-class, minor plutocrats or wealthy entrepreneurs, who had used their connections to come on board to expand their network. Chen Chen even noticed a few people from China.

At this point, a handsome young man with long hair, dyed brown, passed by Chen Chen, tirelessly explaining to his companions:

“This is the last time I’m buying the chips. This 4 million is my final streak of stubbornness. You won’t see me spending another dollar in this casino. With this 4 million I’ll win 400 million!”

Chen Chen shook his head slightly. It was not that easy to win four million in a casino...

Leo, Davis, and the rest led Chen Chen to a gambling table with a notice that said “Blackjack”. This must be the Blackjack that they were talking about.

“Blackjack is actually 21-point.”

Leo quickly explained to Chen Chen, “First, take two to three decks of cards and remove the two Jokers from each deck. For the remaining cards, the points of 2 to 9 are counted as is while 10, J, Q, K each counts as 10 points. The Ace is counted as 1 or 11 points, depending on the situation. The rules are simple. The player may keep drawing cards, then compare the number of points in their hand. However, the total can only add up to 21 points at most. If it exceeds 21 points, you ‘bust’, and you lose immediately. If your points are the same, it’s a tie.”

“So, who am I playing against?” Chen Chen asked.

“The dealer.” Davis pointed at the sexy dealer opposite the table. “The game is divided into the dealer and the player. All players only need to compare with the dealer. They don’t have to compare with each other.”

“That’s right. After all players have finished drawing their cards, then it’s the dealer’s turn to look at the cards. The rule for the dealer is: If it’s greater than or equal to 17 points, they won’t draw more cards. Otherwise, they’ll keep drawing cards until they reach 17 points. If they bust, all players win.”

Leo added. “Tonight’s banker is Prince Steele. He’s sponsored each of us a hundred thousand dollars.”

As Leo spoke, a casino staff member came over with some chips and handed them to everyone. Chen Chen took a few and held them in his hands.

“Let’s play a round then,” Davis said eagerly, “my hands are itching already.”

With that, he took a seat and threw down his chips, signaling the dealer across the table to start dealing.

Chen Chen shook his head and sat in the second seat, putting down two chips.

In the first round, Chen Chen received one card that was faced down and one card that was turned over. The former was the 2 of spades, and the latter was the 10 of diamonds.

Chen Chen frowned. 2 plus 10 resulted in a number that was neither here nor there. If he continued to draw, he might bust with just one more card. If he chose not to draw any more cards, his chances of losing were very high.

Right away, Chen Chen looked toward the dealer, whose exposed card was 7 points.

“Seat number 1, do you want to draw?” The dealer started to ask Leo, according to the procedure.

“D*mn it, I don’t want any more cards!”

Leo laughed and cursed. His exposed card was the 10 of hearts, the same as Chen Chen.

A game of probability? This gameplay seemed to be solvable by math...

At this point, Chen Chen could not help tilting his head.

[My points are 12 and the dealer’s exposed card is 7 points. If I choose not to draw, then according to the rule that “the dealer must reach 17 points or more”‘ as Leo said, there’s only one way I can win, that is if the dealer busts.]

[Suppose that the probability of the dealer busting is P(S). In that case, there are currently 13 possibilities for the dealer’s hidden card: let T represent 10, J, Q, K. When F(T+7), the dealer won’t draw or bust. Putting this aside...]

[When F(9+7), drawing 6~T equals to bust. F(9+7) = 8/13... and so on. Finally, P(S) =∑F=0.26231.]

[This means that, if I don’t draw any cards, my probability of winning is 26.231%.]

Chen Chen glanced at Leo and Davis beside him and continued thinking.

[However, if I continue to draw, I’ll win in two cases: 1. I don’t bust, and the dealer does; 2. Neither the dealer nor I bust, but I have more points. The probability of this: P(H).]

[The chances of the dealer busting is the same as above. But if it’s the second scenario, then there are four further possibilities. P1, I have 21 points, the dealer has 17 to 20 points; P2, I have 20 points, the dealer has 17 to 19 points; P3, I have 19 points, the dealer has 17 to 18 points; P4, I have 18 points, the dealer has 17 points...]

[Let’s analyze this first. If I draw the next card and my point increases from 12 to 13, that means I drew an A. Therefore, if W(12) = 1, then W(13) = W(12)×1/13 = 0.07692.]

[From this, W(18) can be calculated. The dealer gets 17 points, and Z(17) can be calculated in the same way. Therefore, the formula for the fourth possibility is P4 = W(18)+Z(17), and the same can be calculated for P1, P2, P3...]

[Therefore, the probabilities of the four scenarios added together is P(H) = P1+P2+P3+P4+P(S)×9/13 = 0.43577.]

[In other words, if I continue to draw, my probability of winning is 43.577%, which is higher than if I don’t draw a card...]

All this seemed lengthy, but it flashed through Chen Chen’s mind in just a moment. When Chen Chen looked up once again, it was his turn to speak.

“Seat number 2, do you want to draw?” the dealer asked in a soft voice.

“One more, please.”

chapters.title: Chapter 143: The Arena
chapters.body:
Chapter 143: The Arena

Chapter 143: The Arena

Half an hour later.

“In this round, the dealer has 18 points. Number 1 has 17 points, Number 2 has 20 points, Number 3 has busted — Number 2 wins, Number 1 and 3 lose.”

The dealer spoke calmly as she pushed some of the chips to Chen Chen with a metal ruler.

Currently, the chips before Chen Chen had piled up to the height of a small table. At the same time, a large crowd of spectators had gathered behind him.

“Oh my god...”

After the dealer’s announcement, the crowd instantly murmured among themselves.

“Chen, how do you do it?”

Leo and Davis, who had already lost all their money, wore incredulous expressions. There was no doubt that everyone had lost and won some rounds, but the chips in their hands had slowly shifted to the dealer’s side. Only Chen Chen’s chips did not decrease but grew in volume instead.

“It’s just a question of probability.”

Chen Chen felt that this was enough, so he threw down the cards in his hand and said to the dealer, “There’s a total of 10.62 million USD here. Please transfer 10 million to my account. The rest is yours.”

“Thank you, sir!” The dealer was stunned and hastily bowed in gratitude.

With just this tip, she could retire right away.

As Chen Chen left, the spectators behind could not refrain from clapping softly before they slowly dispersed.

Chen Chen took out his phone and looked at the time. It was already ten at night. At the same time, he realized that despite being connected to the cruise ship’s Wi-Fi, the internet traffic was zero. At once, he asked, puzzled, “Does the Wi-Fi here have no signal?”

“Let me see...”

Leo took out his phone and said in amazement, “Me too. Although it’s connected to Wi-Fi, I can’t get online. Seems like there’s a problem with the Wi-Fi.”

“This kind of thing often happens at sea.” Davis sounded unconcerned.

Just then, there was the sudden sound of helicopter rotors from outside the doors of the casino. The minute they heard this, Leo and the rest perked up.

“The contenders we’ve invited will be here soon!”

With that, they headed out of the casino.

Chen Chen followed the group. After they walked out, they followed the sound and saw a small helicopter, flickering with a red light, in the dark, heavy sky, hovering above the Symphony of the Seas.

“Once the pathfinder helicopter arrives, the ones we’ve invited would be here right away,” Davis, as if worried that Chen Chen would not understand, quickly explained.

Chen Chen nodded.

Sure enough, a short while after the helicopter landed on the helipad, another cruise ship appeared on the horizon and rapidly drew closer.

This cruise ship was pitch-black all over. Although it was not small, next to the Symphony of the Seas, it looked like a small dirt mound beside a large mountain. There was no way to compare them at all.

The people from the helicopter came forward to exchange a few words. More than ten minutes later, thirty or forty men dressed in various attire boarded the ship intermittently.

From Chen Chen’s perspective, everyone in this group radiated a ferocious, formidable aura as if each one was “reeking of blood”. Only those who had seen blood before would have such a presence.

Chen Chen stood on the upper deck and looked down. Prince Steele’s bodyguards and crew were conducting full-body checks on everyone. They were only brought in one by one after all suspicious items had been confiscated.

At this moment, an icy gust of wind tore past the deck, messing up Chen Chen’s hair with a wailing sound.

The wind was getting stronger, to the point where it could no longer be ignored...

Chen Chen slowly looked up and gazed into the distance once more.

The sky was extremely gloomy. It was no longer a clear indigo sky but eerie darkness. It looked like a ceaselessly rolling thick fog that was startlingly black...

Rain clouds had fully condensed and formed above the Symphony of the Seas. A violent storm was inevitable.

“It looks like there’ll be a storm soon. D*mn this weather!”

Leo was grumbling. “Chen, let’s head back first.”

Chen Chen stood with his back to Leo and the rest. Currently, a smirk was tugging at the corner of his lips, then it vanished.

“Alright, let’s go back.”

They returned to the casino.

As the boxing match was starting soon, Leo and the rest did not continue gambling. Instead, they stood on the sidelines and chatted. From time to time, members from the other families came over and joined in. It was Chen Chen who stood to one side with nothing to do.

Chen Chen glanced at the table where they were playing Fight the Landlord. He happened to notice the brown-haired young man playing together with two forty, fifty-year-old ladies.

Moreover, the 4 million worth of chips on the young man’s table had dwindled to less than 200 thousand...

At this point, the young man was gripping four cards in one hand, blustering at one of the ladies, “You can crush me with seventeen cards? Do you think you can crush me? If you crush me today with seventeen cards, I’ll eat this mahogany table! Right! Here! Right! Now!”

“...”

Chen Chen turned away with an indifferent expression and checked the time again. Just then, a few crew members in uniforms walked over and started to clear out the tables and guests in the back half of the casino. Their team leader even looked over to Leo and nodded subtly.

When Chen Chen turned back, he just so happened to see Leo nodding back. When Leo noticed Chen Chen looking at him, he shot a grin at Chen Chen.

“What are they doing?”

Chen Chen asked curiously.

“My dear Chen, you’ll know soon.”

Leo patted Chen Chen’s shoulders. Then, something happened, which Chen Chen could never imagine.

The back half of the casino, after being cleared of people and tables, started to descend gradually. It was like a modifiable stage. A deep circular pit appeared.

The deck in the pit lowered continuously, revealing a flight of steps, creating a circle of tiered seats like a basketball court.

At last, as the deep pit formed, a circular fighting arena, eight meters wide and surrounded by metal nets, appeared in the middle.

Meanwhile, dozens of partitions were lowered from the ceiling, dividing the surrounding space into dozens of small, private rooms. These rooms formed a circle around the periphery of the venue, giving an unobstructed view of the entire arena.

Some of the crew members began to move the sofa from the lounge area next to them into the private rooms.

Chen Chen shook his head. He truly did not expect that a hidden mechanism like this would exist on such a standard-looking cruise ship. If the ship was not hired out privately, ordinary passengers would never witness this.

Was this the world of the rich?

Chen Chen raised an eyebrow. Astonished cries erupted behind him as well.

Indeed, everyone who was throwing money around in the casino had seen the sudden drop of the arena and was instantly shocked.

“Let’s go, Chen.”

Leo and the rest evidently knew about this mechanism long ago. He called out to Chen Chen, then took the lead and occupied a box. Meanwhile, the crowd that was endlessly surging up from behind was stopped by the crew.

As news of this spread, more people hurried over swiftly. Nonetheless, other than the members of the eleven plutocrat families who could watch from the box, everyone else could only sit in the tiered seats below to watch the competition.

chapters.title: Chapter 144: Cards Revealed
chapters.body:
Chapter 144: Cards Revealed

Chapter 144: Cards Revealed

As it was not an actual room, the furnishings inside were not elaborate. Other than a few sofas and a coffee table, there was nothing else.

Even so, compared to the tiered seats outside the court, it was much more comfortable.

At least the private room was not as messy and crowded as the outside. Not only did it have a floor-to-ceiling observation window, but there was also a 40-inch ultra-thin monitor on the wall, which broadcasted the scene in the arena. This allowed them to view the competition from different angles.

As one took in the entire venue from the full-length window and watched the fight from here, one would feel a sense of superiority.

In a short while, there was a knock on the door and a server came in, respectfully handing a few betting machines to the group. According to him, once they entered their numbers into the machine, they could begin placing their bets.

Chen Chen tossed this machine, which looked like a remote control, to one side. He sat on the single sofa at the side and silently watched as a middle-aged man with slicked-back silver hair stepped into the ring.

He seemed to be acting as the host. With a microphone in his hand, he said in a loud rousing, announcer-like voice, “Gentlemen, ladies, homies, b*tches, welcome to the no-holds-barred fight of the Symphony of the Seas!”

He leaped onto the arena and roared. “Anything in this world consists of two opposites. Where there’s light, there’s darkness. Where there’s earth, there’s the sky. Similarly, where there are UFC and other world-certified fighting, there are also brutal, bloody underground fighting! And today, you’ll be lucky enough to see such a fight!

“They’re bloodthirsty, they’re ruthless, they’re inhuman enough to break your neck — this is the draw of fighting without rules! They are demons from hell, killing machines that have lost their humanity, and they’ll ignite every single violent desire in your heart!”

As the host spat out these crude and even insulting words, it enlivened the once-quiet atmosphere. A few young people, spurred on by the host, started screaming peculiarly.

It was not the first time most of them had watched an event like this.

At the same time, at the end of the ring, an iron door suddenly opened and a bony black man walked out.

“Very well, looks like the first contender for today is a black brother!”

The host yelled excitedly, “Please let me introduce him properly. He’s the proud warrior of Wakanda, the loyal guard of the Black Panther. He’s chewed the Heart-shaped Herb and f*cked Captain America in the ass. He hails from the Rainbow Nation and his mighty, blazing name is:

“Aaaaaarches!”

“Roar —!”

Galvanized by the host’s provocative tone, the audience present howled even louder. The host’s vulgar language had stirred up the darkness deep in the hearts of these upper-class people.

The stadium filled with hundreds slowly became rowdier. Many people were staring at the arena with great interest, including most who were already familiar with fights like this.

Currently, the skinny black man had stepped into the ring, but he did not fit the host’s description. Under the blinding light which shone from above, he displayed a helpless manner and even his legs were trembling slightly.

“Alright, the next one to come out will be our other killer.”

The host turned around and faced the other door of the ring. “He’s a Western cowboy from the Middle Ages, a conservative grouch, half-man, half-machine, shotgun, beer, and b*tches...”

With that, a white man with fierce eyes, wearing a cowboy hat, slowly walked out.

“Oh f*ck, this bastard is so primitive! This bastard, he’ll even eat the butt of a live cow! He’s the cowboy from North America —”

“Saaaaaaarion!”

“Roar!”

There was another raucous cheer from the audience. At this point, the entire arena was fully warmed up.

Within the private room, Chen Chen stared speechlessly at the two men in the ring. The white man in the cowboy hat was not too shabby. Although his build was average, at least he had the right attitude. Still, Chen Chen could see the deep, hidden fear within his eyes.

As for the other black man, Chen Chen was somewhat stumped. With that build and that demeanor, he had just come from a slum, and now he was here to join an underground fight?

However, after thinking about it, Chen Chen understood.

Most people had the impression that this sort of fighting, underground boxing, should be more intense than regular boxing. After all, one would be fighting for one’s life on stage all year round, and had to be even stronger than professional boxers — this was what most people thought.

However, this was a misunderstanding.

First, whether it was boxing or MMA or even fighting without rules, their purpose was nothing more than to make money. Both the boxers and the organizer of the fight were in it for the money.

However, the world’s top UFC fighters could make tens of millions of USD in one tournament. How much would an underground fighter make?

After all, the box office of an underground tournament definitely could not compare to a regular, professional one. Not only that, but there was also no webcast or pre-match publicity for underground fights. Even if underground fights were targeted at this group of wealthy people, there were only a few hundred people. How could it compare to a market that spanned the entire globe?

Therefore, if these boxers were good enough, they could become professional, instead of risking their lives by joining the unprofitable and extremely dangerous venture of underground boxing.

As he thought of this, Chen Chen suddenly recalled the contenders he saw on the deck who had reeked of blood.

Those were not the contenders for this underground boxing. Their identities were...

Time ticked by slowly.

It seemed as if the approaching rainstorm, which turned the air heavy and damp, put the crowd in the casino into a jittery mood.

Leo and the rest kept checking their watches and their faces grew solemn. Although they were still watching the ring, their minds had drifted elsewhere.

The first match between the black man and the white man took only half a minute to determine the winner. The result was truly astounding. Although the white man had appeared to be the more aggressive one, when they fought, he was knocked out by the black man in two or three strikes.

Not only that, but under the inciting cheers of the audience, the black man displayed a ferocious side for the first time. He directly sat atop the white man’s body and pummeled the white man’s face, until it was reduced to a bloody pulp...

Still, this was just the appetizer.

Following that, ordinary folk, with almost no training, stepped into the ring one after another, fighting to the death with strangers they had just met. Folding chairs, steel pipes, and other weapons were even thrown up from under the stage, increasing the bloodiness of the fights.

This was what they called underground boxing. Although there were no rules, it was bloody and merciless.

The cruel scenes caused some people to withdraw from the audience, but the other type of crazed spectators opted to remain.

These people roared and snarled freely like a pack of wild beasts. They had temporarily put aside the rationality and morality that human beings should possess and only unleash the most primitive trait of violence in mankind.

Yes, there were genes in humans that yearned for violence. The history of human progress was a history of massacres. Without this violent trait, humans could not possibly have come this far.

Therefore, were savagery and cruelty in the inherent nature of some people?

“Mr. Chen Chen...”

Just as Chen Chen was deep in thought, Leo spoke up beside him.

This time, Leo sounded different. When he spoke, he had abandoned the intimate manner of addressing Chen Chen. At the same time, there was a decisive edge to his voice.

“Mr. Chen Chen, it’s time for you to choose.”

Meanwhile, Davis and the other two looked away from the ring and stared quietly at Chen Chen.

“I can pretend that nothing happened today.”

Chen Chen said calmly, “I have no interest in joining your little family games.”

“Shame.”

When Leo heard this, his face turned icy. At the same time, on the deck outside the casino, one could hear a sudden explosion, then the fearful screams of countless people.

“The pirates we’ve hired have started their attack on the Symphony of the Seas.”

Leo and the rest got up. Davis said, somewhat ruefully, “Mr. Chen Chen, you’re really too naïve. There’s no middle ground in this world. Even Austria and the other countries who claimed to be neutral had already fallen under the iron boots of the Nazis whereas Switzerland only escaped by luck.”

“Although we haven’t found out who’s backing you, after we capture you, it’ll be easy enough,” Leo said as he looked down at the arena.

At this point, there was a sudden tumult outside the private room. Everyone looked over instinctively, only to see that the underground boxers who had mixed in with the audience were suddenly rushing out, immediately guarding the casino door and trapping everyone inside.

Meanwhile, a few crew members within the casino did not react at all but instead nodded to each other.

“What’s going on?”

“Where did these fighters come from? What’s the matter?”

“D*mn it, what’s that noise outside? Is someone attacking our ship? Leo, what are you guys planning?”

The crowd was in chaos. A few members from the other plutocratic families even dashed out of their rooms and asked in shock.

Leo ignored their questions and directly looked toward those few crew members. “Where are the weapons?”

The crew members dragged out a few metal cabinets at once and gleaming blades were produced before everyone’s eyes. “When we boarded the ship, Prince Steele took away our guns. Sorry, but we couldn’t get it back.”

The leader of the crew shrugged.

Leo frowned but finally said, “It’s fine. In a surprise attack, Wilson’s gang should still be able to take over the Symphony of the Seas. We just need to provide support.”

With that, they started distributing the weapons. From one side, a young man from another family boldly walked to Leo and questioned, “Leo, what are you doing? Do you realize that doing something like this on Prince Steele’s ship will...”

Before he could finish, Leo suddenly drew out a dagger and thrust it viciously into the man’s stomach!

“John, did you know that I’ve put up with you for too long?” Leo’s face twisted sinisterly. “Go to hell, you piece of sh*t!”

With that, he tugged the dagger out forcefully and a large spray of blood splattered his clothes.

When the crowd witnessed the man dying on the spot, there was a high-pitched scream. A few of them tried to flee but were hacked to death by the group of boxers.

At once, the stench of blood pervaded the casino, prompting countless screams and tears.

Leo shot a look at the casino doors that were shut tight. He could hear a ceaseless cacophony of gunshots and shouts. He pointed to Chen Chen who stood at the forefront and said grimly, “Kill them! Except for him, don’t leave anyone alive!”

At Leo’s command, the vicious fighters brandished their blades and rushed into the crowd.

In an instant, the crowd was wailing and howling as blood splashed everywhere! Countless dying screams and desperate sobs could be heard endlessly!

“Mr. Chen Chen, do you now regret turning us down previously?”

At this point, Leo seemed to have become someone else entirely. As he wiped his bloodstained hands, he stared steadily at Chen Chen. “Unfortunately, it’s too late now...”

“Yes, it’s too late.”

Chen Chen silently stood where he was, a peculiar smile twitching his mouth. In his hand, a black handle had appeared at an unknown time.

“Rumble!”

As the thunderous roar split heaven and earth asunder, Leo subconsciously glanced out the window. Beneath the lightning-lit sky, big fat raindrops had started to fall with a patter.

The rainstorm had finally arrived...

chapters.title: Chapter 145: His Highness’ Thought Process
chapters.body:
Chapter 145: His Highness’ Thought Process

Chapter 145: His Highness’ Thought Process

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“Whoosh...”

The rain outside was torrential. Within the blockaded casino, bright red drops scattered continuously like rain.

Like precious rubies, they splattered onto the expensive carpet, staining the dark red fabric with an unsettling hue while exuding a nauseating odor.

A headless body stood, stock-still, as raindrops spouted from the throat like a fountain. Then, the body slowly collapsed.

All around this headless body, corpses littered the floor. The mystifying thing was, these corpses were all missing limbs. There was even one that was severed cleanly in half from head to toe.

The boundless rain spread out, forming fresh, vivid puddles on the red carpet.

As the last fighting died, the rain within the casino stopped.

Currently, within ten meters of where Chen Chen stood, only two people were standing.

Leo Foster and Davis Wilson.

In the inner depths of the casino, the passengers who were trapped there had hidden far away. Many of them were gravely injured and could only lie helplessly on the ground or huddle in the corner, like a flock of lambs waiting to be slaughtered.

“How’s this possible...”

Leo shuddered while gaping at the young man nearby who was drenched in blood and the strange long blade in his hand.

It was black all over without any luster. When it swung, one could not even see a glimmer...

However, it was this blade that had easily sliced human bodies as if they were tofu. Each body was only cut once, but was immediately severed into two...

At the moment, the pirates who had mingled with the boxers as well as the crew members who were bought off, were all dead.

Chen Chen held out the long knife and slowly stepped toward the other two.

“Agh!”

Davis trembled all over and suddenly rushed to the door, crying out, “I’m the heir of the Wilson family. If I die, my family won’t just do nothing! They’ll...”

Before he finished, he felt a cold sensation in his throat and a blade slid out from his neck.

Instantly, there was another large spurt of blood.

“Hurgh... Gurgle...”

Davis’ eyes widened as he gripped his throat tightly, trying to prevent his life from leaking out. Nonetheless, his body grew colder and colder as blood rushed into his lungs through the severed trachea, suffocating him completely.

“Thump!”

Davis’ corpse slowly fell to the ground.

Drops of cold sweat trickled down Leo’s forehead. When he witnessed Davis dying, he seemed to sober up and said softly, “Chen Chen, can I pay for my own life with money?”

Leo pleaded in a whisper, “You don’t have to do anything, just return me to my family... I’ll tell you the password to a Swiss bank account, which contained the two billion USD I’ve earned privately throughout the years without letting my family know...”

“Speak.”

Chen Chen nodded.

“The account number is... The password is...”

Leo quickly spoke, then subconsciously stepped backward. “Just the account number and password alone isn’t enough. You’ll need a key too. To get this key, I’ll have to get off this ship, then...”

However, before he could finish talking, Chen Chen swiped horizontally again. Leo’s eyes froze at once.

Then, his large head toppled off his neck.

There were more screams and sobs behind him.

Chen Chen ignored the noise. He said gently to the head rolling by his feet, “It’s a shame that I’ve never needed any key to take out money.”

With that, Chen Chen looked toward the doors.

There was a flurry of footsteps from beyond the door. Chen Chen flicked his hand and the GS Blade retracted instantly, becoming just a handle once more.

In the next second, the doors were kicked open with enormous force. A fierce ocean squall of rain blew in. A group of armed crew members, who seemed to have gone through a fight, quickly rushed in.

However, when they saw the bloody scene within the casino, many of them involuntarily bent over and threw up. Some of them even fearfully aimed their weapons at Chen Chen who stood in the midst of innumerable sliced-up limbs.

The way they looked at Chen Chen was as if he was a demon from hell.

“Put down your weapons!”

Prince Steele’s voice rang out. Only then did they lower their guns slowly. At this time, Cheng Cao raced in as well, quickly moving to Chen Chen. “Boss, I didn’t carry out my duty...”

“It’s fine. I’m the one who asked you to pass the message to Prince Steele. Plus, there’s no signal on the ship. It’s not surprising that you couldn’t locate me.” Chen Chen waved his hand.

“Mr. Chen Chen!”

After ordering the crew to clean up the place and treat the passengers who were wounded, Prince Steele walked over with a bleak expression. “I didn’t think that Leo’s group would be deranged enough to want to kill everyone on board. If you hadn’t warned me, I’m afraid that the surprise attack of the pirates would’ve succeeded.”

“Your Highness is being humble. With your wisdom, you wouldn’t have fallen for a trap like this even without my warning.”

Chen Chen nodded slightly. They exchanged a few more words before Chen Chen, under the pretext of needing to rest from the shock, made to leave the casino and head to his lounge.

Prince Steele immediately sent a team of bodyguards to escort Chen Chen to the lounge.
