When they stepped out, the rainstorm had grown weaker. The once-glorious cruise ship was now scarred by burn marks in many places. Moreover, the closer they got to the cockpit, the more bullet holes they saw. A fierce shootout had taken place here.

Below the cruise ship, they could also vaguely make out the wreckage of several ships floating on the sea. Nevertheless, these ships had been blasted by RPGs.

At last, Chen Chen came to the lounge assigned to him on the cruise ship — a two-bedroom, four-parlor presidential suite on the 16th deck.

As Chen Chen and Cheng Cao entered the room, the team of bodyguards remained outside the door, guarding it faithfully.

“Boss!”

Inside, Qian Wenhuan, who had disappeared before, hurriedly sprang up from the sofa with a joyful expression. “Boss, are you okay...”

Chen Chen waved his hand to cut off Qian Wenhuan, then looked toward the door with a grim expression.

There was no denying that the old fox, Steele, was truly cold-blooded and selfish.

Generally speaking, mobile phones had no signal on the sea and only satellite antenna devices on ships could communicate with the outside world.

Therefore, when the cruise ship first docked, there would usually be a peak period of mobile phone usage among the passengers.

It was during this time that Little X discovered Leo, Davis, and the other heirs of the four families had secretly contacted the pirate factions from Africa, promising them the wealth of the entire “Symphony of the Seas”.

Their reason for doing this was to kill the patriarchs of the Alexander and the Wilson families.

After all, Chen Chen’s appearance had meant that these family patriarchs would enjoy a much longer lifespan and their reigning period would also increase greatly.

Therefore, these young men became restless.

During this trip, the patriarchs of the two families would be on board. This was a golden chance. If they could kill off those two old men, then Davis and the heir of the Alexander family could easily, naturally rise to power.

Thus, the four of them tried their best to rope in Chen Chen. If Chen Chen agreed to support them, of course, they would be delighted. They could even take action during the treatment, effortlessly killing off the patriarch of the Foster family so that Leo could take over.

Nevertheless, if Chen Chen did not agree, they could only send in their own killers and kill off Chen Chen as well.

After all, from their point of view, Chen Chen’s death would not matter. The operations of a company would not halt just because of one man. It was even possible that they could obtain the various life-extending technologies from Chen Chen’s company in the chaos.

This was what the four of them had in mind. In other words, their targets were the two patriarchs on board the ship.

However, assassinating the two patriarchs alone would surely make their motives too obvious — but what if the entire ship was caught in an incident?

At that point, they would just declare this as the work of pirates and only four of them managed to escape. This would appear perfectly reasonable.

This was the plan of Leo’s group.

Of course, Chen Chen would not allow all this to happen. Even putting aside joining Leo’s group, if Chen Chen did as he said and secretly killed the Foster patriarch during treatment, this would be a massive blow to the credibility of the research center.

Chen Chen was not short-sighted. No matter what benefits Leo could promise, they would never match the disadvantages in the long term. Of course, Chen Chen would not choose this.

Furthermore, a power struggle between Leo and his old man was bound to attract attention and concern. Chen Chen did not want this. To him, he would much rather that the eleven plutocrats grew comfortably richer day by day.

Chen Chen had also never considered using these methods to take over these financial groups. In any case, with his company’s business, he would only expand further. However, once he started to swallow up other companies or families, then he would be plagued with a crisis of credibility, no matter how squeaky clean or flawless his affairs were.

After all, how could the benefits of conquering one or two families in a short timeframe be on par with the benefits of conquering the entire Earth Federation a hundred years later?

There was a sudden dark gleam in Chen Chen’s eyes.

He just did not expect that even after he had warned Prince Steele upon boarding the ship, Prince Steele would merely guard against the sea more stringently, but still allowed Leo’s group to kill rampantly on board.

All this was not because Prince Steele did not trust Chen Chen. Rather, it was mainly because Prince Steele had never cared about the lives of his passengers in the first place.

After all, he had no way of verifying the validity of Chen Chen’s news and was also afraid of alerting his enemies. Thus, he merely prepared for any eventuality and secretly got ready to defend against any sea attacks.

If Leo’s group had contacted the pirates, then he who was prepared would naturally be able to meet the pirates head-on.

As for the guests on the ships, they would have to say their own prayers. In any case, Leo’s group did not possess any firearms. As long as the pirates on the sea were defeated, Leo’s side would be trapped like a rat in a cage.

This was Prince Steele’s thought process.

As for the casualties that might occur among the passengers — well, although those might damage his reputation a little, what were they compared to his own life?

chapters.title: Chapter 146: Customizing A Ship
chapters.body:
Chapter 146: Customizing A Ship

Chapter 146: Customizing a Ship

The pirate attack this time had resulted in a total of one hundred and thirty-five crew members and twenty-three passengers who were dead or injured.

After repelling the first onslaught by the pirates, Prince Steele immediately ordered the ship to turn back and they returned to Walvis Bay overnight.

As the host, Chen Chen casually paid for the remaining vacancies in all the hotels of Walvis Bay, so that the passengers who had escaped with their lives had a place to stay.

These poor people would probably never dare to set foot on a ship again.

As for this pirate attack, the Namibian authorities also expressed that they took this matter very seriously, stating that in the near future, combating pirate forces would be one of their basic national policies.

On Chen Chen’s side, the heirs of the four financial families had died at his hands. Naturally, what followed next was a whole lot of trouble. Even so, it was a good thing that there were so many witnesses in the casino, so there were no issues that could not be cleared up.

Moreover, since it was their foolish sons who wanted to usurp the throne, those fathers, as long as they were not completely useless, would not be oblivious to what was going on.

Chen Chen was not interested in the subsequent matters and simply handed over the whole shambles for Qian Wenhuan to deal with. He took the company’s negotiators with him and flew directly to Germany, Europe.

Germany was a nation under the federal system of the Earth Federation. It had suffered a disastrous defeat after World War II but in 1992, the United Nations’ five core members announced that the United Nations would officially transform into the Earth Federation, causing a thorough reshuffle of all the forces in the world. Germany slickly got rid of its image as a defeated country and had gained an opportunity to rise once more.

With Little X making the appointment, Chen Chen headed straight to the Meyer Shipyard in Papenburg, Germany, to discuss his order of a cruise ship with the shipyard’s boss.

There were only four shipyards in the world capable of building cruise ships of more than 200,000 tons, namely the Aker Shipyard in Finland, the Meyer Shipyard in Germany, the Atlantic Shipyard in France, and the Fincantieri Shipyard in Italy.

Although the Meyer Shipyard was not the most affordable one among these, it had the fastest construction speed among all shipyards.

The Meyer Shipyard was known as the “German Master”, being the largest and most modern shipyard in Germany. As soon as Chen Chen got off the plane, the manager of the Meyer Shipyard was there to welcome him.

After a round of greetings, they all got into the car and headed toward the headquarters of the Meyer Shipyard.

After that, Chen Chen toured the dock of the Meyer Shipyard, which was the largest indoor dock in the world up until now.

This dock was 504 meters long, 125 meters wide, and 75 meters tall. Being in it was like standing in the middle of a dragon’s nest, in which humans were just tiny bugs.

The person in charge of bringing Chen Chen around the dock was a middle-aged man in his forties, who had a goatee and a calm, steady temperament.

At this moment, the man was saying, “Mr. Chen Chen, that ends our tour. May I ask what sort of cruise ship you’re thinking of customizing?’

“Are there many types of cruise ships?” Chen Chen eyed a cruise ship under construction as he asked.

“Not that many, but according to the categories of economic level, cruise ships could be divided into economic and commercial cruise ships. Besides, there’s also the luxury cruise ship.”

The man with the goatee explained, “For example, the Oasis-class cruise ships, which are the largest in the world, are all luxury cruise ships.

“The main feature of a luxury cruise ship is to emphasize the travel experience of passengers. You should know that the size of the Oasis-class cruise ships can accommodate tens of thousands of passengers.

“However, to provide the passengers with a more superior experience, the cruise ship only designed more than 2,000 suites, which can only accommodate 6,000 people. Most of the space has been turned into public facilities for recreation.

“Moreover, the facilities in these ships are also top-notch.”

Mr. Goatee pointed at a ship under construction. “On the macro-scale, the restaurants; on the micro-scale, the furniture within the guest rooms — it’s just a ship cabin, but the furnishings within are that of a five-star hotel. This is what characterizes a luxury cruise ship.”

Chen Chen nodded as he listened. “What about in terms of the engine’s power system?”

“For their power systems, cruise ships have high standards for performance, noise, and other benchmarks. According to our company’s statistical analysis of global cruise ships, most luxury cruise ships use ‘diesel-electric’ propulsion power systems, accounting for more than 70%.

“Only 10 cruise ships are using the ‘combined diesel’ hybrid power system while 33 cruise ships are using diesel power systems. Only 1 cruise ship uses nuclear power for propulsion.”

Before Chen Chen could ask anything, Goatee automatically explained, “This ‘diesel-electric’ propulsion power employs diesel generators to generate electricity, providing electric power to the motor which drives the propellers. It’s extremely suitable for luxury cruise ships, both in terms of cost and performance.”

“What about nuclear energy?”

Chen Chen asked right away.

Goatee looked a little puzzled by this but still replied, “Although humans have created nuclear power plants, it must be said that the power in cruise ships is very different from land-based installations. The reactors are expensive and difficult to maintain. On a cruise ship, the investment needed for nuclear power is ten times that of ordinary mechanisms, which is very uneconomical.”

“I see.” Chen Chen nodded. “In that case, let’s use a diesel-electric system for my cruise ship, just like everyone else.”

“That would be most sensible.”

Goatee approved. “So, are you planning to customize the cruise now? I don’t know what your requirements are. We have a large number of ship designers who can help you with this.”

“I want my cruise ship to be as big as possible.”

Chen Chen thought for a while. “About 500 meters in length and 100 meters in width. The more power, the better. I hear that ships have more than one power system?”

“In most cases, cruise ships have one main power system and another backup system,” When Goatee heard the measurements Chen Chen listed out, his eyes bulged a little as he replied reflexively.

“In that case, I’ll double the whole thing.”

Chen Chen nodded. “I want the cruise ship to be equipped with two main power systems and two backup power systems.”

“...”

Goatee was startled and then he smiled drily. “Mr. Chen, I’m afraid there’s some misunderstanding. A power system includes diesel generators, electric motors, and other equipment. These devices are massive and extremely heavy. Four sets of power equipment would occupy the entire bottom of the hull. Economically speaking, it’s not advantageous...”

“That doesn’t matter.” Chen Chen shook his head. “I’m not building this cruise for profit, but to build a maritime experimental base.”

“A maritime experimental base?” Goatee sounded even more baffled.

Chen Chen nodded. “Yes, I want plenty of recreation facilities on my ship, but I don’t need guest suites and the like, just some dorms and laboratories at most. Plus, the laboratories need a lot of power system support.”

“I understand.”

Goatee finally understood and forced a smile. “In other words, you’re not planning to build a cruise ship, but a work ship?”

“Yes, a work ship that can be anchored in ‘international waters’ for experiments,” Chen Chen replied pointedly.

“Very well...”

Goatee wiped the sweat dripping from his forehead. “In that case, how much power are you expecting to use? If your boat needs so much room, we recommend that you choose our company’s largest Leroy-Somer LSA62 generator. Each generator produces around 20,000 kilowatts.”

“Of course, I’ll choose the most powerful one then,” Chen Chen replied matter-of-factly.

“That’s the wisest choice.”

Goatee was a little relieved. “We have 12 Leroy-Somer LSA62 generators left here. If you want to install a few extra for backup, how many generators would you like to buy?”

When Chen Chen heard this, he stretched out his hand and clenched it tightly into a fist before Goatee’s eyes.

“I want all of them!”

chapters.title: Chapter 147: Renting The Symphony Of The Seas
chapters.body:
Chapter 147: Renting The Symphony Of The Seas

Chapter 147: Renting the Symphony of the Seas

After establishing the fundamentals, it was time for the designer to design the cruise ship.

To show how highly they regarded Chen Chen, the general manager of the shipyard personally came over and appointed ten designers to listen to Chen Chen’s requests.

Like students in a class, the ten designers sat behind their desks, each with a tiny notebook, as they jotted down everything that Chen Chen said.

“I want the hull to be as large as possible. If it’s doable, the laboratories should be below the main deck of the hull. They must be sealed and well protected.

“I don’t need too many laboratories — just about ten large ones and forty to fifty medium and small ones.”

Chen Chen thought for a while, pointed to a diagram of a ship’s hull, and continued. “For everything above the deck, it can be similar to the ‘Symphony of the Seas’. I want a garden at the stern. It doesn’t have to take up a lot of space, just enough for those working on the ship to get a sense of reconnecting with nature.

“On both sides of the garden, there should be two blocks of equal height. The interior of these buildings should feature a duplex structure, best constructed as suites, with two bedrooms, one living room, and a balcony with a view of the sea.

“In this manner, the residents who live here can view the garden on one side and the sea on the other. Wouldn’t that be an enjoyable pleasure?

“Plus, there must be recreational venues like theatres, pools, and bars on board, along with restaurants and conference rooms. Besides, there should also be crew dormitories and training grounds.”

“The suites you mentioned — those won’t be the crew dormitories?” a designer asked in surprise.

“Those suites are for the researchers. The crew and guards working for the ship could be arranged to be on the lowest floor, to make emergency meetings more convenient.”

Chen Chen explained, “Also, there’s no need to be stingy in terms of space. After all, my ship won’t need to carry passengers. The rooms for the crew don’t need to be too fancy, but they shouldn’t be too small. Just design them as standard rooms.”

These designers took down Chen Chen’s main points in accordance with his planning, after which they started to design at once.

As for the size of the hull, Chen Chen first hoped that it would be more than five hundred meters in length. After all, a ship’s stability increased proportionally along with its volume. Nonetheless, this was discouraged by the Meyer Shipyard because if a ship was too massive, it was prone to more hidden risks in terms of building materials.

Therefore, the final hull was set to be 404 meters long, 80 meters wide, and 60 meters tall. There would be 8 layers above the deck, each 4 meters tall.

The length was not a round number because Chen Chen had particularly favored the number 404. It was just like how Chen Chen selected Dorm 404 back then. In Chinese culture, this number had a special significance...

After this group of designers did the calculations, the weight of this customized cruise ship would exceed three hundred and fifty thousand tons. It was one-and-a-half times heavier than the Symphony of the Seas, but because there were fewer guest rooms with luxurious furniture, the final price proposed by the factory was only one and a half billion USD, which was only two hundred million more than what the Symphony of the Seas had cost.

It would take about three months for the Meyer Shipyard to build the components of the hull. In addition to the time needed for the subsequent assembly and furnishing, it would take half a year in total.

Moreover, this was because they were using the modular construction method, just like building blocks.

If they had used the conventional construction method, such a gigantic cruise ship would take at least two years to be completed.

Finally, Chen Chen had to name this cruise ship.

This was because, according to international practice, when a ship’s construction had begun, a piece of steel plate would be cut into the shape of the hull’s silhouette or the initials of the ship’s name, marking the official beginning of the construction work.

Chen Chen thought it over and decided in the end. “In that case, I’ll name it ‘Blacklight’.”

Thus, a cruise ship named Blacklight began its construction process.

Although Blacklight was not the world’s biggest ship, it must be the biggest cruise liner in the world as of now.

As Chen Chen walked out of the Meyer Shipyard, he was suddenly at a loss for words.

Now he had another project under construction.

The earliest one was the Blacklight Biotechnology Namibian Nuclear Power Plant, a project which was planned to be finished in eighteen months. It was approved in March 2022 and by now, there were still 11 months left to completion.

Other than that, there was the Eco Science City, which was under the first phase of construction, along with the underground experimental base that would take four months to complete.

Today, he had another cruise ship in the works that would take half a year.

Even after all this, he still did not have any charging facilities he could use...

Chen Chen was rather speechless. In the next second, his phone started ringing.

He turned on the phone, only to see that it was Prince Steele calling.

Chen Chen frowned and wanted to decline the call, but answered it on second thought.

“Dear Chen, where have you been for the last two days?”

Prince Steele’s voice sounded a little worn out, but he still laughed out loud. “I want to have a drink with you to properly thank you, but I’ve been completely frazzled the past two days because of what happened.”

“It’s fine. When I helped you on the ship, I was also helping myself.”

Chen Chen said lightly, “I wonder if there’s a reason Your Highness is calling?”

“It’s nothing much. I just wanted to ask you something,” Prince Steele said, “back then, how did you know that Leo’s group had contacted the pirates and got them to attack the Symphony of the Seas?”

“Naturally, I have my own channels.”

Chen Chen raised an eyebrow. “Is there a problem?”

“No, no, please don’t get me wrong,” Prince Steele hastened to explain on the phone, “then, why did you still board the ship?”

“To be honest, if I had received the news earlier, I wouldn’t have boarded.”

Chen Chen replied, “But unfortunately, I only learned about that when I was on the ship, and by that point, the ship had already left the shore.”

The voice on the other end of the phone could not help laughing heartily. “That’s truly a shame, Mr. Chen Chen. Still, I want to thank you. If you need my help in any way, please let me know.”

When Chen Chen heard this, his heart leaped. “Since you’ve said that, there’s actually something you can help me with.”

“Oh?”

Prince Steele sounded a little startled.

“Is the Symphony of the Seas still parked at Walvis Bay?” Chen Chen asked.

“Yes. The Symphony of the Seas belongs to the European Continent, which has sent over investigators to find out what happened.”

Prince Steele said helplessly, “I’ve been asked not to leave Walvis Bay. And they’re looking for you, too.”

“Looking for me?”

Chen Chen nodded. “I got it. In that case, can you pass a message to the company that owns the Symphony of the Seas — tell them I’m willing to rent the Symphony of the Seas for half a year at the same price you did. I wonder if you can help me with this?”

“This...”

At this, Prince Steele sounded a little embarrassed. “Mr. Chen Chen, you may not know this. Although I rented the Symphony of the Seas at twenty million USD a month, surely you’ve seen the multitude of shops on board, which are rented by other business partners. Apart from the rent, I also had to pay a sum to these partners.”

“That’s fine, I can do that too.” Chen Chen was unconcerned.

“Since you’ve said that, things will be easier.” Prince Steele sighed in relief. “I believe that I still have a little influence in these matters.”

“In that case, I thank you, Your Highness.”

After exchanging a few more lines, Chen Chen hung up.

chapters.title: Chapter 148: Federal Bureau Of Investigation
chapters.body:
Chapter 148: Federal Bureau Of Investigation

Chapter 148: Federal Bureau of Investigation

A few days later, Chen Chen arrived at Walvis Bay once again.

This time, besides him and Cheng Cao, they were also accompanied by the local Namibian police chief, Maddaloni, and the captain of the Symphony of the Seas.

Chen Chen and his group got off the car on the dock and walked toward the large, mountainous ship that was not far away.

Under the cloudless clear sky, the huge cruise ship was still quietly moored by the dock. What was startling were the black burn marks from the explosions that were like scars on the body of the ship.

On the surface of the exquisite hull, they seemed exceptionally prominent.

“Mr. Chen Chen, this ship doesn’t belong to me personally, but since the day she went out to sea, I’ve been her father. To me, she’s just like my daughter...”

The white-bearded captain pleaded earnestly. “The Symphony of the Seas is now covered in scars. I hope that when you rent it, you can do a little light maintenance on these scars. You don’t need to spend too much time or money, a simple treatment is fine. For example, applying a little waterproof glue on the blemishes and...”

“No problem, Captain Bernard.”

Chen Chen was so amused that he could only nod. “Believe me, I’ll treat her well.”

“Thank the heavens, you’ll surely be a kind master.” When the white-bearded captain heard this, he sighed in relief.

“Sorry, but this ship is sealed off.”

However, just as they reached the entrance of the Symphony of the Seas, they were blocked by two European men dressed in black. One of them sized up the group, then said something indiscernible into his headset.

“These are the people from the European Federal Bureau of Investigation. Play nice as much as you can.” The Captain reminded Chen Chen in a low voice.

“I’m the chief of the Walvis Bay Police Station. We need to check out something on the ship. I hope that you can accommodate us.”

The black man who had remained silent all this while now spoke in a dignified tone as he showed his identification.

“All of you are going into the ship?”

When he heard this was the police, the European man lightened up a little, but still said, “There are too many of you. Only three can pass at most.”

“Very well, in that case, I, Chief Maddaloni, and Captain Bernard will go.” Chen Chen waved his hand and made the choice.

Seeing that they did not object, Chen Chen let Cheng Cao and the rest remained where they were while he moved on first.

On the ship, Chen Chen ignored the facilities on the main deck and immediately headed below. With Captain Bernard guiding the way, they reached the engine room.

“This machine that looks like a firing pin is the generator of the Symphony of the Seas.”

Captain Bernard pointed to the colossal component that was four to five meters high and dozens of meters long. “Although it looked different from diesel generators on land, there’s absolutely no problem with its power output.”

“I know. That’s a Leroy-Somer LSA62 generator with a power of 20,000 kilowatts,” Chen Chen replied without having to think.

“Huh, you’ve researched engines before?” Captain Bernard asked, astonished.

“It’s because I’ve just visited the Meyer Shipyard two days ago.”

Chen Chen smiled as he explained, “My customized cruise ship will also use this generator in the future.”

“I, I see...”

Captain Bernard gave a dry laugh and said nothing else.

The three of them did a quick tour around the bottom section. Chen Chen observed carefully and only nodded in satisfaction after he had walked around the entire generator room.

The three main generators and the two backup generators were all intact. The combined power of the five generators would reach a hundred thousand kilowatts.

One hundred thousand kilowatts of power meant that the power generated would be one hundred thousand kilowatts per hour. Even if these generators could not generate electricity at full power throughout the day, they could still generate at least ninety percent.

This level of power generation was one-tenth the efficiency of the power plant Chen Chen had used in Cape Town.

Although it was only one-tenth, Chen Chen only had five days to absorb the electricity from the power plant in Cape Town. That was far more restrictive compared to this ship, where he could absorb electricity anytime he liked.

Just when Chen Chen was about to disembark, his cell phone rang suddenly. Chen Chen turned it on to find that Little X had sent a message.

“Heh.” When he saw the message from Little X, Chen Chen chuckled, then immediately dialed Cheng Cao’s number.

“Ask everyone from the Black Security Service to gather at the dock.”

After giving that order, he hung up.

“Mr. Chen Chen, what’s going on?” On one side, Chief Maddaloni raised an eyebrow. He knew what sort of people were in the Black Security Service.

“Nothing much. Someone’s just trying to trap me.” Chen Chen shook his head.

“What, someone dares to trap you?”

When Chief Maddaloni heard this, he instantly said angrily, “I’ll call my officers from the police station!”

“No need for that. I’ll settle this myself.” Chen Chen smiled peculiarly as he stopped Chief Maddaloni from making the call.

What Chen Chen did not say was, if Chief Maddaloni called his officers over, the officers would end up having to help the other party.

“Very well.”

Seeing this, Chief Maddaloni said hotly, “If there’s any way I can help, just let me know.”

After making sure that the generators were working fine, Chen Chen and the rest made their way to the deck and strolled around. Chen Chen only suggested that they disembarked from the ship after Cheng Cao called to inform him that those from the Black Security Service had arrived.

The three of them quickly left the Symphony of the Seas.

However, just as they got off the ship and reached the dock, Chen Chen immediately saw twelve white Caucasians dressed in suits approaching him aggressively.

“Are you Mr. Chen Chen?”

The man at their head wore a pair of dark sunglasses, looking like Agent Smith from The Matrix.

At this moment, he was walking to Chen Chen as he flashed his identification. “We’re from the European branch of the Federal Bureau of Investigation. There’s a case that requires your cooperation. Please come with us.”

As he spoke, two large, imposing European men came up from behind, ready to lead Chen Chen away. One of them was even holding handcuffs.

“Hold on!”

Chief Maddaloni hurried forward and said in shock, “What are you doing? Mr. Chen Chen is our guest here in Namibia. You have no right to do this!”

“Sorry, but we’re asking Mr. Chen Chen to cooperate with the investigation on behalf of the Federation. Those who aren’t involved should stay out of this.” The man in sunglasses pushed Chief Maddaloni aside with an icy expression. Chief Maddaloni only flushed red but dared not retaliate.

“Just a minute.”

Chen Chen spoke breezily, but they did not seem about to stop. Just as the two men had moved to Chen Chen’s side and were about to seize him, several firearms popped out from behind Chen Chen and promptly pointed at the group of federal agents.

It turned out that, at an unknown time, Brando had arrived with the thirty members of the Black Security Service.

“Click click click!”

Thirty Glock 17s were aimed at the twelve agents without any hesitation. The federal agents were stunned. In a conditioned reflex, they drew their pistols and faced off against Brando and the others.

All at once, the atmosphere at the dock turned into one of taut hostility...

chapters.title: Chapter 149: Resolve
chapters.body:
Chapter 149: Resolve

Chapter 149: Resolve

At one end of the dock, two groups of people had suddenly drawn their firearms and were facing off. Behind them was the blue glassy stretch of ocean where seagulls and flamingos were wheeling about close to the water.

When Chief Maddaloni was confronted with this sudden turn of events, he was rather dumbfounded, not knowing which side he should assist.

According to international law, of course, he should help the Federal Bureau of Investigation. After all, this department could be said to be the Federation’s core law enforcement agency that possessed cross-continent authority in carrying out its duties.

Although some powerful regions had their own forces and did not give a f*ck about this agency, in a minor nation like Namibia, they did not have this capacity.

On the other hand, Chen Chen was a major bankroller to the authorities. If Chen Chen was apprehended in Namibia, Chief Maddaloni’s career would naturally come to an end. From then onwards, they could forget about Chen Chen continuing to invest in Namibia.

Chief Maddaloni was momentarily caught in a dilemma.

“Mr. Chen Chen, are you planning to rebel against the Federation?”

The man in sunglasses was fearless as he stared down the gun muzzles. Instead, he proclaimed, “We represent the Federal Bureau of Investigation. If you go against us, you’re going against the entire Earth Federation. Please think carefully.”

“I’ve always been a legitimate businessman and a law-abiding citizen. Why would I rebel against the Federation?”

Chen Chen smiled carelessly. “I’m just afraid that someone would impersonate the Federal Bureau of Investigation and try to kidnap me. After all, Officer, you must also know that I’m of considerable status. Many people have their eyes on me.”

“Is that so?”

The man in sunglasses narrowed his eyes. He took out his identification and held it before Chen Chen. “Have you got a good look now?”

“Sorry, I’m short-sighted. I can’t see it at such a distance.”

Chen Chen spread his hands innocently. “Please hold it closer.”

The man in sunglasses snorted coldly and walked closer, holding his identification right in front of Chen Chen’s eyes. “Surely you can see...”

“Swoosh!”

Just as he had placed it right before Chen Chen’s eyes, his vision went blurry for a split second. The identification he was holding was now in Chen Chen’s hands!

“You!”

The man in sunglasses bellowed in rage.

“Shh —”

Chen Chen raised an index finger, making a silencing gesture. Following that, he slowly tore the entire document in half in front of the man while a sneer curled upon his lips. “This identification is fake!”

“How dare you!”

The man in sunglasses stepped forward, but Cheng Cao’s gun was directly pressed into his face.

Chen Chen tore the document into fragments and raised one hand. Countless shreds of leather and paper fluttered through the sky. Meanwhile, Chen Chen walked forward at once without even glancing at the ashen expression of the man in sunglasses.

“Mister Officer, next time, remember to bring some authentic identification before you come.”

“Chen Chen!” The man in sunglasses clenched his fist, yearning to arrest his target. Even so, with thirty guns trained on him, he dared not take a single step past the boundary.

Each of Chen Chen’s bodyguards was bursting with a bloodthirsty aura. Were these still bodyguards? They were a group of savage mercenaries!

This businessman from Mainland China had nurtured his own private army!

Cheng Cao took the lead in putting his gun away only when Chen Chen had got into the car. Then, he made a gesture and everyone put away their firearms, turned, and walked away.

This display of extreme obedience caused the man in sunglasses to narrow his eyes.

He watched steadily as Chen Chen got into the car, then tore off his sunglasses and flung them onto the ground. At the same time, he glared at the black police chief beside him. “Chief Maddaloni, where are your officers? Even for a case under our Federal Bureau of Investigation, your local officers won’t cooperate at all?”

“I...”

Chief Maddaloni opened his mouth with a blank expression.

“Sir, we...”

The deputy on the side was about to ask the man in sunglasses a question.

“Enough. We’ll just report this to the higher-ups. Those who resist the Federal Bureau of Investigation will get what they deserve!”

With that, the man who wore sunglasses dusted off his hands and stalked off in the other direction.

Chen Chen sat in the car, silently watching the departing federal agents with a grave expression.

While he was on the Symphony of the Seas, Little X had sent a message, saying that a group of agents sent by the Federal Bureau of Investigation had reached Walvis Bay. Moreover, they had completed the extradition procedures and were ready to transfer him to the North American Continent while he was caught off guard.

In truth, if the Federal Bureau of Investigation had just come to question him, there would be no issue at all.

However, the crucial thing was that the Symphony of the Seas was a cruise liner owned by Royal International Cruise Line, which was an European company, just like how the man in sunglasses and his team belonged to the European branch of the Federal Bureau of Investigation.

Therefore, even if Chen Chen was extradited, he should be extradited to Europe, not North America.

Something fishy was certainly going on.

Indeed, Little X now sent another message.

Through targeted investigations, Little X had eliminated endless information on the Internet and finally came up with some useful data.

It turned out that several financial groups in North America had formed an organization called the “Als Mutual Aid Association”. Through certain channels, this organization learned about the technologies Chen Chen possessed and started to get its own ideas.

The plutocrats in this mutual aid association came from families that were not inferior to financial oligarchs such as the Edwards and the Rodriguezes. Naturally, they were not afraid of the eleven financial groups backing Chen Chen.

“Sure enough, some things can’t be hidden forever.”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes. Although God’s Forbidden Zone had never shown itself in formal events, no secret could be kept forever. Furthermore, so many members of the families had regained their youth. Once something like this was exposed, it would instantly spread like wildfire among the upper classes.

Presumably, many plutocrats had learned of all this by now.

At first, they were merely onlookers, but now that Chen Chen was involved in a case, of course, those who were eager would take advantage of this, using certain methods to test Chen Chen.

With the lure of technologies that could restore one’s youth, even the upper echelons of the Federation would be highly tempted, to say nothing of those plutocratic families.

If Chen Chen could not even handle this first wave of challenges, naturally, he would be seen as a weak target to be bullied. In that case, more forces would surround and assault him.

It was akin to a pack of wolves that smelled blood, gradually entrapping a sheep in their midst.

“Since that’s the case, you can’t blame me...”

A fiery light flashed in Chen Chen’s eyes as he mumbled softly.

Although he could rely on the power of the eleven great families to resolve this crisis, surely that would be going too easy on his opponents? Since they had extended their claws toward Chen Chen, they should leave their claws with him.

Even if they were to end up sitting down and talking it out, Chen Chen would have to rip off their claws first before doing so.

Otherwise, would people not think that they could sh*t on his head in the future?

With this in mind, Chen Chen opened Little X’s app on the phone and said in a low tone, “X, hack into the airline’s network and book two tickets for me and Cheng Cao under fabricated identities. You know the destination...”

chapters.title: Chapter 150: Kidnapping
chapters.body:
Chapter 150: Kidnapping

Chapter 150: Kidnapping

In that same afternoon, Chen Chen and Cheng Cao both boarded a plane to New York.

After only five hours, when it was evening, two of them stepped into a narrow but busy street in Manhattan, New York City.

This street was only five hundred meters in length and a pitiful eleven meters in width. There were various sorts of towering buildings on both sides. Even on a sunny day, there was no sunlight here to speak of.

Nonetheless, this haphazardly planned street was well known throughout the entire Federation.

In one glance, Chen Chen could see the headquarters of Citibank, JPMorgan Chase, the New York Stock Exchange, and other buildings.

This location represented the financial hub of the entire North American Continent. It was Chen Chen’s current destination — the Blanc Company’s headquarters, owned by Paul Blanc, the president of the Als Mutual Aid Association.

Paul Blanc was the founder of the Blanc Financial Group. Compared to other long-established groups, they were a relatively young financial group first set up by Harlan Blanc in 1922.

The group started as the “National Bank”. In World War II, Harlan Blanc and several other consortia of the new generation took control of the oldest aluminum company in North America and joined the ranks of financial oligarchs.

Through these channels, the Blanc Financial Group controlled the banking and industrial capital of the Pittsburgh area. In the following decades, his son Paul Blanc gained control of Gulf Oil, and the family became a top consortium in one leap.

This company had also become the most important industrial pillar of the Blanc Financial Group.

Through these years of development, the Blanc Financial Group finally broke into Wall Street and became a major puppet master of North American finance and economy.

Chen Chen’s current opponent was a colossal figure like this.

“In other words, the one who tried to trap me this time was Paul Blanc, the president of the Als Mutual Aid Association and leader of the Blanc Financial Group...”

Chen Chen was in a black car. As he read the information sent by Little X, he displayed a pensive look.

He read on in the car, then looked out the window again. On a building by the street, a massive B-shaped logo was ostentatiously displayed, resonating with the logos of other financial groups.

This building was the headquarters of the Blanc Company.

“Cheng Cao, this is the man you’re looking for.”

Chen Chen took out his phone and sent Cheng Cao a photo of a middle-aged Caucasian man who had swept-back hair and a steely expression. “Sloane Blanc. He’s in the general manager’s office. Just knock him out and bring him out. Remember not to alert anyone else.”

“Got it.”

Cheng Cao glanced at the photo, nodded with a grimace, got off the car, and walked directly toward the main doors of the Blanc Financial Group headquarters.

Soon, Cheng Cao pushed past the heavy glass doors and said something to the front desk. Then, he moved out of Chen Chen’s field of vision.

At the same time, all surveillance devices in the building suddenly became ineffective as they fell into a mode of playing videos on loop. The security in the building did not notice this at all.

As remote surveillance was often needed, most foreign surveillance systems were connected to an external network. Due to this, Little X was able to interfere with the building’s surveillance.

With help from Little X, in addition to Cheng Cao’s GS protective suit and cloaking device, every defensive measure would be futile against Cheng Cao, unless the other party had already prepared and were lying in wait.

Indeed, in a little over ten minutes, the glass doors of the Blanc Financial Group headquarters suddenly swung open by themselves as if there was a strong wind. Those at the front desk merely glanced up in bafflement, then stopped paying attention.

However, if one looked closely, one would notice a mass of distortion in the air walking out from the doors and making a beeline for the car in which Chen Chen was waiting.

“Yawn!”

Chen Chen yawned and opened the trunk of the car which was facing the Blanc Financial Group headquarters. Instantly, the mass of distorted light moved into the back of the car.

At this point, the cloaking function was halted.

Chen Chen looked down. A middle-aged Caucasian man was lying in the back seat. It was the man in the photo sent by Little X.

This was Sloane Blanc, the son of the Blanc family’s leader, Paul Blanc. Paul Blanc was on the verge of retiring and Sloane Blanc had assumed control over ninety percent of the family business. He was long recognized as the heir of the Blanc family.

Chen Chen glanced at this important, famous heir of the Blanc family, then stopped paying further attention. Cheng Cao had turned around and got into the car, driving it toward the suburbs of New York.

It was not until Cheng Cao had driven past the suburbs of New York that the employees in the Blanc building gradually realized their own general manager had seemed to vanish...

The entire building descended into an instant state of panic.

A few hours later, with Cheng Cao driving, large private estates slowly loomed up on both sides of the road.

They had entered the northern suburbs of New York City, which was where the rich lived.

In New York City, the rich usually lived near Manhattan Island. There were all kinds of modern duplex mansions and high-rise buildings everywhere. It was a sharp departure from the lifestyles of ordinary people.

Nonetheless, to some of the wealthy older generation, this dense, bustling area was not suited to them. Therefore, suburbs for the wealthy came into existence.

These residences in the suburbs of New York were all manors. In truth, this was where the truly wealthy gathered and lived in New York. Only the richest could stay here.

Chen Chen felt that this location was about right, so he asked Cheng Cao to stop. After that, he looked back at Sloane Blanc who was still knocked out and took out a surgical box from the car’s refrigerator, from which he withdrew a large number of drugs.

Following that, Cheng Cao cloaked himself and picked up Sloane Blanc. Meanwhile, Chen Chen got out of the car, put on a duckbill cap and a mask, and walked toward the only five-star hotel nearby.

Chen Chen opened the doors to the hotel and walked straight to the counter. “My name is Stephen Chow. I’ve booked Presidential Suite No. 3.”

“Please hold on.”

The gorgeous female receptionist behind the counter shot Chen Chen a weird look before lowering her head to check. Then, she confirmed. “Yes, Mr. Chow, you’ve booked it this morning. Do you need someone to take you to the room?”

“It’s fine. Just tell me which floor.” Chen Chen turned down her suggestion.

When she heard this, the receptionist handed a gold-inlaid card to Chen Chen. “Your room is on the 7th floor.”

“Thank you.”

Chen Chen took the card and headed toward the elevator.

As she watched this guest, who had concealed his appearance, turn to leave, the female receptionist shook her head speechlessly. Although Chen Chen’s getup was not a common sight in hotels, it could still be explained.

He must be a certain celebrity or a wealthy man who was cheating on his wife or a celebrity who was cheating on his wife...

She had worked here for over three years and had seen many of these sorts of affairs.

However, when she was lowering her head absentmindedly, she suddenly felt that something was wrong. Why did there seem to be a distortion in the air behind the guy?

The receptionist quickly looked up, only to see that Chen Chen had gotten into the elevator. Noticing her gaze, he even nodded back at her.

She swiftly looked back down. When she glanced up again after a while, the doors of the elevator had closed.

Were her own eyes just playing tricks on her?

The female receptionist could not help but feel unsettled.

chapters.title: Chapter 151: Kidney Operation
chapters.body:
Chapter 151: Kidney Operation

Chapter 151: Kidney Operation

After reaching the presidential suite in the hotel, Chen Chen first checked the entire room.

After making sure that there were no cameras installed here, Cheng Cao showed himself and placed the unconscious Sloane Banc into the bathtub in the bathroom.

As this was a five-star hotel, the entire room was luxuriously decorated. Even the bathtub was a Duravit and was large enough to hold two people.

The bathroom walls were made out of translucent glass, situated in the center of the bedroom. One could vaguely see into the bathroom no matter where one stood.

Seeing this design, Chen Chen could not help reflecting that it was truly appropriate for this dandy they had on their hands. Following that, he called the front desk. In a short while, a hotel attendant brought about a dozen ice trays.

After thanking and tipping the attendant, Chen Chen locked the door and handed the ice trays to Cheng Cao.

While Cheng Cao was making the ice cubes, Chen Chen returned to the bathroom and took out a pair of rubber gloves and a mask from the surgical box with a stoic expression.

After putting on the mask, Chen Chen took off Sloane’s shirt and quickly disinfected his patient with iodophor. After all, their medical resources were limited here. This was the best Chen Chen could do.

After the disinfection, Chen Chen used his fingers to measure the width of two fingers below the man’s lower left ribs. Then, taking note of that point, Chen Chen retrieved a medical marker from the medical box and starting from the spot, drew a line over the left iliac fossa, all the way to the lower abdomen.

The iliac fossa was the bone that held up one’s belt. At this time, Chen Chen drew a line about twenty centimeters long on the left.

After he was done with the left side, Chen Chen drew a line on the right in a symmetrical manner.

The incision had to be this big so that the operation would be safer. After all, the patient’s safety must be ensured. As the medical conditions of the hotel were lacking, Chen Chen could not use minimally invasive methods.

After this, Chen Chen took note of the time and injected a second anesthetic into the comatose middle-aged man, preventing the patient from waking up in the middle of the operation.

After injecting the anesthetic, Chen Chen took out a towel provided by the hotel, bolstered Sloane Blanc’s waist, and immediately took out a No. 10 scalpel, gently cutting it along the line drawn on the left...

Instantly, dark red blood flowed soundlessly from the wound. It looked particularly garish against the clean, white tub.

Still, this was just blood from the capillaries, which was like a drop in the ocean compared to the entire body. Chen Chen took out some surgical cotton and wiped the wound.

After confirming that the epidermis had been fully incised, Chen Chen put down the No. 10 scalpel and instead, picked up a sharper No. 11 scalpel. He began to cut the subcutaneous tissue.

After several layers of skin had been completely cut, Chen Chen took out the hemostatic forceps and fastened it onto the wound. Then he used scissors to cut the oblique aponeurosis. At this point, the organs of his patient’s abdomen were completely exposed.

Chen Chen took out some tools and started to clean the wound. With his hands, he moved Sloane’s colon out of the way. Only then was an object encased in yellow fat revealed to him.

That was a kidney.

Chen Chen felt around the kidney to ascertain the position of the renal artery, the renal vein, and the ureter. After that, he cleaned off the fat on the kidney until the three tubes were fully exposed.

Generally speaking, a longer section of the kidney pedicle should be preserved during kidney transplantation to facilitate the transplantation, but Chen Chen did not intend to let his dear patient proceed with a transplantation.

Therefore, Chen Chen first clamped the artery and vein of the kidney with the hemostatic forceps, then cut off the ducts with a diagonal stroke of a scalpel. All at once, a live kidney had been extracted.

Chen Chen put the left kidney aside into the surgical tray, picked up the suture thread from the surgical box, and began to suture the patient’s wound.

Chen Chen sutured with great concentration. First, he stopped the bleeding of the severed vein and artery to an adequate extent, then sutured the vascular stump. After that, he returned the intestines to their initial position and performed the final wound suture.

When he sutured, Chen Chen used the internal figure-of-eight suturing method, employing absorbable biological thread to close the wound from inside. In this way, the thread would not be visible externally.

This method of suturing could also minimize the formation of scars.

Forty minutes later, Chen Chen finally straightened up. Before him, Sloane Blanc, this strong European Adonis, looked just as he did before. If a difference must be pointed out, it would be that his face was paler, like someone who had just recovered from a serious illness.

In addition, there was a twenty-centimeter-long wound on Sloane’s left waist.

However, this was not over. Chen Chen picked up the No. 10 scalpel he had used just now and cut along the line on the right side...

After another forty minutes, when Chen Chen finally straightened up, there were two kidneys in the surgical tray in front of him.

He just did not know whether this dandy could continue fooling around now that his two kidneys were gone.

Chen Chen shook his head. It was not that he was cruel, but this man had provoked him first. Although Paul Blanc was the one who decided to capture Chen Chen, it was the heir of the Blanc family who made the proposal.

If Chen Chen was captured, it was obvious that a set of extremely cruel punishments would await Chen Chen until he disappeared from the face of the earth.

Now, Chen Chen was not even after this man’s life, only his two kidneys. Chen Chen was already being kind.

After finishing with this, Chen Chen turned and exited the bathroom. In a short while, there was a clinking noise from the refrigerator in the living room. Cheng Cao carried a bucket of solid ice cubes and poured them directly onto Sloane.

“Alright, we can leave now.”

Chen Chen looked at the time and started cleaning up the medical waste in the bathroom. He even intentionally placed a phone on the sink so that Sloane could call for help as soon as he woke up. At the same time, Chen Chen took out the medical marker and wrote “Happy Birthday” on the wall.

If the information supplied by Little X was correct, today was indeed the birthday of the young master, Sloane Blanc.

According to Chen Chen’s calculations, the anesthetic would only wear off in about an hour. After that, this playboy heir would wake up from his induced stupor. At that time, he would naturally know what to do, so Chen Chen did not need to worry.

By the time they left the hotel, it was already late at night.

Chen Chen wrapped up the two kidneys. He did not get into the car but headed directly to a private estate nearby.

This private manor was the residence of the Blanc family.

In other words, Chen Chen had removed Sloane Blanc’s two kidneys right under the nose of the Blanc family.

As Chen Chen approached the manor, he could sense the obvious tension in the air. News of Sloane Blanc’s disappearance had reached this place. Currently, the security in this estate was much tighter.

Nevertheless, this was nothing to Chen Chen. He immediately took out a small silver-white ball from his pocket and tapped it lightly.

There was an abrupt electromagnetic crackle. Chen Chen’s figure faded from solidity to transparency, until he finally melded into the night.

This was the cloaking device from The Predator.

The principle behind this device was not complicated. It formed a magnetic field around the user, rendering the effect of invisibility by distorting the light waves around the user.

It was because of this that this distortion would become apparent if the user encountered the flow of water, thus temporarily losing the invisibility effect.

It was late at night and Chen Chen did not have to worry about being spotted. He walked to the wall of the private manor, did a quick run-up, and kicked off the wall with both feet.

Instantly, he flew up over two meters high. His arms pushed off the top of the wall, and his entire body flipped over the wall as if he was climbing over a railing.

“Thud...”

There was a soft noise as Chen Chen landed lightly on the lawn on both feet.

When he stood up, Chen Chen noticed that there was a sprawling garden before him. It was bursting with colorful blossoms, creating breathtaking scenery.

A hundred meters away, there was a lavish, large manor, like the castle of a villa that towered over a kingdom of flowers.

This was where the Blanc family stayed.

chapters.title: Chapter 152: Getting Acquainted
chapters.body:
Chapter 152: Getting Acquainted

Chapter 152: Getting Acquainted

“He’s not found yet?

“Okay, please look carefully. No matter what tactics they used to abduct Sloane, he can’t be brought too far away. I think that it’s even likely that Sloane is still in the building, only he’s hidden away.”

In a dim study room, a white-haired old man slowly hung up the phone with a grave expression.

The old man was in his late years. When one became old, one disliked harsh light. Therefore, there were no lights in the study room, only the faint glimmer of the glowering fire in the fireplace.

Like many elderly people, this old man was not particularly fond of installations like floor heating and air conditioning. Instead, he preferred a fireplace. This furnishing allowed him to recall the memories of his youth.

Currently, there was a brand new file in front of the old man.

There was a photo attached to the file, showing a young man with ordinary features. Upon this file, the name of this young man was stated to be — Chen Chen.

“Could it be him?”

The old man mumbled softly.

Just last month, the Als Mutual Aid Association sent a message that the members of the Alexander family, the Edwards family, and the Rodriguez family, who were all based in Wall Street, had simultaneously regained their youth over the past months.

However, this did not attract everyone’s attention at first.

After all, there was such a thing called plastic surgery in this world. As long as one was willing to spend some money, it was not difficult to make a sixty, seventy-year-old fogey look like he was forty or fifty years old. It was just that most people thought this was unnecessary.

Even if they looked younger after plastic surgery, they would still be the age that they had always been. Their bodies could not possibly become young just because their appearance did.

Even so, as everyone received more successive news updates, they could no longer sit still.

They discovered that the members of these families had not only become younger in appearance, but their physical fitness had also improved significantly!

No, more accurately, those members of the families now looked younger because the youth of their bodies had been comprehensively restored.

In other words, those people had come across a technology that could turn them young again!

Once this conclusion was drawn, Paul Blanc could not sit still at all.

By now, Paul, who was over eighty, had long been in his sunset years. Nonetheless, he had always maintained an optimistic outlook. He still firmly believed that as technology evolved day by day, life-extending technologies would emerge sooner or later.

Due to this, he had invested in more than ten types of technologies that might extend one’s lifespan over a decade ago.

Even so, what followed next was an encounter with the cruel reality.

Three of the technologies he had invested in quickly announced failure while another four ended up the same halfway through the research. As for the remaining three, the researchers took his money, moved abroad, and changed their names, never to show themselves again...

As these technologies failed one after another, Paul’s heart gradually fell to the bottom of the pit.

Finally, he even started to suspect that perhaps the world would never allow the existence of life-extending technologies?

However, it was not until he saw the message from the Als Mutual Aid Association that hope slowly blossomed in his heart.

According to the data, the reason this group of people had regained their youth was not plastic surgery. Rather, it was because they had gone to Namibia and met with a company named Blackwatch.

This company seemed to own a safe and effective life-extending technology.

Therefore, after investigating Chen Chen’s family background, a feeling known as greed started to swell in him.

In the end, he agreed to the mutual aid association’s suggestion that they kidnapped Chen Chen for interrogation. After all, as an ordinary citizen with no influence in society, the mentality of having his life in the hands of others must have been ingrained into Chen Chen’s heart, even if he was not aware of this.

However, for some reason, after Paul gave the command, the Bureau of Investigation failed in their mission.

Not only that, but before the news of this failure had spread around, his son, Sloane Blanc, went missing in broad daylight.

The first thing Blanc thought of was that this was his opponent’s revenge.

Paul’s eyes flickered with a touch of regret. Before this, he had only considered the possibility of extending his own life. All this was too tempting to him, who would see the inside of a coffin soon. Therefore, he had resorted to extreme methods without thinking twice.

He had thought that it would be a cinch, never imagining that, not only did he fail in kidnapping his target, but he also managed to gain a deadly foe.

As he thought of this, Paul picked up the phone and dialed a number in the directory.

“It’s me.”

“Please investigate — I need all the information you can get on Blackwatch Corporation. I suspect they have something to do with Sloane’s disappearance...

“Thank you.”

After hanging up, Paul started to flip through the information which had been flipped to tatters by now. However, just then, there was a sharp burst of applause from behind him.

This sound came from behind Paul but he could swear that, ever since he had sat down here in the afternoon, not a single living soul had come into this study room...

If this sound did not come from his bodyguards or servants, could it be...

Paul’s heart sank at once. He took a deep breath and slowly turned around. Instantly, he thought that he saw a figure standing in the corner of the room.

No, someone was standing there indeed!

The sound seemed mocking, but no matter how the old man stared, with his fading, blurry vision, he could only make out a figure and nothing else.

“Clap clap clap clap...”

The applause kept ringing out from that corner, following which a slightly raspy voice spoke, “Your Excellency Paul, have you already thought of the reason so soon?”

“Who are you?”

When he heard this voice, Paul Blanc said quietly, “What are you doing in my manor? If it’s money you want, I promise you that you can pick any item you like and leave without trouble.”

“No, no, no, I’m not interested in material items. I’m not here for money either.”

The voice was soft and raspy, with a taunting lilt. “I came here with a gift for you, Your Excellency.”

“A gift?”

Upon hearing that this person was not a thief, Paul was not surprised. In any case, if this man was a thief — regardless of whether he was able to sneak in through the strictly guarded periphery of the manor — if he made it in, he would stay as hidden as possible. Why would he reveal himself?

“It’s a simple gift.”

The voice continued. “But I think you’ll like it.”

With that, Paul saw a plastic bag flying through the dark air toward him.

Paul did not hesitate and immediately swiped the air with one hand, grabbing the plastic bag.

There was a cold, squishy texture within the plastic bag, like two slippery pieces of meat. Paul frowned and slowly opened the bag. A pungent stench leaped out at him.

Paul’s sense of uneasy premonition grew heavier.

“Who are you and what is this?”

“A pair of kidneys. Can’t you even recognize them?”

The dark figure laughed softly. “I picked it up nearby your house and thought that someone from your family must’ve dropped it. So, I’m sending it back to you. As for my identity...

“Weren’t you just looking at it?”

As the figure spoke, he slowly moved out of the darkness to an area illuminated by the firelight. A face, identical to the young man on the file, was revealed.

There was a hint of a smile on the young man’s lips and a malicious glint in his eyes. These eyes were now staring coldly at Paul.

Confronted with this familiar face, Paul’s jaw slowly slackened as disbelief crept over his features.

“How could this be, it’s actually... You...”

chapters.title: Chapter 153: Submission
chapters.body:
Chapter 153: Submission

Chapter 153: Submission

Paul, whose eyesight was poor due to old age, finally got a good look at the visitor’s face.

However, this face was incomparably familiar to him.

As if in disbelief, Paul lowered his head and looked at the file on the desk, only to see that the photo on the file looked the same as the youth before him...

“You’re Chen Chen...”

Paul gradually raised his head and muttered, “You were clearly still in Namibia this morning. How can you suddenly show up here? How is it possible...”

“It’s not impossible, Mr. Paul.”

Chen Chen slowly walked to the swivel chair opposite Paul and sat down nonchalantly. “Weren’t you the one who wanted me here? Here I am. And I even brought you a gift.”

At the mention of the gift, Paul’s face darkened. He drew in a breath and asked in a low voice, “What did you do to my son, Sloane?”

“Be rest assured, I didn’t kill him.”

Chen Chen turned his palms up and looked at the Rolex on his wrist. “In just about half an hour, you’ll hear about him.

“Still, I wanted to give your son a piece of advice — in the future, he shouldn’t fool around so much. You know, doing all that isn’t good for your kidneys...”

When Paul heard these words, he stared at the object within the plastic bag in his hand and his heart sank completely.

At the same time, with his other hand that was under the desk, he gingerly opened a drawer, revealing an intricate, polished pistol.

“Your pupils are dilating.”

Just as Paul was about to take the gun, Chen Chen suddenly spoke again, “Not only that but your breathing has become heavier than before. My, you’re truly tensed up. Let me guess — you’re thinking of taking the pistol in the drawer?”

On the other side of the desk, Paul froze. Cold sweat trickled down his forehead.

Although the room was pleasantly warm because of the fireplace, Paul only felt a chill all over his body. The young man opposite him was not doing anything, yet for some reason, he had the impression that he was faced with an extremely ruthless beast.

It was as if this person might erupt at any second and tear him to pieces...

“Everyone must take responsibility for their own actions. Isn’t that so?”

Chen Chen tapped his head and chuckled lightly. “Since the Als Mutual Aid Association tried to cause me trouble, of course, I had to return the favor. If you want to shoot me, then naturally, you’d have to bear the corresponding consequences — two people tried the same thing once and they both ended up dead.”

Paul’s heart descended into the pit of his stomach.

Right, this young man had the ability to appear in his study room without a warning. Would a pistol save him now?

Not even Paul himself believed that.

“So, what do you want?”

As Paul observed the young man sitting opposite him, he fought down the anxiety rising in him. “I can make up for it. How does one billion USD sound?”

“Do you think you’re trying to send away a beggar, Mr. Paul?”

Chen Chen leaned forward and gazed unwaveringly at the white-haired old man across the desk. “Let me suggest an alternative. I want you to be my dog, serving me unconditionally for thirty years.”

“What did you say?”

Paul’s face turned white, with a trace of fury in his voice. “That’s impossible...”

“Nothing is impossible!”

Chen Chen resolutely cut off Paul. “Paul, you know very well that your body might not last even three years. In that case, if you follow me, I can grant you a long life...”

Chen Chen’s voice suddenly softened enticingly. “Just like the Alexander and the Edwards families who’ve attained young bodies and gained decades of life. Isn’t that what you’ve always wanted?”

“...”

Paul suddenly hesitated when he heard these words, appearing to be a little swayed. Even so, he whispered, “I will not betray my family, even if that means death.”

“No one’s asking you to betray your family,” Chen Chen said breezily, “not only that, but your family can also rise further in my hands, even reaching the level of the top ten financial groups in the North American Continent.”

Paul fell silent.

“And all you have to do is to be under my restraints. I’ll install a chip in you. After that, I’ll control your every aspect, including your life...”

After Chen Chen finished his speech, he showed no signs of impatience. He simply sat in the opposite chair, serene and composed while his right hand lightly tapped the desk.

“Thump!”

“Thump!”

“Thump...”

The tapping on the desk, to Paul, sounded like the thumping of his own heart.

A few minutes later, the phone on Paul’s desk suddenly rang.

“Ring ring...”

The abrupt phone call made Paul jump in fright. He glanced at Chen Chen reflexively and was about to cut the call when Chen Chen gestured “go ahead”.

After a pause, Paul slowly picked up the phone.

“Master, we’ve found Young Master Sloane!”

The moment he accepted the call, the butler’s flustered voice burst through the receiver. “Young Master Sloane is in the Hilton Hotel near the manor. We’ve found him!”

“What is he doing there?”

Paul shot a look at Chen Chen across the desk. “Is Sloane injured?”

“Young Master Sloane is...”

The voice hesitated. “Someone had operated on him. We’ve just located him and are about to send him to the hospital for an examination.”

“Got it...”

Paul gently closed his eyes when he heard this, then hung up.

“Have you decided?”

Chen Chen said, “If you want to protect your family with your own life, it’s pointless. Don’t forget, I can remove Sloane Blanc from the company building and bring him here, then appear before you anytime I like. In the same manner, I can visit your heir.”

As Chen Chen spoke, he took out a document and tossed it onto the desk. “And what if I say that there’s still hope for Sloane?”

When Paul heard this, he looked down skeptically, only to see that the document was an explanation of various technologies.

There was the anti-aging treatment that he had dreamed of, the taboo technology of human cloning that was banned across the entire Federation, even the drug technology for treating Alzheimer’s...

The more he read, the more astounded Paul became.

By the end, Paul inhaled deeply and slowly looked up. “What price must I pay to access these technologies?”

“As long as you submit to me.”

Chen Chen stood up and spread his hands softly. “Your Excellency Paul Blanc, I admire your talents. It took you and your father a lifetime to turn an ordinary company into a financial behemoth. Although you disrespected me once, I’m still willing to give you a chance. As long as you submit to me, you and your family will thrive and prosper.

“Of course, you may refuse as well!”

Chen Chen changed his tone now. “After you refuse me, I’ll slaughter the entire Blanc family, erasing your family completely from the history of North America. No matter how much glory you’ve achieved before, I’ll wipe out everything!”

As Chen Chen spoke, the corners of Paul’s eyes twitched involuntarily.

“What are you waiting for? You’ve worked so diligently your whole life — for what? For money?

Chen Chen started with his questions. “It’s alright, your money still belongs to you!

“For your family? Your family can become stronger than ever.

“For status?

“You’ll remain the master of the Blanc family. Not only that, but you’ll also gain higher authority, and even command the entirety of North America! And all you have to do is submit to me!”

After Chen Chen spoke, Paul suddenly inhaled sharply and slowly closed his eyes.

A long time passed. When he opened his eyes again, there was no flicker of doubt. He merely stood up, walked toward Chen Chen, then...

He knelt and bowed down.

chapters.title: Chapter 154: Chen Chen’S Oath
chapters.body:
Chapter 154: Chen Chen’S Oath

Chapter 154: Chen Chen’s Oath

Paul Blanc disappeared.

Nobody knew where he went, he vanished just like his child, Sloane Blanc, did previously.

Just as the entire Blanc family began mobilizing their forces in full to search for traces of Paul, he suddenly appeared again at the gate of their manor.

Nobody knew where he went in the duration he went missing. Paul did not intend to bring it up either.

Shortly after that, the bedridden Sloane Paul was suddenly flown out to Namibia. Word was there was an institute known as Blackwatch Research Center that could cure Sloane of his illness.

This was a subject of interest for countless people.

Most of the general public could not make out the connection between Blackwatch and the Blanc Financial Group. However, what were the chances of the top business circles of the North American continent not knowing about Als Mutual Aid Association’s previous attempt at targeting Blackwatch?

As it turned out, not only did Sloane Blanc have his kidneys taken away as an act of vengeance, even the head of the Blanc family went missing for two days. Furthermore, he did not comment about Sloane’s incident upon reappearing again.

Even without having the specific details, it was quite apparent that Blanc Financial Group’s maneuvers proved to be completely ineffective against the new Blackwatch Corporation. Apart from that, they seemed to be forced to make a compromise due to the opposing party getting an edge on them...

Countless observers found themselves awed by how things that had turned out. The general opinion of the newly formed Blackwatch Corporation was starting to take on a more mysterious shape...

Blackwatch engineered the world’s highest-grade life preservation laboratory in Namibia while at the same time, invited the ten financial titans of North America and Europe and successfully established a mutual association with them.

These accomplishments were the results of their sharp and precise execution.

It had to be noted that Blackwatch’s success was partially attributed to it being based in Africa, thus allowing it to leverage its advantage longer. If it was established in North America, the vast arsenal of astounding technology would sooner than later attract the attention of many predators and be feasted on.

Now, the corporation had proved its mettle by effortlessly subduing the Blanc family and even the Als Mutual Aid Association. It would seem that many financial groups who were gearing up to make a move would have to think twice now.

On the other side of things, Chen Chen had returned to Namibia after installing the God chip in Paul. By applying an effective and straightforward technique, he was able to address the root of the problem without having to enlist the help of the eleven financial titans.

Of course, it was not a big deal even if Paul refused to cooperate. Chen Chen could simply take him out along with the rest of the Blanc family members.

Resorting to this decision may cause an outrage among the ranks of the European and American consortium. However, being labeled as a target of public outcry was not particularly helpful for the next stages of Chen Chen’s plans.

After that, at Chen Chen’s request, Sloane was sent to the Blacklight Biotechnology Research Center.

The first reason for this decision was in part due to the technological advantage at the research center. The second reason being that Sloane essentially played the role of a hostage as seen in ancient wartimes, with the chip installed in Paul to boot, he served as double insurance.

From here on, the research center would cultivate a clone for Sloane. After a year when the clone had matured, a new pair of kidneys could be implanted into Sloane to help his body recover anew.

Chen Chen’s removal of Sloane’s kidneys was not a wasted effort. It was about sending a message to the financial corporations of the North American continent.

The promise of curing Sloane also further persuaded Paul to submit to Chen Chen.

Either risk the complete destruction of the Blanc family or regain his youth was also ensuring the recovery of his son.

With these options provided, not many people would be willing to go the route of mutual destruction just for the sake of it.

This incident should deter whatever plans from being hatched in the North American continent for a while.

Surely, without exceeding Chen Chen’s expectations, many families and financial groups began contacting Chen Chen via the eleven disciples. Some of them came directly to Namibia to contact the research center. These people had one common goal – they were all seeking the technology to restore them to their youth.

“In this case, it’s about time we publicize the ‘Age-reversal treatment’...”

Chen Chen mumbled to himself when he looked at the stream of new updates from Little X.

Back then, Chen Chen still had his qualms about publicizing this technology since he only had two cards up his sleeves at the time – The “Age-reversal treatment” and “Cloned organ transplantation”.

With the recent advent of the AD-001 that was able to effectively cure Alzheimer’s disease, he had another card up his sleeve.

With these three aces, there was no reason to not play one of them to alleviate the surrounding pressure.

Chen Chen only had to make sure to clarify things properly with the eleven disciple families.

After all, these families had each paid a billion-dollar for entry. What was the point of their major investment if Chen Chen was simply going to publicize all these technologies?

If everyone could receive the same treatment, what were they paying so much for?

What Chen Chen needed to do now was to get across the point that there was a good reason for their billion-dollar entry fee.

Of course, even if it had to be publicized, it must be publicized in the form of Chen Chen’s announcement. With the first phase of the Eco Science City construction nearing completion, the announcement of the “Age-reversal treatment” along with the official opening of the Eco Science City was sure to attract an endless stream of clients.

This was the advantage of having someone to back him. Compared to his previous situation where he did not have any supporting pillars, an announcement of this scale was a suicidal move.

With the help of the eleven financial groups acting as his mediators, Chen Chen only had to pay a small price to ensure a constant stream of revenue generated from the Eco Science City.

It was the same principle of a child with a pocket full of cash. If Chen Chen only had the technology without the capital to back it up, it would be an invitation for others to snatch it away from him. If he was on equal footing with any interested parties, the other party would naturally treat him as an equal and attempt to obtain his technology by way of trade.

This was the answer Chen Chen had for these financial groups: In half a year, after the Eco Science City opened, everyone could come and enjoy the prospects of this technology.

As a result, the major financial groups did their best to contain their greed and patiently waited for the grand opening of the Eco Science City.

Over the next few months, Chen Chen wholly rented the Symphony of the Seas anchored by the Walvis Bay. After a round of modification, Chen Chen had transformed the main power distribution room on the main deck into a tightly guarded secret room and promptly moved in there himself.

To keep up his disguise, he did not fire any of the crewmates. Instead, he had the Symphony of the Seas leave the harbor and camp in certain sections of the open seas. The cruise would stay there and only return upon nearly exhausting the diesel supply.

Again, the Symphony of the Seas would restock itself with sufficient diesel and various other resources at the port before setting sail again to the high seas.

Just like that, four months had passed and given way to the new year.

On this day, the cruise received a message from Hollywood using satellite signals. The three movies Chen Chen had invested in previously were all completed.

Subsequently, Chen Chen received the original unedited versions of the three movies.

The three movies were Immortal, Cancer Man, and Chronicle 2: Darkness Rises.

Sitting in the cabin, Chen Chen began watching the three movies that had not officially premiered just yet. For the first time, he began looking forward to what was coming next.

chapters.title: Chapter 155: Pick One Out Of Three
chapters.body:
Chapter 155: Pick One Out Of Three

Chapter 155: Pick One Out of Three

Immortal.

Cancer Man.

Chronicle 2: Darkness Rises.

Chen Chen did not try to insert the three originals sent from Hollywood right away. He issued an order to the captain to take the Symphony of the Seas back to port.

It took four hours for the Symphony of the Seas to return to Walvis Bay from the open seas, enough time for Chen Chen to quickly finish the three movies.
