Chen Chen took an NZT-48 before watching the first movie, Cancer Man. He quickly finished viewing the movie at a fast forward speed.

The movie was filmed from the perspective of a mad scientist. The genius scientist had developed a targeted drug known as “CAN-05” that could cure all forms of cancer. This led to the scientist eventually losing a basic sense of respect for life and beginning to lose his sense of self.

After that, with his astounding brilliance and talent, he managed to develop “CAN-09”, a drug that was able to transform all human body cells into controllable cancer cells. Once again, he began conducting subsequent clinical trials.

However, the clinical trials proved unsuccessful. He was also arrested by the police for breaking international regulations.

Before being captured, he injected CAN-09 in himself as a final act of desperation...

After an hour had passed, Chen Chen nodded contently at the ending credits displayed on the screen.

Excellent, a captivating opening, sensible plot development, all capped off with the ending of the protagonist taking a self-destructive approach. The movie perfectly encapsulated the nature and moral choices of mankind.

It could be said that this was a standard, streamlined commercial blockbuster.

Especially in the ending of the movie where the mad scientist underwent an endless loop of division and proliferation, ultimately turning into an indescribable huge lump of sarcoma. To the average audience, it would be an extremely shocking scene.

The director seemed to be using the movie as a cautionary tale to warn some scientists to not challenge the nature of mankind, lest they end up suffering from the consequences of their choices as seen in the movie.

This movie was going to sell like hotcakes!

Before even premiering, Chen Chen projected the movie’s global box office to reach at least 500 million U.S. dollars.

What was more important was that all aspects of the movie, whether it was the plot logic or scientific principles, matched the conventions of reality. Similar to the previous movie Deadly Crisis: A Shocking Scheme which Chen Chen had invested in, it was extremely likely to be able to be contained in the USB drive.

Chen Chen started the second movie on the computer – Immortal.

Compared to the previous movie, this one was a lot tamer since it was a literary movie. Still, Chen Chen patiently finished it at double speed.

Alright, still not much issue to be seen...

After that, Chen Chen turned to the final movie.

Since there was an appropriate amount of funding, Chronicle 2 did not adopt a mockumentary style but instead used a lot of long-distance filmography methods to convey a more realistic effect.

The plot was as Chen Chen described, set in ten years after the first movie. Cousin Matt traveled the world before ultimately returning to the small town he grew up in. He ended up discovering that the government had transformed the cave into an underground base.

Inside the underground base, the alien plantations inside the crystalline had long since perished and no longer produced new radiation. The giant sea urchin crystal was dissected into thousands of pieces by the government.

Each one of the several thousand pieces of crystals contained a portion of the power of the alien plantation. This was used to produce several hundreds of superpower users.

The powers of these users were nearly identical to that of the protagonist where they were able to move objects using telekinesis.

However, since they had only absorbed measly few pieces of crystals, the strength and potential of the power were nowhere close to that of the three protagonists in the first movie. The most they could do was manipulate small objects like coins or insects.

Cousin Matt also found out that Andrew had apparently survived. Only he was left in a vegetative state for the government to conduct secret research.

With Matt’s arrival, the telepathy connection between the two was triggered. Andrew finally awoke from his decade long slumber...

The movie ended with a battle between the two characters again. Compared to the previous battle, their powers had seen tremendous growth to a point where they were almost a mini Superman on their own.

A decade ago, Andrew could only stop a regular bullet. After a decade had passed, the two of them could effortlessly stop a tomahawk missile.

In the end, Matt finally got through to Andrew with a message of affection. The two of them decided to leave the country for good to start a brand new life.

The conclusion could be described as a very heartwarming ending.

The two protagonists of the movie were the two original cast of the first movie. Having the movie based in a decade after the first also explained the maturity of the two protagonists, since a decade had passed in canonical time as well.

After finishing the three movies, Chen Chen shut his eyes and made a mental review of the movies.

Upon ensuring there were no issues, Chen Chen went to the bathroom to wash his hands. He then returned to the computer and inserted the USB drive.

Over the last four months, the USB drive practically never left the charging port of the cruise ship. Taking the power generation rate of the five Leroy-somer LSA62 generators multiplied with an 85% power factor, the final tabulation was 100,000(KW.h) x 24(h) x 120 days x 0.85(cosΦ).

This meant that over the last four months, the USB drive had been charged with approximately 250 million kilowatt-hours of electricity.

Based on Chen Chen’s previous calculations, extracting an object of one gram from the USB drive required 1,224 kW.h of electricity. Which meant that at this moment, the USB drive had enough electricity to extract objects weighing up to 200 kilograms.

This amount of power still proved to be quite lacking.

Chen Chen shook his head. Previously at Dlamini’s side, he was able to obtain 120 million kilowatts of electricity in five days. Yet, he only managed to yield double that amount of power with more than twenty times expended for the previous period.

Chen Chen exhausted nearly a combined 200 million U.S. dollars for the rental cost, crew salary, and fuel over the last four months for the sake of powering the USB drive. After conversion, the cost per kilowatt-hour is equivalent to more than 0.8 US dollars.

Uhmmm...

Constructing the nuclear power plant remained a more reliable approach. Powering the USB drive using the cruise ship alone was going to burn up all his money.

Chen Chen silently pondered on the matter.

The good news was that this was the largest amount of power Chen Chen had in the USB drive so far. Chen Chen felt like one of those housewives ready to go on a spending spree on Taobao during the 11.11 promotion season as he was filled with excitement.

Chen Chen took a deep breath before silently turning on the laptop and tapping on the USB drive logo.

There were a total of eight movies within the USB drive.

Apart from the four horror films that came with the USB drive, the other three films were inserted as an experiment back when he had just acquired the USB drive.

Apart from that, the last one was the first Chronicle movie he inserted before extracting the GS Combat Suit.

The eight movies sat idly in the USB drive, remaining the same as they had ever been.

Chen Chen paid no mind to the eight movies. He selected the [Cancer Man.mp4] folder and familiarly tapped [ctrl + c] before switching tabs to the USB drive window and clicking [ctrl + v].

“Ding!”

The long-awaited notification alert. Chen Chen raised an eyebrow.

Cancer Man. Failure.

Chen Chen, who was under the effect of the drug, did not feel too dejected. He promptly held the [ctrl] button, highlighted the two remaining films, and pasted them in the USB drive together.

“Ding!”

Once again, there was a system error alert, but there was a shine in Chen Chen’s eyes.

He noticed that there was only one error window from the system error!

Chen Chen fixed his eyes and saw that it was Immortal that prompted the system error output. Chronicle 2, on the other hand, went through...

chapters.title: Chapter 156: Come Out, Crystalline!
chapters.body:
Chapter 156: Come Out, Crystalline!

Chapter 156: Come Out, Crystalline!

Chronicle 2: Darkness Rises, approved by the USB drive!

A shadow of a smirk appeared in the corner of Chen Chen’s lips.

Even though two out of the three movies were rejected, just having one of them go through was enough for Chen Chen to consider the investment a worthwhile one.

Conversely, it was an extremely lucrative business venture!

Chen Chen quickly checked the USB drive window but could not find the newly inserted movie.

Slightly taken aback, Chen Chen thought for a moment before placing the cursor on the Chronicle folder.

The computer quickly displayed the size of the folder.

.4G.

As far as Chen Chen could remember, the movie he had downloaded previously was 2.3G. Chronicle 2 was 13.1G, which meant that the two movies combined on their own inside the USB drive.

There was a function like this?

Chen Chen felt slightly at a loss for words.

Chen Chen remembered that back then when he downloaded the Limitless TV series, there was no such occurrence. Otherwise, he would have long been aware of it instead of just finding out now.

Leaving this topic aside, what was more worth addressing was why were Cancer Man and Immortal rejected?

Even though Cancer Man was set in a parallel universe, other movies he had successfully extracted items from such as Men In Black and Limitless were set in a parallel universe as well. Thus, concluding that this was not the deciding factor that caused the USB drive to reject it.

The other movie Immortal was set three hundred years into the future of the present time. Since it worked with Deadly Crisis: A Shocking Scheme, the first movie he had filmed, there was no reason that it should not work for Immortal.

What was the exact cause of their failure?

Countless speculations and suspicions flowed through Chen Chen’s mind. The only way to obtain the answer was to perform more experiments.

Chen Chen took a deep breath. The Symphony of the Seas had docked and arrived at Walvis Bay.

Chen Chen paused his experiment temporarily. He took the computer and quickly got down from the cruise ship and approached his designated vehicle.

“Boss?”

Qian Wenhuan emerged from the passenger seat and quickly held the door open for Chen Chen.

“Is the quarantine room of the underground laboratory ready?” Chen Chen asked the moment he entered the car.

“Yes. It’s ready for use at any time.”

Qian Wenhuan nodded. “Boss, are we heading back to the research center first or...”

“To the underground research lab.”

Chen Chen tapped on the headrest.

Cheng Cao stepped on the pedal upon receiving the instruction and the vehicle drove into the distance quickly.

Half an hour later.

The Wrangler entered the construction site of the Eco Science City. After four months of construction, the project was nearing its final stages. Safety nets were being removed from the high-rise buildings and the low-rise buildings had been completed some time ago and were now in the renovation stages.

The Eco Science City would be open to the public within two to three months.

The scenery outside the window reminded Chen Chen of being back in the Mainland. He began feeling a little nostalgic.

Even he of all people would never have expected to build a modern civilization city in the poor and barren land of Africa.

Barring any accidents, this place would eventually become his main camp. Perhaps even becoming the technological center of the Federation in the distant future...

The Wrangler coasted across the streets of the Eco Science City, quickly arriving at the entrance of the Sun District in Eco Science City.

The district was still being isolated by the staff of the B.S.S. After passing through, Cheng Chao, who was no stranger to the area, drove into the underground garage and entered the huge cavern leading to the underground base.

The foreman, Hai Sanchuan, from the last meeting came to greet them. After exchanging some small talk with Chen Chen, he led them into the underground section.

Unlike the last time they were here, the underground base was well illuminated by bright lights. Apart from that, the surrounding boulder riddled walls were covered with concrete and steel beams and the underground hole was transformed into a large square formation.

The pyramid-shaped laboratory made of tungsten-gold stood majestically in the center of the cavern like a magnificent fortress.

“Boss, the laboratory foundation of the Spire is completed. We are now working on the living quarters and military areas which are built in accordance with the specifications of military refuge bases.”

Qian Wenhuan explained in a low voice, “Even if a global nuclear war were to break out in the future, all we need to do is relocate our families here. Should be able to live a self-sufficient life for several decades.”

Spire was the name of the laboratory.

The top section of the laboratory was a platform built to support the structure of the building and the roof of the cave. It was somewhat similar to the Mayan pyramid architecture.

Chen Chen shook his head upon hearing Qian Wenhuan’s remark.

There was no telling what was going on in that head of his, was it so difficult to just think of something optimistic for once? Why must it be either nuclear warfare or a Resident Evil scenario?

The group entered from the main entrance into the laboratory of the Spire.

Upon entering the laboratory, the first thing that came into view was a gigantic front hall. Since operations had yet to officially commence, it was still in an empty and barren state.

After that, Hai Sanchuan took them to a metal gate in the laboratory section and began explaining:

“Following President Qian’s instructions, I’ve set up a quarantine zone built entirely of lead plates in the Spire laboratory. On top of that, I also installed a layer of ZF6 grade heavy lead glass protective window in the front section of the quarantine zone.”

Sensing the arrival of the crowd, the door of the laboratory automatically opened. This was done to ease the entry and exit of the staff. When the Spire laboratory commenced operation, the setting would be reverted.

Chen Chen took the lead and stepped into the laboratory. He immediately noticed the giant quarantine zone in front of the laboratory.

According to Hai Sanchuan, the quarantine zone was constructed of lead plates. The viewing glass should be the ZF6 grade heavy lead glass protective window.

This kind of glass was commonly used for the observation windows in a nuclear power plant reactor.

The entire laboratory gave an impression of the X-ray operating room seen in hospitals.

Chen Chen also noticed two sets of fully enclosed radiation protection suits sitting in the corner. This was also ordered by Qian Wenhuan as per Chen Chen’s instructions.

All facilities were readily available in the laboratory including safety measures, surveillance systems, fire safety facilities, and various other implementations.

The laboratory of the most heralded standards had cost Chen Chen an extraordinary amount of funds. Still, it would appear that the investment was well worth it.

“Cao, Wenhuan, you guys head back to the research center and bring me several healthy mice.”

After surveying the scene, Chen Chen turned to the two of them. “Make it fast.”

“Understand.” The silent Cheng Cao nodded and headed out without a word.

Qian Wenhuan first did a double-take before bringing Hai Sanchuan to leave the laboratory with him.

“Eh, what is it? I can stay here and chat with Boss Chen.”

Hai Sanchuan quickly mentioned.

“It’s alright Boss Hai, why don’t you head out with Wenhuan in case he gets lost.”

Chen Chen smiled.

Hai Sanchuan was then dragged away by Qian Wenhuan in confusion.

After waiting for everyone to leave the laboratory, Chen Chen turned to the camera. “Little X, can you hear me?”

“Yes!”

Little X’s tender voice came out from the speakers. “Ever since being connected yesterday, I already managed to gain full control over the Spire laboratory. All of the operations of the laboratory are handled by me now!”

“Good job. Lock the door of the laboratory for now until Qian Wenhuan and Cheng Cao get back.”

“Got it!”

Following Chen Chen’s orders, the electric lock indicator beside the gate turned from green to red. “The radiation laboratory is locked down.”

“Excellent.”

Chen Chen remarked before he started to put on the fully enclosed radiation protection suit.

After putting on the fully enclosed radiation protection suit, Chen Chen turned on the laptop he had brought along and entered the innermost quarantine zone of the laboratory. He put on the Chronicle movie in the quarantine zone.

The movie immediately started playing from the first part. According to the runtime label, the movie length was 218 minutes.

There was no mistaking it. The two movies had merged automatically.

Based on this observation, Chen Chen received confirmation of his speculation. Without any hesitation, he moved the progress bar to the segment of the second movie until a piece of cut crystal the size of a fist appeared on the screen. He then tapped the spacebar button to stop the movie.

Chen Chen then extended his hands wrapped in the radiation protection suit and reached into the screen.

Then, he gently pulled it out.

“Dong!’

With an instantly recognizable voice, a crystal shimmering with blue light with a surface like that of a diamond...

Appeared in Chen Chen’s hand!

chapters.title: Chapter 157: Experiment
chapters.body:
Chapter 157: Experiment

Chapter 157: Experiment

Chen Chen gently lifted the blue glowing crystal in his hand. The volume of this crystal was about 0.001 cubic meters, which was 1000 cubic centimeters, but the weight was in the vicinity of 4.5 kilograms.

Something was off...

Chen Chen frowned. There was no way this was the relative density of a crystal. The main component of a crystal was silicon dioxide which chemical formula is SiO2, its mass was about 2.66g/cm3.

Following that logic, if the main component of this piece of crystalline in his hand was silicon dioxide, it should not weigh above 2.7 kilograms.

According to the movie setting, the crystalline was a protective layer of shell formed by a reaction caused by the alien plantation secretion reacting with underground soil. It should not be composed of foreign matter.

It should only contain the residual biological energy field of the alien plantation at most. Energy fields had no weight behind them.

In other words, the matter composition of this crystal was closer to that of a spinel.

Just like a crystal, the spinel was a variation of a gemstone. It was superior to the crystal whether in terms of Mohs hardness, quality, or even price. The chemical formula of spinel was MgAl2O4. It weighed 4.5g/cm3, with Mohs hardness reaching up to grade 8.

Things were getting interesting...

Chen Chen continued dissecting the subject.

After all, he had personally read the setting written by the scriptwriter. The crystalline was simply referred to as a crystal in the movie setting. There was no mention of its base component, which made sense since the screenwriter was not a chemist. As far as Chen Chen and the rest of the audience was concerned, it was a crystal, which was silicon dioxide.

As it turned out, upon extracting the crystalline, he had discovered that the crystalline was not silicon dioxide but aluminum-magnesium tetroxide instead.

In that case, did that make the universe to which the USB drive connected to reality or an abstract universe?

If it was an abstract universe, how was it able to overturn the crystalline component logic as portrayed in the script and yield an unexpected result instead?

What was the explanation behind its appearance if it was reality?

Chen Chen looked down at the crystal in his palm. Was it the screenwriter Max Landis who had devised an instance of a real universe in the USB drive or did the universe come before Max Landis and in turn inspired Max Landis’ work?

Also, how was it that the sequel which he had instigated managed to synchronize itself accordingly with the built universe?

“It would be great if there was a way to explore the opposite universe...”

An idea suddenly struck Chen Chen’s mind like a zip of lightning. It was an unprecedented revolutionary idea. Chen Chen’s pupils shrunk faintly as he seemed to have thought of a possibility.

Could that be the truth?

No, it was impossible!

In that case, what was the explanation behind him being able to constantly instantiate the same item from a movie?

Hundreds of billions of neurons in Chen Chen’s mind began operating at maximum efficiency. Countless thoughts and ideas bounced off of each other in Chen Chen’s mind. In that very instant, numerous possibilities raced through Chen Chen’s mind.

Phew!

Chen Chen shook his head and buried these ideas in the depths of his mind. It was not yet the moment to verify his hypothesis as it would have to wait until the completion of the nuclear power plant.

Chen Chen picked up a radiation detector and tested the crystalline with it. He discovered that the radiation field of the crystalline was very small.

It would appear that just like in the movie, the force field of the crystal would not be triggered unless it sensed the surrounding biological force field.

This was the exact reason Chen Chen wore the nuclear radiation quarantine suit.

At this point, Chen Chen had already long confirmed that the attributes of items extracted from a movie were the same as its variation inside the movie. Any item that was harmless inside a movie was harmless in real life as well. The same logic applied to drugs like NZT-48 and AD-001.

Still, it was better to be safe than to be sorry, especially since Chen Chen was going to directly use it on himself. He decided to conduct an animal experiment.

There was no need for a complex experiment. Chen Chen only had to make sure that the irradiation process did not harm the body in any way before conducting the irradiation on himself without cause for worry.

Qian Wenhuan and Cheng Cao quickly returned to the Spire laboratory.

Upon being notified by Little X, Chen Chen placed the crystalline in the quarantine zone before exiting and unlocking the exterior door for the two men.

“Uh, Boss, you...”

Qian Wenhuan and Cheng Cao were slightly startled when the sliding metal door revealed Chen Chen who was tightly wrapped in a heavy suit of protective gear, standing at the opposite side.

“Where are the mice?”

Chen Chen asked.

“Oh, yes. It’s here!”

Qian Wenhuan quickly took out two mouse cages from behind him.

Each of the cages contained four white mice with a bed of pure white fur and stark red eyes.

Chen Chen nodded as he took them with one hand while he further instructed, “If there’s nothing else going on, Cao can take a look around the area. It’s going to be some time before I come back out so Wenhuan can head back first.”

“Okay.” After receiving Chen Chen’s instructions, Qian Wenhuan took his leave hesitantly.

After all this time, he was starting to figure out Chen Chen’s style. Chen Chen was a standard efficiency-oriented leader, there was no need for any flattery or curry favoring with Chen Chen. All he needed to do was do his part and complete whatever further instructions given to him.

After sealing up the laboratory again, Chen Chen first placed one of the cages on a bench outside the quarantine zone. He then took the other cage into the quarantine zone with him.

Upon stepping into the quarantine zone, Chen Chen suddenly heard a low but piercing hum. This noise originated from the crystalline with its faint blue glow.

Did it sense the surrounding biological force field?

Chen Chen glanced at the cage he held in his hand. He decided to just open the lid of the cage and place the fist-sized crystalline inside the cage right away!

One of the mice seemed to be taken by curiosity. It stared at the clear, transparent crystal before crawling up to it without any reservation and gave it a sniff.

Upon the mice making the first contact with the crystal, the humming of the crystal suddenly began to grow louder. Its blue body was slowly morphing into a red tint as well!

Chen Chen was fascinated. He quickly picked up the radiation detector to his side to check its reading. The radiation at this moment had spiked to up to 30 millisieverts.

It was rather clear that the radiation inside the crystal was completely released following the mice’s touch!

“Is there something decaying in the crystalline?”

Chen Chen considered silently. He was going to have to run a proper test after this experiment concluded. He needed to see if this crystalline was truly a spinel and if some substance within it triggered a decaying process upon contact.

The substance in the crystal decayed rapidly. Within a little over ten seconds, the glowing red light it had emitted slowly faded out and soon vanished without a trace.

The crystal regained its original blue luster as well.

The mice seemed to be affected by the crystalline activity. When the crystalline was releasing its radiation, the four mice became visibly irritated and were running around inside the cage. Two of the mice had fresh blood spilling out of their eyes and the tip of their noses.

All of the mice regained their composure after the crystalline had completely released its radiation.

chapters.title: Chapter 158: Accepting Irradiation
chapters.body:
Chapter 158: Accepting Irradiation

Chapter 158: Accepting Irradiation

Chen Chen waited for a moment after the crystalline regained its blue luster.

After ten minutes, Chen Chen tested with the radiation detector again and noticed that the radiation level had reverted to normal levels.

Upon noting this, Chen Chen took the cage out of the quarantine zone. He collected blood samples from the four mice and also chipped off a small piece of the crystalline and had it sealed.

Chen Chen then took out a marker to label the irradiated mice. They were labeled A1, A2, A3, and A4 respectively. The cage was labeled as Cage A.

The other cage was labeled as Cage B afterward. Three mice were taken out of Cage B and settled into Cage A. The final mouse in Cage B was taken into the quarantine zone.

Chen Chen repeated the same set of procedures as before, extracting the same piece of crystalline from the computer and placing it into the cage.

The crystalline released its radiation again and the lone mouse was thus irradiated.

Fortunately, the lone mouse which was exposed to the radiation all by itself did not display any signs of vital instability.

Following this, Chen Chen left the room again and moved the healthy mice previously moved to the other cage back to its original cage. He also labeled the lone mouse which had been irradiated as B1 and collected blood samples.

Chen Chen left the laboratory with the collected blood samples and the remnant of the crystalline.

The devices from Switzerland were not imported into the underground base yet so the blood tests would still have to be conducted in the research center for now.

Chen Chen took off the protective suit and returned to the research center with the blood samples and both the A and B cages in tow.

Chen Chen was worried about the off chance that these irradiated mice would somehow manage to escape while he was gone. Despite the small chance of it happening, it was better to be cautious since they were mice exposed to the crystalline radiation.

When Chen Chen returned to the research center, he passed the blood samples to the relevant department to have them produce a report on the complete blood count and microRNA of the mice soonest possible.

Next, Chen Chen found a vacant small laboratory where he locked the door and began to observe the state of the mice in the cages.

The first thing he did was place the mice in the two cages into two larger steel cages. Next, he placed several melon seeds in both cages A and B to get them to fight for it.

Throughout the entire sequence, there were no signs that may resemble the usage of superpowers.

Chen Chen thought about it and decided to get two scoops of melon seeds and place them outside both cages A and B respectively. This way, the mice could see them but could not access them.

As expected, the group of mice was starting to get anxious. Two of them constantly tried to poke their heads through the gap of the steel cage. However, due to the compact netting of the steel cage, they only managed to stick out the tips of their noses.

Chen Chen observed silently with an experiment recorder set up to film the experiment.

After two to three hours, the pile of melon seeds remained outside the cage completely untouched.

At the same time, the results for the complete blood count were out. Chen Chen looked at the blood count reading and noticed that the irradiated mice seemed to display signs of leukopenia, albeit not very severe.

Of course, these were just the initial symptoms.

Even for regular people exposed to radiation, the potentially harmful effects may only surface after several weeks or months.

It was the same for mice. Experiments had been performed by testing two groups of mice, one exposed to a lethal amount of radiation and the other a non-lethal amount. It took three to four weeks before any vivid observation could be noted.

This meant that the microRNA tests should be essential.

A microRNA was a small non-coding RNA molecule involved in post-transcriptional gene expression regulation found in plants and animals. There were speculations that RNA regulated one-third of human gene expression.

After yet another hour, the microRNA results were released. From group A, 13% to 15% of microRNA in all four mice were exposed to radiation changes. This reading increased to 19% for group B.

Chen Chen allowed himself a sigh of relief upon noting these results, all his worries were gone.

Due to time concerns, there was no way to investigate the later effects for now. Still, he was at least able to confirm that this radiation did not cause any life-threatening issue.

There was no longer any need for concern at this point.

Upon returning to the cages, Chen Chen raised his eyebrows in wonder when he saw what was on the table.

While he was looking at the laboratory report, the irradiated mouse in Cage B was gnawing away at a large melon seed.

The pile of melon seeds outside the cage seemed to have been moved as well. It was as if an invisible hand had nudged them toward the direction of the cage.

The mouse labeled B1 had undoubtedly begun practicing the use of its superpowers.

Upon noting this fact, Chen Chen waited for it to finish the seed before capturing it from the cage. With his right hand holding its head, he pulled its tail with his left hand!

“Crack!”

The little mouse which had just learned to use its superpower and had a bright future ahead of it left the world without so much as a whimper.

After that, Chen Chen executed all of the mice in the two cages one by one. He found a bottle of concentrated sulfuric acid and poured it into a large beaker, and then threw the carcasses of all the mice in it.

It did occur to Chen Chen to keep these mice alive for long term observation, but he ultimately gave up on the idea.

After all, the items instantiated from the USB drive were still more reliable. The same logic applied from the earliest NZT-48 to the immunity shot, even to the more recent AD-001. Keeping this group of mice alive may cause him problems in the future.

Next, Chen Chen returned to the underground laboratory. This time, he did not put on a protective suit. Instead, he entered the quarantine zone and turned on the computer to make a copy of Chronicle 2: Darkness Rises and inserted it into the USB drive.

Chen Chen, who was well familiar with the routine, straight away dragged the movie to the part with the cut crystal.

Chen Chen took a deep breath before reaching into the computer screen...

When he pulled his hand back out, the crystalline with its faint blue glow appeared in his palm.

The moment it came out, the crystalline sensed the biological force field and immediately emitted a piercing screech. The blue glow was slowly replaced with a red tint!

It was releasing radiation.

Chen Chen remained holding the crystalline this way. He felt the crystal slowly heating up before subsequently cooling down.

Five minutes later, the crystalline had reverted to its original form.

That was it?

Chen Chen put down the crystalline and tried to feel some changes in his body to no avail.

Next, he turned to the marker pen beside him and began to imagine the pen being lifted by an invisible hand. No matter how hard he tried to visualize it, nothing came out of it.

Was it because the dosage was too small?

Chen Chen shook his head. There was a scene in the movie that mentioned that a lot of users who had been exposed to the crystalline radiation hoped to get more crystalline. This meant that the radiation effect was additive.

Arriving at this thought, Chen Chen instantiated yet another crystalline and absorbed it as he did previously.

This time, Chen Chen felt something in his head that he could not put into words...

Chen Chen frowned. There was a hot sensation in his nose which made him rub his nose instinctively.

A small puddle of blood appeared before him.

Was this an indication of the beginning stages of acquiring the superpower?

Chen Chen wondered silently.

chapters.title: Chapter 159: Nine Pieces
chapters.body:
Chapter 159: Nine Pieces

Chapter 159: Nine Pieces

Half an hour later, there were nine crystalline fragments by Chen Chen’s feet.

A mouse weighed around 100 grams. Chen Chen weighed 70,000 grams, which was seven hundred times the weight of a mouse.

Judging from the previous radiation experiment, a mouse was able to withstand the full radiation exposure of a single crystalline fragment. This implied that Chen Chen should be able to handle the combined radiation exposure of 700 crystalline fragments without much issue.

In that case, it should not be a rash decision to absorb nine crystalline pieces at once.

Just like the observation as seen in the movie when exposed to crystalline radiation, Chen Chen’s nose began to bleed. A large spot on his shirt was dyed red by the blood.

Chen Chen vaguely estimated, he had lost approximately 100 millimeters of blood from the radiation exposure, which was equivalent to the amount of half a bag of blood donation.

The bleeding from Chen Chen’s nose quickly halted as he stopped the irradiation process. He shut the laptop, packed up the USB drive along with the crystalline fragment that was exhausted of its energy, and started thinking.

The research report of the crystalline fragments was released as well. As expected, they were aluminum-magnesium tetroxide, which was the chemical formula of spinel.

Based on the market price on the Earth Federation, a spinel the size of a fist sculpted into an artful sculpture should be worth hundreds of millions of U.S. dollars.

However, it would appear that Chen Chen had the slightest interest in auctioning off these spinels. He had Cheng Cao crush the spinel into powdered pieces, mix them into the trash along with other glass shards and whatnot, and had them disposed of.

After getting rid of the trash, Chen Chen finally sat back in the laboratory and began experimenting with his newly acquired powers.

To start things off, he sat in front of the test table with a recorder set up in front of him and started recording.

After that, Chen Chen stared at a black marker pen placed on the table. As he invoked a thought, he had the sense that there was a string latched on to the object in front of him.

Chen Chen tried to lift it gently and the marker pen proceeded to be lifted!

From Chen Chen’s point of view, the marker pen hung in mid-air and spun in circles repeatedly. It was like it was suspended in the air by some string.

Stop!

Chen Chen issued an order in his mind to get the marker pen to stop spinning. As expected, the spinning marker pen suddenly paused as if the air surrounding it had frozen in place. It was suspended in the air, unmoving.

Upon witnessing this scene, an idea suddenly occurred to Chen Chen. He tried closing his eyes.

“Click!”

A noise immediately followed after, it was the marker pen clapping against the surface of the table.

“Influenced by the line of sight? No, probably not...”

Chen Chen kept his eyes shut. He turned his head and scanned the table in his mind.

Surely, thin strands began to emerge in his vision. With his eyes still closed, he was able to sense the smooth and cold surface of the table as well as a long strip of object laid on the table.

Following Chen Chen’s will, the marker pen that fell on the table slowly dangled in the air again.

“I knew it, this ability doesn’t have anything to do with the user’s vision. The visual aspect only serves as an aid to help better visualize the ability’s effects. Upon losing sight of the targeted object, the ability’s manipulation effect on the object is subconsciously cut off as well.

“It should be possible to overcome this behavior with more practice. At the very least, this proves that the ability doesn’t stem from the user’s sight, but something else deep inside the brain...”

Chen Chen mumbled to himself in a low voice with his eyes still closed shut.

The experiment was still going on.

Chen Chen allowed himself to faintly feel the floating marker pen. He felt the marker pen’s build, temperature, and shape. Unlike touching it with his hands, these sensations were transmitted into Chen Chen’s mind in another form.

It was a truly wondrous feeling. Unlike the sense of sight, hearing, smell or touch, this was a brand new sensation. Chen Chen had never felt something like this in his twenty-plus years of living.

When he tried to apply some force, he heard a small clacking sound coming from the marker pen in front of him. The body of the acrylic pen became slightly deformed.

“This is the power at work. Is it able to transmute some form of energy in the mind into direct kinetic energy used to manipulate objects?”

Needless to say, these were all mere speculations on Chen Chen’s side. Want to find out what sort of energy this was and the workings behind transmuting mere thoughts to kinetic energy? It was going to require an intensive amount of experimentation.

Next up, Chen Chen put down the floating marker pen. He began to reach forward with his conscience with his eyes still closed. In his mind’s eye, he felt a large number of strands extending forward from his mind, they began reaching in front of him...

However, these strands were unable to extend any further after reaching out close to a meter.

“Looks like the ability is only able to extend about one meter outward. The range of control appears to decrease as the distance increases.”

Chen Chen made a brief analysis. After that, he tried to replicate the same effect in his mind except this time, he was extending them outward across all directions.

This time, the strands reached their limit at about half a meter out.

The range could extend up to a meter if probed in one direction. By equally distributing them across all directions, their area of effect was limited to just half a meter.

Though it was not a particularly impressive number, Chen Chen was not dejected. After all, these powers could be further improved with more practice. Chen Chen could also endlessly extract crystalline to build his power capacity.

“If this ability isn’t limited by sight, couldn’t it essentially replace my sense of sight as another form of sensory device?”

Chen Chen considered silently. He slowly lowered his head in a manner like he was staring at the table, the only difference being that his eyes closed shut...

In Chen Chen’s perception, he was gradually zooming into the test bench. What was once the smooth surface of the epoxy tabletop zoomed in until it turned into a rough and bumpy-textured surface.

Zooming to this extent was not easily achievable, he had to maintain a constant stream of focus.

“No, I’m only able to zoom slightly above a hundred times at most. I still can’t see the bacteria, let alone the molecular structure...”

After a while, an involuntary frown appeared on Chen Chen’s face.

Even though he was able to zoom a hundred times into a selected object using the ability, he was unable to zoom into the microorganism scope. He was still largely unsure if this was due to his current power concentration or influenced by other factors.

Still, it did make sense since the size of bacteria were generally between 1 to 5 microns. Humans would need to magnify their vision three hundred times just to see the outline of bacteria. For further observation, magnification of above a thousand times was required.

Sensing the molecular structure of a substance was on a completely separate plane since a magnification of a whooping two million times was needed.

Chen Chen wondered, was there any chance of amplifying the ability on a microscopic scale in addition to an enhancement on the macroscopic scale?

The way Chen Chen saw it, if the subsequent upgrade of the ability in the movie was interpreted as an upgrade in terms of quantity, then enhancing on a micro-scale should be considered an upgrade in quality.

As Chen Chen was immersed in deep thought, he suddenly felt a sharp sting at the tip of his nose. A gush of blood flowed out.

“Approaching my limit...”

Chen Chen calmly opened his eyes. He felt groggy and his head was spinning. He proceeded to go to a sink and splashed cold water on his forehead and the bridge of his nose.

The water used in the laboratory was filtered underground water with a temperature below ten degrees. The moment Chen Chen splashed his face with the water, he felt a chill emanating across his body, jolting him to his senses.

The bleeding from his nose finally stopped after some more time had passed.

Deciding to stop the experiment, Chen Chen got back up and packed up the USB drive. He then turned around to leave the laboratory and had Cheng Cao take him back to the Symphony of the Seas.

As Chen Chen took his leave, the electronic lock indicating green turned red as the laboratory operated by Little X locked itself down...

chapters.title: Chapter 160: Initial Superpower Speculations
chapters.body:
Chapter 160: Initial Superpower Speculations

Chapter 160: Initial Superpower Speculations

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Two days later, Chen Chen returned to the underground laboratory with a vast amount of experiment devices.

The first thing Chen Chen did upon returning to the laboratory was removing the SD card from the experiment recorder and inserting it into the computer. He proceeded to review the footage from two days ago.

In the footage, Chen Chen was sitting in front of the camera with his eyes closed. Positioned in front of him was a black marker pen spinning in the air.

“Influenced by the line of sight? No, probably not...

“This is the power at work. Is it able to transmute some form of energy in the mind into direct kinetic energy used to manipulate objects?

“Looks like the ability is only able to extend about one meter outward. The range of control appears to decrease as the distance increases.

“Nearing my limit...”

As the video began to show Chen Chen having nosebleeds, the footage ended.

Two days ago, while Cheng Cao was taking Chen Chen back to the cruise ship, he felt like he could not take it anymore. As he sat in the moving car, he could feel his mind fluttering about absently. It was like the feeling you get when you start another game in the morning after cramming out an all-nighter in the internet café.

This was the first time he had experienced such symptoms ever since taking the NZT-48. It could be seen that the effects of radiation were more than they may appear.

Upon returning to the Symphony of the Seas, Chen Chen barely mustered the strength to lock the door of the power distribution room and plug the USB drive back into the charging port before he fell into a deep slumber.

Chen Chen slept from noon until the morning of the next day.

The next morning, Chen Chen was woken up by his starving belly.

A maddening hunger took over his body and mind as if he had been starving for three to four days. He only regained his strength after devouring half a kilogram of chocolate and six steaks.

This phenomenon should be a result of some form of changes happening inside his body. If Chen Chen’s estimation was correct, the crystalline had probably configured parts of his DNA structure.

This was not the sort of transformation that would take place over the span of merely a day.

Chen Chen took several large sacks of energy bars from the research center. These energy bars were originally meant for the consumption of B.S.S. members during their tasks. The calories contained in an energy bar should last a regular adult through the day.

After reviewing the footage from the other day, Chen Chen set the recorder in front of him once again. He then took out a load cell and placed it on the table.

A load cell resembled a disc-shaped electronic scale, except it measured force such as tension and pressure instead of weight. Chen Chen planned to measure the current strength of his ability this way.

Following the commencement of the experiment, Chen Chen placed the load cell on the table and supported himself on the table with his arms crossed. He fixed his gaze intently on the load cell.

Immediately after, Chen Chen felt invisible filaments appearing in his mind, they spread out and began touching the load cell!

The electronic display on the side displayed 3.4, the unit measurement following it was mN.

Chen Chen frowned, obviously not very content with the result. He tapped the load cell by applying a smaller amount of pressure.

.5mN.

Chen Chen repeated this sequence more than a hundred times. He got as low as 0.3mN and as high as 4mN. Barring the highest and lowest reading obtained, the average was 1mN.

Chen Chen tilted his head and appeared to be in deep thought.

mN was equivalent to 0.001N. According to Newton’s second law F=ma, he should be able to simulate an acceleration of 1m/s2 in an object weighing one gram.

This was the current concentration level of the energy inside Chen Chen’s mind.

This was a pretty terrible level. To be fair, Chen Chen was not familiar with the ability yet. There should be an improvement as he began practicing.

After that, Chen Chen tried to attack the load cell with the highest amount of force he could muster. He heard a clear “dong” from the load cell as if it had just been hit by an invisible force and the electronic display showed 036N!

That was right, N. This meant that Chen Chen managed to evoke a force of almost 0.3N using his mind.

Chen Chen gave it several more shots after that and obtained the same results each time.

After completing the strength benchmark, Chen Chen took out a container the size of a loaf of bread.

Upon opening the box, it was revealed that its content was a box of standard experiment-use weights.

The international standard categorization for weights was E1, E2, F1, F2, M1, and M2. E2 was the most precise among the collection, able to pinpoint down to the milligrams.

“JF-1 non-magnetic stainless steel weight. Cylindrical. Weight grade: E1.”

Chen Chen muttered to the recorder feed as he strapped on a pair of disposable gloves. Using a pair of tweezers that came with the box, he gently lifted that smallest 1mg weight and set it on the table.

The 1mg weight was shaped like a tiny metal sheet. It was so tiny that it may be easily blown away by a gust of wind.

Chen Chen tried to lift it. He was able to suspend the 1-milligram weight in the air without expending too much effort.

In that case, Chen Chen decided he was just going to skip the milligram weight and took the one gram weight.

Chen Chen still found it relatively easy to lift the weight. He was even able to effortlessly make the weight circle around his head.

After that, Chen Chen experimented with the 5-gram and 10-gram weights. By the time he got to the 50-gram weight, his expression changed.

He could distinctly feel that a larger amount of concentration was required to control the 50-gram weight with his mind. It was nowhere close to the effort required for the 10-gram weight whether in terms of speed and agility.

With that, Chen Chen decided it was time to pause the experiment. He went to the side to grab several energy bars and chocolate and proceeded to eat them. After he had finished eating, he returned to the experiment table and tried his luck with the 100-gram weight.

At this weight, Chen Chen could barely lift it, if at all. It was like getting a baby to swing a hammer, the resulting movements were very slow and clumsy.

Chen Chen took note of all the data accumulated so far and turned his attention to the largest weight.

The largest weight was roughly the size of half a fist, the label on it displayed 500 grams.

Chen Chen went ahead and set the weight in front of him and squinted his eyes while visualizing the weight being suspended in the air.

Suddenly, the giant weight began to tremble as if something was attempting to lift it but could not muster enough strength to do so.

Amid its hectic rumbling, Chen Chen suddenly groaned. “Rise!”

In a split instant, the weight produced a “whoosh” sound and moved for a brief moment before slamming back on the table with a “bang”.

“Phew!”

Chen Chen appeared to have exhausted all the energy in him as his vision turned black as he felt yet another warm sensation in his nose. When he reached and touched his nose, blood came gushing out again.

“As suspected, overexerting the ability over a certain threshold causes nosebleed. Is this caused by the brain being overworked which ruptures the nasal capillaries?”

Chen Chen continued speculating as he went to the sink to stop the bleeding.

After stopping the bleeding for good, Chen Chen did not risk continuing the experiment anymore. If he were to continue bleeding like this regularly, he would get anemia eventually and weaken his body.

This explained why Andrew, the protagonist of the movie always appeared pale and sickly while his cousin did not display similar symptoms. Perhaps it was because he had overworked himself with training?

Chen Chen became speechless and shook his head, his mind suddenly went back to the fundamental question —

To sum it up, what was this power he had acquired supposed to be?

Amplified mental prowess? Telekinesis? Or was it something else?

The purpose of the last few experiments conducted was all to find out the underlying principle behind his abilities. If he could figure out the gist of his abilities and the scientific theories behind it, he should be able to incorporate specific, targeted methods to make something out of them.

chapters.title: Chapter 161: Electromagnetic Force
chapters.body:
Chapter 161: Electromagnetic Force

Chapter 161: Electromagnetic Force

Just as Chen Chen was about to pack up the weights, he noticed something.

There were some fine specks of dust attached to the weights.

These specks of dust were fine powder from when Chen Chen chipped the crystalline to test its substance. These fine fragments attached themselves to the weight as if some unknown force pasted them there directly.

Static electricity?

Chen Chen raised an eyebrow.

He took out a weight from the briefcase again and brushed it against the table. Nothing happened.

Logically speaking, this was a perfectly sensible observation considering the weights were made of stainless steel with high nickel content. The maximum magnetic susceptibility was only 0.25, designed specifically so that it should not have its precise weight affected under any circumstances.

However, after being altered by his superpowers, these non-magnetic weights were ever so slightly magnetized.

Chen Chen attempted to peel off the crumbs and tested with the magnetized weights again. As expected, the crumbs stuck themselves to the weight.

What was the reason behind these weights becoming magnetized? Was it caused by a magnetic field? Or the electric field?

Or was it...

An idea suddenly occurred to Chen Chen.

Chen Chen pointed the radiation detector at himself and began trying to discharge the energy filaments from his mind.

Even though he might have already overexerted his energy today, it should still be fine to discharge a tiny amount. After all, the protagonists in the movie seemed to be doing fine even after bleeding out a river worth of blood from their noses.

Bearing that in mind, Chen Chen clicked the button on the detector.

“Beep!”

Following the resulting signal prompt, Chen Chen quickly retracted his power and took a look at the detector. Just as he had suspected, the detector displayed results in both the electric field and magnetic field criteria.

Based on Chen Chen’s understanding of physics, electric fields referred to the static electricity generated by a static charge. Since there was no such thing as an idle static charge, magnetic fields were present as long as there was a moving electric field. Thus, electric fields and magnetic fields were two co-existing entities. Whenever there was an electric field, a magnetic field would be subsequently generated, and vice versa.

“In other words, this power of mine is actually a form of electromagnetic force?”

As Chen Chen read the numbers on the display, he began further diving into the subject.

If that was the case, Chen Chen began to get a vague idea of how his ability operated. However, the specifics would still need to be verified.

With this facet in mind, he took out a draft paper and jotted down several strings of formula:

Coulomb’s law formula: [F=(k·q1q2)/r2]

Uniform electric field formula: [W＝F·s·cosθ]

Electrostatic field formula: [W=qU]

Afterward, Chen Chen brought Coulomb’s law into the two latter formulas and then calculated them based on the electric field strength of the earth’s surface set at 100V/m. The results of his finding were that the formula for uniformly strong electric field did not conform to his results from his previous observation which was 0.36N. Instead, it was closer to the electrostatic field formula.

Was this indication that his control of the weights and marker pen was done using electrostatic field manipulation?

An alternative way to describe it would be electric charge manipulation.

This was not an uncommon application in real life.

Back in the early sixteenth-century, humans already discovered that spiders possessed the ability to fly.

Along some coastal regions, spiders were able to glide across the ocean up to hundreds of kilometers. Back then, people believed that it was simply caused by the extremely lightweight spiders being blown by the wind, but the truth was not as it seemed.

Even without any wind blowing, the spiders were still able to take flight. Many people had even noticed spiders gliding four to five kilometers overhead.

Unlike the case with other creatures, this variation of flight was not enabled by the propulsion force generated by the flapping of wings. It was another form of energy – electrostatic field.

The gist of it was that spiders had an organ known as a trichophyta that emulated a reaction with the electromagnetic field of the earth.

It was similar to how one could make the ends of their hair stand up by rubbing a balloon against their scalp. This phenomenon was known as an electrostatic force. The trichophyta in spiders enabled them to float along the electrostatic field on earth.

To summarize Chen Chen’s ability in a simplified way, he was creating a highly concentrated localized electric field around a targeted object, thus simulating a thrusting effect. This was how the weight came to be magnetized.

Chen Chen himself was not even aware that he was actively channeling a localized electric field. This observation was similar to the way that humans were naturally born with the need for breathing but very few people were aware of how they were able to perform the act of breathing.

It was the same for the protagonist in the movie. They simply recognized that they were able to move objects without ever questioning the underlying principles behind it.

Following this process of thought, if Chen Chen’s ability truly stemmed from manipulation of electric and magnetic fields, chances were likely that he could go one step further. For example, the manipulation of electric current, perhaps even electromagnetic waves or better yet, light.

Chen Chen recalled that in the first Chronicle, the protagonist Andrew controlled a bolt of lightning amid the thunderstorm to strike and kill the protagonist Steve with it.

At the time, Chen Chen assumed that it was merely a coincidence. Looking back at it now, everything clicked.

After all, the essence of this ability laid in the manipulation of the electromagnetic force. Similarly, the essence of electromagnetic force was the interaction between charged particles and protons, therefore, the protagonist could not have manipulated electric currents by chance.

Upon making the connection, Chen Chen turned to the laptop beside him.

If he was truly able to manipulate electric currents, he should be able to destroy the computer lying in front of him.

Upon noting this possibility, Chen Chen unplugged the laptop just to be on the safe side. After that, he began imposing the will in his mind onto the laptop lying before him.

In an instant, the display on the laptop began flickering rapidly as if it was facing some form of magnetic field disruption.

As the disruption gradually grew in severity, the laptop suddenly turned off with an abrupt “pop”.

Having witnessed this, Chen Chen rubbed the resurfacing nosebleed as he began to find himself intrigued.

Surely, it was an interference effect of the electric field.

This explained how the protagonist in the movie was able to apply the superpower on themselves to fly, form energy fields around them, and block bullets. All of these had to do with the usage of electric and magnetic forces.

When Chen Chen thought of this, he became surprisingly delighted.

All this time, he thought that the superpowers from Chronicle originated from sources such as concentration or willpower. Although abilities of that caliber would be remarkable as well, it was still comparatively weaker than electromagnetic forces.

After all, manipulation of electromagnetic force was the equivalent of manipulation over all things related to it. It was a force present in all known entities. It could be applied on the most minuscule base atomic scale as well as on a macroscopic scale on significant entities.

This was an ability that was effective down to the most microscopic scale...

It could therefore be concluded that the protagonist’s usage of electromagnetic force in the movie was still far too unrefined.

Chen Chen contemplated.

After all, if they were able to switch from macro to micro, they should be able to use electric fields to create light anywhere similar to Gandalf’s holy light technique.

At the same time, the created light could be utilized to form phantoms and holographic images, essentially casting an illusionary spell to trap someone.

Furthermore, Chen Chen could freely manipulate the chemical bond and structures of matter, making a toy out of all kinds of substances...

This was one of the four fundamental forces of the universe, the electromagnetic force.

chapters.title: Chapter 162: The Galaxy
chapters.body:
Chapter 162: The Galaxy

Chapter 162: The Galaxy

Of course, Chen Chen was still a long way from achieving that step.

Even the movie’s protagonist, Andrew, could only fly freely through the sky, move buses that weighed a few tons, and block the kinetic force of many bullets. He was still manipulating matter on a macro level. One would need to be extremely proficient to shift this power from the macro to the micro.

This was similar to the principles of global symmetry and local symmetry:

With global symmetry, it was just as it sounded. If all parts of an object could transform according to the same motion, this transformation was global.

As an example, it was like when every actor on a stage moved forward and backward in sync or they all performed the same action. To the audience, the actors were in harmony and everyone was merely a copy of one person. This would be a global transformation.

If a certain invariance could still be maintained even after a global transformation, then it would possess global symmetry.

The other thing would be local symmetry.

To make the performances individualized, the director would ask the actors to create something like a wave or the Thousand-Hand Bodhisattva[1], forming a variety of patterns that changed constantly. In that case, everyone’s actions were different, so the transformation was local.

The individuals would no longer change according to the same rule. Rather, each person of each local part would have their own unique rule.

Similarly, if a certain invariance could still be maintained after a local transformation like this, it could be said to have local symmetry.

Therefore, if a piece of work required local symmetry, it would be much more complicated than if it required global symmetry. Local transformation had stricter requirements when it came to physical laws.

Therefore, the protagonists in the movie could easily crush a car, but they could not directly break down the car on a molecular level because they had no way to shift their control over their power from a global level to a local level.

Chen Chen wondered if, with the limitations of human beings, he could attain control at the molecular level?

After some pondering, Chen Chen only felt that his head was hurting more and more. In the end, he shook his head and gave up the idea of further experimentation.

Chen Chen first turned off the experiment recorder, then left the underground laboratory and returned to the Symphony of the Seas.

...

In the next few days, Chen Chen would visit the underground laboratory for a period every day, then return to the Symphony of the Seas to rest.

To outsiders, Chen Chen was going there to supervise the project each day because the Eco Science City was about to be completed. Thus, this did not attract any unwanted attention.

Today, after a phone call with his family, Chen Chen glanced at the phone’s screen and was taken aback.

The upper right corner of the phone screen, which indicated the battery level, stated that there was only about 10% left.

However, Chen Chen clearly remembered that when he answered this call, there was still more than 20%.

This was not the first time this had happened. Something similar had happened before, so Chen Chen had changed his phone.

Even so, this problem had occurred again. In that case, it was not the phone’s problem...

Chen Chen looked down and felt around his neck. He touched something hard and round.

It was a pendant.

This pendant was the “Galaxy” that Chen Chen had extracted from the movie Men in Black.

Although Chen Chen found that it did not contain any energy after he extracted it, he thought that there might be other uses for it, so he did not throw it away.

Now, it seemed like he was right. Could it absorb electrical energy?

After some thought, Chen Chen took off the pendant and hesitantly placed it on his phone.

Instantly, the unthinkable happened. Chen Chen’s phone had 10% of the battery power left. When the pendant was placed on top, the phone immediately sent out a window prompt:

“Insufficient power.”

Before the window could end its prompt, the phone went dark and the screen had turned off.

Chen Chen quickly tried to turn the phone on again but found that he could not, no matter what.

“It could absorb electrical energy too?”

Chen Chen’s eyes lit up. He thought for a moment and turned on his computer. After that, he placed his pendant on the laptop and observed the electric meter at the door.

The rate of electrical consumption was higher than before on the meter, but this rate made Chen Chen frown.

This rate of absorption was truly below par, even though it was better than the USB drive.

After all, the USB drive could not actively absorb energy at all. It could only do so passively. Therefore, Chen Chen had to connect it through a massive conductor each time. Otherwise, the immense current would wreck most conductors.

However, this pendant was different. It just had to be near the phone or laptop to be able to absorb some of the electrical energy of the device.

Chen Chen just did not know whether its efficiency could be increased, to the point where even the USB drive would not be rejected?

Furthermore, though energy could be stored within, how should it be extracted?

Chen Chen looked down again toward the interior of the pendant. There was only a dark red star glimmering faintly within. Other than that, it was pitch black.

Chen Chen tried putting the USB drive and the pendant together but nothing happened. The USB drive could not get any energy from the pendant and the pendant could not absorb the USB drive’s energy either.

The energy within the pendant would have to be retrieved through some other method.

Therefore, Chen Chen went to the electrical power control room and used several tools such as wires and plugs, to create a holder for the pendant.

Chen Chen immediately installed the pendant in the holder and connected the other end to an electronic device.

Nonetheless, the device was not supplied with any power.

This meant that conventional methods could not activate the energy within the pendant.

Erm...

Chen Chen thought about it and suddenly had a flash of inspiration. He said at once, “Little X, tell Cheng Cao to get a sniper rifle, drive here to the Symphony of the Seas, and wait for me.”

With that, Chen Chen put away the pendant, turned, and left the power control room.

Half an hour later, Cheng Cao was driving Chen Chen in a Jeep Wrangler to a deserted patch of wilderness.

“Your mission is simple — shoot this pendant from 1000 meters away.”

Although Cheng Cao was mystified, he nodded without hesitation.

Chen Chen dangled the pendant before Cheng Cao’s eyes, then hung it upon a dead tree trunk. Following that, both of them got into the car and drove off for a thousand meters.

Chen Chen recalled that when he first extracted this item, it had not consumed much energy. After all, the pendant did not have much mass. According to the energy conversion formula of extracting items from the USB drive, this pendant weighed no more than twenty grams, which took a total of twenty-five thousand kWh of electricity to extract.

This was the same as 90,000,000,000 joules. With something like TNT, each kilogram could produce 4.2 million joules of heat. In other words, the pendant contained energy equivalent to approximately 21.4 tons of TNT.

Even if all of this energy was released at once, it would only spread to a range of about five hundred meters.

The way by which the Galaxy absorbed energy was through the simplest contact.

If one wished to charge it, it was even simpler. Use copper or silver to construct a hollow metal ball with a diameter of twenty to thirty centimeters. While it was connected to a power source, place the pendant into the metal ball and insulate the outside.

In this manner, all the electrical energy would be absorbed by the pendant.

However, it was not enough that it could absorb electricity. As a tool for storing energy, it must be able to release the said energy.

Now, Chen Chen was trying to figure out how to release this energy...

[1] it’s a dance piece directed by Zhang Jigang.

chapters.title: Chapter 163: Engine
chapters.body:
Chapter 163: Engine

Chapter 163: Engine

The two of them stood atop a sand dune a thousand meters away. Cheng Cao was holding an AWP sniper rifle and he aimed at a dead tree trunk a kilometer away.

“There’s a full-speed southeast wind, wind force level 3, wind velocity at 6.5 meters per second, minute of angle is 7.”

Beside Cheng Cao, Chen Chen reminded him. “After firing, don’t look to see if you’ve made your shot. Turn your back and hide behind the sand dune at once. Got it?”

“Alright.”

Although he did not quite understand Chen Chen’s intentions, Cheng Cao still nodded without any comment.

Chen Chen put on a pair of sunglasses and only his head popped up from behind the sand dune. He prepared to duck. “Okay, you may fire!”

Chen Chen’s behavior alarmed Cheng Cao a little. He knelt on top of the sand dune and aimed steadily at the pedant a thousand meters away.

“Bang!”

After the gunshot, Cheng Cao did not even look. He immediately turned around and jumped off the sand dune. Copying Chen Chen, he got down and put his hands over his head.

Both of them stared at each other without blinking and waited for ten seconds.

Erm...

Nothing happened.

Cheng Cao gave a sheepish grin. Then he returned to the sand dune and made another shot.

“Bang!”

There was another gunshot.

To prevent himself from being blinded, Cheng Cao turned away and glanced at the target out of the corner of his eyes. Seeing that the shot did not land, he continued firing another round...

“Bang!”

“Bang!”

“Bang!”

...

In this manner, Cheng Cao kept at it for five minutes. He had used up three magazines’ worth of bullets. Just when he was feeling somewhat numb, a white light flashed before his eyes.

Instantly, Cheng Cao understood something. In pure instinct, he turned his head and leaped off the sand dune!

Behind the sand dune, Chen Chen also sensed a flash before his eyes. The sky suddenly became much brighter. Three seconds later, there was a deafening sound!

“Boom!”

Along with this deafening explosion, Chen Chen could feel a shockwave that was dying out. The gigantic impact flung the sand off the top of the dune!

That was a shockwave formed after a powerful explosive was triggered!

All at once, both of them were showered and nearly buried in yellow sand.

The shockwave came and went quickly. After a few seconds, Chen Chen raised his hand from the sand, took off his glasses, and shook his head vigorously. After that, he cleared out his ears while forcing a yawn.

The thunderous sound had caused both of them to experience tinnitus.

Not only that but when Chen Chen looked up to the sky, he also noticed that there was a neat circular pattern above them. Wisps of clouds were gathered into a large circle with an empty center. It was truly eye-catching.

This power...

Chen Chen felt quite disturbed. This item should not be used as an energy storage device, but as a bomb. He had hung this bomb around his neck for half a year.

After clambering over the sand dune, Chen Chen saw that the dead tree upon which the “Galaxy” had hung was gone. Not only had the tree disappeared, but even the ground had also vanished.

There was only a huge crater with a depth of dozens of meters and a radius of a hundred meters on the land.

“Is it just about a hundred meters?”

Upon seeing this, Chen Chen could not help but frown.

The width of the crater was only one-fifth of the expected range. In that case, a sniper bullet would only trigger the “Galaxy pendant” to release energy akin to 4 to 5 tons of TNT.

This was about one-fifth of the energy it contained.

The AWP used a 12.7×99mm rifle bullet. The known mass of the warhead was 46.01 grams, the initial velocity was 850m per second, and the final velocity was 0. The impact time of the warhead hitting the “Galaxy” was about 0.01 seconds. According to the momentum theorem, one should be able to work out that (N-mg)t = 0-(-mv), N = mg+mv/t.

In other words, when the bullet hit the “Galaxy pendant”, the force acting on it was about 4000N. If one divided that by the contact area, one could easily calculate the pressure.

This was the force and pressure that triggered the power akin to four tons of TNT explosive within the galaxy pendant.

Hmm, what an empirical display.

Having thought of this, Chen Chen walked toward the crater and finally located the undamaged Galaxy pendant there.

Even if the energy was released, it remained unharmed. Although Chen Chen did not know how the aliens in the movie had activated the energy within, it was clear that impact excitation was also a method of activation.

Now that he had figured this out, Chen Chen knew what he had to do next.

He just had to find someone who could make an electrical device that could charge the Galaxy pendant so he could charge it 24 hours a day. If he ever had to go off and take the USB drive with him, he could replace the USB drive with the Galaxy pendant so that no electricity would be wasted.

As for releasing the energy that had been absorbed, it was very simple.

Chen Chen only had to use a car engine as a reference. First, he would install and affix the Galaxy pendant in the combustion chamber. After that, when the machine started, the piston would automatically hit the pendant.

Each force of impact would release some of the energy. This energy would push the piston back. At the same time, the connecting rod of the piston, due to inertia, would produce the force of the next impact...

Creating an endless loop.

The only difference between this kind of engine and the car engine was that fuel excitation was replaced by the way the piston would strike the pendant to excite energy. Thus, the need for fuel was eliminated.

By adjusting the force of the piston hitting the pendant, it was also possible to control the amount of energy released by the pendant.

Not only that but Chen Chen also thought of another use for the Galaxy pendant.

If only a small amount of energy was stored in the pendant and it was then installed upon a detonating device, would it not mean that only a tiny amount of explosive could be used to detonate all the energy within the pendant?

This was undoubtedly a super-powerful bomb. Just like before, with a force of only 4000N, it could attain ten times the power of a Tomahawk missile. It was truly a daily essential for killing people, whether one was at home or traveling.

Most crucially, this item weighed only 20 grams, so Chen Chen could extract many of them as backup...

With this in mind, Chen Chen swiftly returned to the research center and began to sketch the engine design.

Furthermore, to be able to activate as much energy as possible at one time, Chen Chen designed the engine with a multi-cylinder form. Each cylinder was two meters in diameter and the fuel chamber was replaced with an impact chamber. At the same time, Chen Chen eliminated the air intake and fuel injection ports, and even the spark plug was completely abandoned.

After finishing the design illustrations, Chen Chen called Qian Wenhuan, asking him to contact those in Germany and find out which engine manufacturer could produce the engine he had designed.

Chen Chen was not worried about this being leaked to others. He could just pretend that this was a handicraft, a hobby. After all, from their perspective, this kind of engine was simply something they had not seen before, something without any function.

They did not know about the existence of the Galaxy pendant, let alone imagine that there was a substance in this world that could release energy through impact. The key point was that this substance would not be damaged even as the energy was activated.

chapters.title: Chapter 164: In The Eye Of The Storm
chapters.body:
Chapter 164: In The Eye Of The Storm

Chapter 164: In the Eye of the Storm

As the Eco Science City got closer to its completion date, this newly built town was also getting more tourists who came here to visit.

Since the construction of the Eco Science City began, it had practically become a local attraction for Namibia. After all, these towering buildings had been erected in a wasteland. In the eyes of a bunch of idle natives, this was the mighty power of civilization.

They praised this great power and they always went to the construction team to ask for water from the well. Every day, before daylight, some villagers from nearby villages would even earnestly haul up water from the wells created by the construction team.

Finally, in desperation, the research center had to apply for assistance from the military before they managed to drive away these natives.

Even so, in this sense, the lack of manpower in Blacklight Biotechnology became evident.

As Chen Chen’s territory expanded, he was stretched for manpower, whether it was the scientific research personnel or the technical personnel or even the guards in the Black Security Service force. A new round of talent recruitment was urgently needed.

Meanwhile, as the precedents from the eleven Disciple families also served to create buzz for his Eco Science City, Chen Chen chose to announce the “age-reversal treatment” in the form of a paper in the journal, Cell.

The core of this technology was to cultivate the patients’ cloned embryos, from which embryonic stem cells would be extracted. Therefore, after the announcement, the entire academic community was in a complete uproar!

Following that, this information quickly went viral. Numerous news outlets and even independent media, like flies that had caught a stench, all published the results of Chen Chen’s paper on the Internet.

“Journal Cell Publishes Amazing Research Results, Human Life Expectancy Is Expected to Reach New Limits! — Old Wave News.”

“Can Human Aging Be Reversed? Scientists Have a Shocking Answer — TX News.”

“What Is a Cloned Embryo? What Does It Have to Do With Cloned Humans? Is It Illegal to Study Cloned Embryos? — Search Bear Express.”

All at once, the words “age-reversal treatment” were everywhere online all over the world.

On one hand, many marveled at the progress of science and the fact that even aging could be treated now. On the other hand, countless people immediately leaped up angrily and began to raise their banners to oppose Blacklight Biotechnology.

Soon, the Internet was full of comments that lambasted Blacklight Biotechnology.

This was why Chen Chen previously had reservations. Many people thought that an embryo was a new life and killing embryos to extract embryonic stem cells was a kind of murder, which should be strongly rejected.

Of course, if there were detractors, there would naturally be supporters too. Some people thought that this was a price that was not even worth mentioning when compared to increasing human lifespan.

For instance, on a certain technological forum currently, the debate between both sides was in full swing in a post titled [Where did a third-rate company like Blacklight Biotechnology even come from]:

LunarSirius: [What right does Blacklight Biotechnology have to take away the lives of those cloned embryos? I seem to recall that China has forbidden human cloning over and over? Why is this company of theirs not punished?]

LuyaoZou: [OP, are you an idiot? Yes, the company is a China company, but they’ve even built a city in another country, who even gives a f*ck about that law?]

JoyLovesGrayWolf: [It’s bad enough that the OP is dumb, but the reply is just as stupid. The Earth Federation and every continent oppose all forms of human cloning, yes, but cloned embryos aren’t included in this definition. Which continent or country in this world has not researched cloned embryos? If researching cloned embryos is a crime, then the whole world is guilty.]
