Poison: [Lol, so you can harm a life just like that? Embryos aren’t human? In that case, dear poster, didn’t you start out as an embryo? Or you jumped out from a crack in a rock?]

Childheart: [dude you’re a troll right]

...

These discussions either started out on an extreme note, after which both parties started to attack each other’s parents or they began as a friendly debate which gradually turned violent, finally devolving into comments that revolved around genitals and parents, all in the spirit of cheerful academic discourse.

In short, the “age-reversal treatment” had taken over every screen across the entire Earth Federation. Before the situation cleared up, some authoritative agencies would not even deign to come to a conclusion.

Nonetheless, Chen Chen was no longer the simpleton he once was. If he had publicized this technology one year ago, he would have been crushed to death by international public opinion. However, by now, these criticisms and verbal abuses had no effect on him.

This was because he had capital backing.

Not only that but as Chen Chen kept publishing papers in major journals one after another, his reputation gradually improved accordingly. Although he was not at the level of those academic luminaries, he was at least considered to have some clout.

When people in academia mention Chen Chen’s name, they could immediately associate him with results such as “Alzheimer’s disease treatment” and “age-reversal treatment”.

This was a form of recognition by the academic world.

Before the public, Chen Chen had deliberately downplayed things and even allowed Little X to manipulate online public opinion so that his name appeared in the public eye as little as possible. Nonetheless, the names, “Blacklight Biotechnology” and “Eco Science City”, had become known to everyone.

Even so, with the announcement of these technologies, the principles behind the technologies also became fully known to the public. After all, the technology of the “age-reversal treatment” was not complicated. It was simply using embryonic stem cells to transform the hematopoietic stem cells, then rebuild the human immune system.

Although there were difficulties with this technology, they were not impossible to overcome. In Chen Chen’s view, as long as he had publicized the technology, the world would be able to develop a mature version of the technology within a few years even without the core data.

In other words, the world would have been able to develop this technology sooner or later, even without Chen Chen. He merely advanced it by a few years.

Still, for the time being, the Eco Science City was the only place in the entire Earth Federation that could utilize this technology. This was an intangible advantage. Once the Eco Science City was launched, the money would come rolling in at once.

Of course, when there was profit, there would also be more conflicts.

In North America and Europe, the eleven Disciple families served as his sentries. Coupled with the Blanc family incident some time ago that acted as a deterrent, Chen Chen could hold down the fort. Now he was just afraid that someone from Mainland China would try to trip him up.

Therefore, Chen Chen headed to the Embassy of Mainland China again and sold the core data of the “age-reversal treatment” to the authorities of China.

Though it was called a sale, naturally, it was more like a gift. At this point, some of the researchers in this country could use this technology to extend their lives. Chen Chen had only one condition, which was, the technology must not be developed for commercial use for three years.

By now, Chen Chen had everything ready and was only lacking manpower.

However, after his rise in fame, things like manpower came to him in a steady stream. Chen Chen no longer had to employ threats and rewards as he did before. He only had to toss out hundreds of invitations and more than one-third of these researchers would gladly accept this olive branch and come to Namibia.

chapters.title: Chapter 165: Poker Cards
chapters.body:
Chapter 165: Poker Cards

Chapter 165: Poker Cards

Two months later, Chen Chen stood in the middle of an empty metal room. There was a white card before him that fluttered up and down in his vision.

It was a poker card.

If someone came across this sight, they would be shocked to notice that the card was fully suspended in the air.

Yes, fully suspended.

It was not only suspended. If one got closer, one would find that the poker card was trembling slightly as if an invisible hand was holding it and the tremor was just a natural response of the human body.

Meanwhile, as Chen Chen moved his gaze to the right, the poker card also moved to his right. When Chen Chen turned to look at his left, the poker card swooped to the left too.

Following that, Chen Chen turned around in a circle. The poker card maintained a distance of thirty centimeters in front of Chen Chen the entire time like it was Chen Chen’s shadow.

Upon observing this, Chen Chen suddenly closed his eyes.

Nonetheless, even after his eyes were shut, the card still hovered before him. Then, in accordance with Chen Chen’s thoughts, the poker card started to twirl around Chen Chen.

It was like a dancing butterfly.

Finally, Chen Chen prodded with his mind. From a table beside him, three or four poker cards flew into the air and revolved ceaselessly around Chen Chen. Every movement of each card was identical.

“My current limit is controlling four poker cards at the same time…”

Chen Chen mumbled with his eyes shut. After that, the four poker cards began to move on different trajectories, but this only lasted for a brief moment.

In the next second, he suddenly lost control over the four cards which scattered onto the ground.

“Trying to focus on four things at once is still too much…”

Chen Chen shook his head, then opened his eyes.

Unlike the protagonist Andrew in the movie Chronicle, Chen Chen had specially designed several training courses for himself. For example, the current training with poker cards was a way to improve the accuracy of his control.

By controlling the poker cards, he could increase his brain’s control over the electromagnetic force.

Besides, Chen Chen also had corresponding strength training and endurance training. After all, as the movie mentioned, this ability could be enhanced through exercise, just like a muscle.

Therefore, Chen Chen certainly hoped that this ability could be fully developed in terms of speed, strength, and endurance.

At the present, Chen Chen’s utilization of electromagnetic force was mainly in the form of field energy, so he decided he might as well name his current power as — Field.

Chen Chen stretched out a hand and turned his palm flat up.

At once, four poker cards flew up in succession and landed into Chen Chen’s palm one by one.

As he looked at the messy poker cards in his hand, Chen Chen shook his head helplessly. Compared to Andrew in the movie who casually put away an entire deck of scattered cards, his own control was inadequate.

Chen Chen was suddenly struck by another thought. A tin box of chewing gum, as thick as a finger, flew out from his pocket by itself. After that, it opened with a click and a crystal clear pill floated up.

Chen Chen opened his mouth and the pill flew smoothly into his mouth. At the same time, the box containing the pills snapped shut and returned to Chen Chen’s pocket.

He closed his eyes lightly. After around thirty seconds, the corners of his mouth curled up and he gently put out his hand once again.

Suddenly, what was left of the fifty-four poker cards on the table flew up one after another, spun once in mid-air, then dropped lightly into Chen Chen’s hands.

One, two, three…

Ten, twenty…

They moved faster and faster. At first, it was just one card after another. By the end, all the poker cards were simply connected in one long chain, tracing a unified arc in the air before landing into Chen Chen’s hands like birds returning to their nest.

“Phew!”

Chen Chen exhaled loudly and opened his eyes again.

At the moment, the deck of cards was tidily stacked in Chen Chen’s palm. It was a world of difference from the disordered attempt from before.

“As I thought, after taking NZT-48, even my control over ‘Field’ increases by more than ten times…”

As Chen Chen murmured, he spread out both his hands. Instantly, the fifty-four poker cards formed a neat circle that floated and revolved around Chen Chen continuously.

Chen Chen suddenly exerted some force.

There was a flash of white light. The trump card of these fifty-four poker cards abruptly raced out, spinning over a distance of five, six meters, before it slowly drifted to the ground.

Following that, the rest of the cards flew out one after another. The furthest of them reached seven or eight meters and even the nearest ones covered four to five meters.

Chen Chen turned to the metal wall and sent these cards into the wall. They thudded loudly as they hit their mark.

After this, Chen Chen wore a pensive look.

“Little X.”

Chen Chen suddenly spoke and looked at the monitor in the corner, “Send Qian Wenhuan a message — ask him to cut the imported graphene oxide from the research center into the size of playing cards and send it to me.”

“Okay!”

As Little X replied, Chen Chen turned on his laptop and continued extracting crystalline from the USB drive.

After two months of cultivation, Chen Chen felt that his body had recovered to its initial state and could continue to endure the radiation of the crystalline.

One, two, three pieces…

This time, Chen Chen had absorbed twenty-two pieces of crystalline in one go, reserving only 50 kilograms worth of energy within the USB drive.

When the last of the crystalline had been absorbed, Chen Chen wiped away the blood from his nose and stretched himself. His whole body made cracking noises.

Chen Chen could clearly sense that his “Field” had grown stronger…

Therefore, he looked at the poker cards scattered all around and beckoned gently.

Instantly, the poker cards within two meters of him flew up, whirled giddily in the air, and returned to Chen Chen’s hand in a neat curve!

Chen Chen smiled. He shut his eyes matter-of-factly and continued stepping forward.

As he moved forward, his hand was like a vacuum cleaner that constantly sucked in the scattered poker cards, which returned to his palm like sheep returning to their flock.

Right, the first time he had only absorbed nine crystalline fragments and his control over his “Field” had only extended to one meter. This time, having absorbed twenty-two pieces, his range of control over “Field” had increased from one meter to around two meters instantly.

He had just absorbed the crystalline, but already Chen Chen felt that his command over “Field” had become more assured.

With a mental prod from Chen Chen, a poker card flew out with a “whoosh”, hitting the wall ten meters away with a loud “thud”!

“Boss? It’s me, Wenhuan.”

At this point, Qian Wenhuan’s voice came through the electronic lock.

Chen Chen turned toward the door and gestured gently with his right hand. Instantly, the entire deck of cards floated into the air, then fell gently, but amazingly enough, they landed into a tidy stack on the table behind him, not an inch out of place!

After that, Chen Chen kicked the crystalline pieces whose power had been exhausted into a corner. Only then did he snap his fingers to signal Little X to open the door.

The red light on the electronic lock turned green and Qian Wenhuan walked in with a welded iron box.

“Boss, here’s the thing you asked for.”

Qian Wenhuan glanced around curiously and noticed the bloodstains on Chen Chen’s shirt. His expression changed suddenly. “Boss, are you feeling unwell lately?”

“Unwell?”

Chen Chen cracked his neck and said softly with a smile, “I’ve never felt better.”

chapters.title: Chapter 166: Graphene Poker Cards
chapters.body:
Chapter 166: Graphene Poker Cards

Chapter 166: Graphene Poker Cards

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“Boss, I think you should do a checkup. After all, frequent nosebleeds for two months in a row may be a sign of leukemia…”

Qian Wenhuan recalled that this had happened to Chen Chen two months ago and he looked worried. “You’re only twenty-two this year. You should still have plenty of time left to squander, so don’t overwork yourself. After all, you’ve accomplished so much. If you collapse, all of this would fall into the hands of others…”

“Don’t worry. Didn’t I just get a blood test recently?”

Chen Chen waved his hand. “I’m just heaty, that’s all. It’s fine.”

“Okay…”

Seeing this, Qian Wenhuan could only nod. He then took out the iron box. “This is what you asked for.”

“Is this functionalized graphene obtained through oxidation?” Chen Chen asked.

“It seems so…”

Qian Wenhuan scratched his head. “I heard from Professor Farad that this thing is harder and tougher than ordinary graphene sheets. However, to maintain this strength and toughness, Professor Farad had to stack 10 layers of this graphene. He said that this thing is very dangerous and should be used with care.”

“10 layers of functionalized graphene, is that it?”

Chen Chen nodded and took the iron box. “Good, that should do it.”

After sending Qian Wenhuan away, Chen Chen locked the door of the laboratory again.

As the opening date of the Eco Science City approached, Qian Wenhuan barely had time for a break as he was so busy preparing for the opening ceremony. Even so, no matter what order Chen Chen gave, Qian Wenhuan would personally attend to it.

This was also a type of sincerity.

Chen Chen shook his head. It looked like he would have to reward Qian Wenhuan by promoting him whenever possible.

However, on second thought, Qian Wenhuan was already the general manager here in the African Continent. There was no way for him to rise further, so Chen Chen gave up on the idea of compensating Qian Wenhuan.

After all this, Chen Chen gently opened the iron box. At once he could see the thin, almost-transparent pieces that lay in the box.

Graphene transmitted light very well. Although many layers were stacked together now, they still allowed light to reach the bottom of the box, like glass.

Qian Wenhuan mentioned that this thing was dangerous, which was true.

After all, what was graphene? It was a layer of carbon atoms. A layer of carbon atoms was called a layer of graphene and the thickness of a single carbon atom was only 0.34 nanometers.

Currently, what Chen Chen had was a 10-layer structure of graphene, which was 3.4 nanometers thick. This was only one twenty-thousandth of the thickness of a hair.

Moreover, this graphene layer had been especially oxidized, which meant that the edges of this material were incredibly sharp.

The serrations at the edge of the graphene could easily pierce the cell membrane of humans and penetrate the human body. It was thus extremely lethal to humans and other animals.

Graphene was first separated from graphite by two physicists at the University of Manchester in Europe: Andre Geim and Konstantin Novoselov.

Due to this, both of them had won the 2010 Nobel Prize in Physics.

From then on, the physics community gained this material called graphene. It was a two-dimensional crystal, the strongest substance known to mankind, and even tougher than diamonds.

It was not only the thinnest material but also the strongest. Its ultimate tensile strength was 200 times stronger than steel.

A laboratory in North America once did an experiment. Before the graphene particles began to split, they could withstand a pressure of about 2.9 micro Newton per 100 nanometers. This was equivalent to having to apply a pressure of 55N to break a 1 meter-long graphene.

If this graphene was made into the thickness of ordinary food-packaging plastic bags, then a pressure of 20,000N would be required to tear the packaging bag.

In other words, if a packaging bag was made out of graphene, it could withstand two tons of items.

In that case, how strong was the ten-layer graphene sheet in Chen Chen’s possession?

With this in mind, Chen Chen sent out a thought. Immediately, a sheet, so thin it was almost transparent, floated up. Chen Chen observed intently and saw that it was about the size of a poker card.

Nonetheless, unlike a poker card, this sheet was practically transparent. If he was not paying extra attention, he would not have noticed it there.

Although this graphene sheet was very thin, it was very tough too, not much different from a playing card. Chen Chen thought about it, then simply picked up a book, opened it to the middle, and placed it standing on the table.

After that, Chen Chen controlled the graphene he was staring at. Similar to the poker cards, he exerted a force and the graphene sheet flew forward without a noise.

“Rip!”

There was a tearing sound that was almost inaudible. Chen Chen looked carefully, only to see that a few pages in the middle of the book had been severed in half!

Chen Chen cautiously picked the book up. Right in the center of the book, there was a clean, horizontal slit, about ten centimeters long, which extended to the back pages too.

The surface of the cut was incomparably smooth as if the book was printed like this…

This was an attack on the molecular level.

Chen Chen raised his eyes and looked past the book. He saw that there was a pen holder behind it. The thin graphene layer was embedded in the pen holder, revealing only a small section.

This power…

Chen Chen was dumbstruck. If he used this method to attack someone, what would the result be? It was likely that the person, even if they died, would not know how they died.

As it was so thin, they would not even feel any pain. At most, there would be a slight itch at their neck. They would reach out to scratch their neck and the adhesion between flesh and blood would fall apart before their head would fall off with a snap…

Chen Chen did not use his hands but continued using the power of “Field” to tug out the graphene sheet. He saw that one corner of the sheet had been completely shattered.

When he saw this, Chen Chen was able to confirm something.

This meant that, although the 10-layer graphene sheet could cut objects that had weaker tensile strength, it still could not cut harder materials.

After all, it was too thin.

Still, even though it could not cut through metal, its power was sufficient for Chen Chen’s use.

With this in mind, Chen Chen aimed his “Field” at the iron box and lifted all the graphene sheets. He counted — there were a hundred graphene “poker cards” in total.

He nodded and collected these graphene sheets again, keeping them in the box.

These graphene sheets were too deadly. If one used their hands, one would easily get injured. Plus, as they were too thin, they would break easily when force was applied. No one else besides Chen Chen could use this kind of thing as a weapon.

Just when Chen Chen had finished his experiment, Little X had a notification.

“Sir Godfather, the Meyer Shipyard in Germany has just sent a message that construction for your customized cruise ship, the Blacklight, has been completed and is ready for collection!”

chapters.title: Chapter 167: The Blacklight
chapters.body:
Chapter 167: The Blacklight

Chapter 167: The Blacklight

“Ta-da, this is your ‘Blacklight’.”

Two days later, outside the Meyer dock in Papenburg, the general manager of the Meyer Shipyard pointed to the dock in front and proclaimed loudly to Chen Chen.

Chen Chen looked at where he had pointed. A large group of people stood on the pier, looking at the dock that was the size of a hill, by the sea.

Three towing boats tugged at the towing ropes at full horsepower, pulling straight the steel cables that were as thick as arms.

The other end of the steel cables was connected to a pitch-black mammoth vessel within the dock.

Tens of thousands of people had formed a dense crowd on the pier. These were the local residents of Papenburg. With nothing better to do, they had come to watch the launching ceremony of cruise ships.

Meyer Shipyard had spread the news. Apparently, they had paid money to be a trending topic, announcing to the world that they had built the world’s largest cruise liner — the Blacklight.

Currently, as Chen Chen observed the large crowd of onlookers that had gathered on the pier, he mused to himself that it was not just Chinese people who enjoyed a spectacle. This probably applied universally to everyone in the world.

As the tugboats kept up their effort, a black behemoth slowly revealed its head. The bow of the Blacklight, which was being pulled forward by the tugboats, appeared in everyone’s visions.

There was a collective gasp from the crowd of onlookers.

Black!

This tanker had been painted black all over, which was a rare sight!

Not only that, but on both sides of the main deck, they could also see a massive logo. It was a black ball that was surrounded by red and yellow lights.

Under the contrast of the lights, the black ball looked just like a black hole…

This was the Blacklight, which could also be called the Blackwatch.

As more of the ship was revealed, its presence grew stronger and stronger. It was four hundred and four meters long, eighty meters wide, and seventy-five meters high. This gigantic ship had completely redefined the limits of cruise ships in the Federation!

Half an hour later, when the stern had broken free of the dock, the crowd erupted into an enthusiastic cheer. Chen Chen and the people from the shipyard who were standing on a platform of the dock also applauded.

“Congratulations, Mr. Chen Chen.”

The general manager of Meyer Shipyard shook hands with Chen Chen and joked. “This is the largest cruise ship in the Earth Federation to date. Even if it’s ranked among all the ships in the world, it must be in the top ten. I heard that you’ve previously rented Royal Caribbean’s cruise ship, the Symphony of the Seas. Presently, the ship you’ve ordered is much larger, whether in terms of length or width. I don’t know if you’ve hired employees — this ship can’t even move without one or two thousand employees.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Fortunately, I’ve poached the captain and the staff who worked specifically in engine navigation from the Symphony of the Seas by offering a higher salary. With them, I should be able to operate the Blacklight.”

“Then, I can be rest assured…”

The general manager of Meyer Shipyard laughed dryly.

As they were exchanging their pleasantries, the tugboats had pulled the Blacklight to moor at a port a few hundred meters away from the dock. The crew members who had been waiting a long time boarded instantly, including the white-bearded Captain Bernard of the Symphony of the Seas.

However, from the next second onward, he would be the captain of the Blackwatch.

Finally, Chen Chen and the personnel from the Meyer Shipyard followed suit and boarded the ship. A podium had already been prepared on the bow deck and all the employees on Chen Chen’s ship had lined up automatically, quietly waiting for Chen Chen’s speech.

Chen Chen had always disdained such bureaucratic speeches. After a few careless lines, he picked up a pair of scissors and quickly cut the rope on the bow, causing a suspended bottle of champagne to smash into the bow of the ship.

“Crash!”

As the sound of shattering glass rang out, everyone applauded. This was the launching ceremony of the ship. The west had always used this form of “bottle-smashing ceremony” to celebrate a ship’s official entry into the big family that was the ocean.

When this cruise ship was designed, Chen Chen had added a core power room, as was his habit. After the motors on the ship generated the electricity, it would pass through here first before being distributed to the engines and cabins.

A total of 12 Leroy-Somer LSA62 generators had been installed on the ship, providing a total power of 240,000 kilowatts, which was 2.4 times more than the Symphony of the Sea. Now that Chen Chen thought about it, this would be his largest device to generate electricity before the official operation of the nuclear power plant.

A few days later, Chen Chen had cleared the red tape of various countries. He boarded the Blacklight and left Germany directly, moving from the Heligoland Bight to the Strait of Dover, then passing through the English Channel, and finally entering the Atlantic Ocean.

The Blacklight traversed the entire Atlantic Ocean in only eight days. From Europe, it returned to Africa and arrived at Walvis Bay.

Eight days flew by quickly.

In the early morning of the ninth day, as the Blacklight berthed at Walvis Bay for the first time, Chen Chen also got off the cruise ship.

Qian Wenhuan was waiting on the dock, like clockwork. He opened the car door for Chen Chen and suddenly asked tentatively, “Boss, do you have any recent plans for this ship?”

“Not for now. I’ll have to recruit more people first,” Chen Chen responded.

“In that case, shall we hold the press conference for the Eco Science City here?”

Qian Wenhuan seemed delighted. “At the moment, newspapers all over the world are promoting the launch of the world’s largest cruise ship. With all this buzz, let’s hold the press conference on the cruise ship. Wouldn’t it better reflect the strength of our company’s technology and funding?”

“Sure. You take care of it then.”

Chen Chen nodded nonchalantly. “When do you plan to hold the press conference?”

“Ten days later, on 1 April,” Qian Wenhuan replied.

“An April Fools’ Day conference?”

Chen Chen laughed. “How interesting.”

“Will you…” Qian Wenhuan asked uncertainly.

“I won’t be joining.”

Chen Chen patted Qian Wenhuan’s shoulder and spoke in an encouraging voice, “I’ll leave it all to you then. Keep up the good work — I have faith in you.”

“…”

Cheng Cao was still playing the part of the mute driver. After he drove Chen Chen back to the research center, Chen Chen immediately went to the hidden human cloning laboratory on the 6th floor of the laboratory building.

“Boss!”

“Boss!”

In the laboratory, a group of researchers was bustling around and they immediately saluted Chen Chen when he arrived. Hannibal and Li Lei came in and greeted him too.

Chen Chen nodded, then looked at massive cylinders behind. There were over a hundred of them.

These cylinders were situated at the back of the laboratory. They were the life incubators that Chen Chen had been using.

These life incubators were much larger than the previous ones. They were about forty centimeters in diameter and two meters in height, with a body made entirely of tempered glass.

The life incubators were filled with a turbid, pale yellow liquid. Currently, a large number of bubbles kept forming in the liquid before they rose to the surface with a gurgle.

Through the yellow liquid within the glass, one could vaguely make out a figure, curled up like a fetus, floating up and down.

These figures looked like fifteen or sixteen-year-olds, but the strange thing was, their belly buttons were still attached to umbilical cords.

The ends of these umbilical cords were connected to a large number of pipes, which extended from the inside of the life incubator, out to a huge apparatus behind.

Chen Chen stared at the large number of life incubators in front of him. Some of them were cloned bodies of the members of the eleven Disciple families while the rest were clones that Chen Chen would use to conduct experiments.

Long ago, Chen Chen had gotten an idea:

To prevent these clones from thinking, they would all be injected with targeted, inhibiting serotonin to inhibit brain development. Eventually, their brains would atrophy completely, leaving behind a cavity in the brain.

Nonetheless, strangely enough, their cerebellum and brainstem would be unscathed.

In that case, Chen Chen wondered, if these clones were implanted with the God chip, would he be able to control them to act like normal people?

Therefore, Chen Chen had immediately asked Hannibal to begin a “hormone regulation experiment”. Through the regulation of hormones, they would make these clones grow into their adult forms within a year.

At this point, it appeared that the experiment had been successful.

chapters.title: Chapter 168: Black Knights
chapters.body:
Chapter 168: Black Knights

Chapter 168: Black Knights

“How is it going? Are there any hidden risks in the clones that have grown through hormone regulation?”

As Chen Chen observed the cramped, uneven rows of life incubators before him, he posed this question to Hannibal and Li Lei.

“None that we know of for now.”

Hannibal replied immediately, “After all, the human body depends on hormones for growth. We’ve just sped up the process and shortened the time from more than ten years to just one year. We just need to maintain an adequate supply of nutrition.”

On one side, Li Lei added. “Remember that we cultivated an extra clone for each of the five villagers from Gorob Village? Those five have just undergone transplant operations some time ago. We’ve performed liver transplantation on three of the cirrhotic patients and the remaining two pneumoconiosis patients also had lung transplantations. Now, they have recovered.”

Chen Chen nodded.

He had known about these operations, just that he was still in Germany when they were carried out, so he had not gotten involved.

It was worth mentioning that the thirty-three Gorob villagers still lived in the research center.

These people were Chen Chen’s first batch of subjects for experimental observation. All sorts of data still had to be extracted from them and verified, but the frequency of data extraction was not as high as before.

There was a time when this group of people still clamored to leave. Nonetheless, they had accepted the benefits that Chen Chen had offered them and they still wanted to leave? Dream on.

This group of people was proof of Chen Chen’s various experiments and even crimes. Of course, Chen Chen could not let them leave. Otherwise, the Earth Federation would be able to easily locate the proverbial smoking gun.

Thus, ultimately, Chen Chen implanted these thirty-three people with the God chip and kept them in the research center.

At the moment, Chen Chen was looking at the clones within the life incubators. After some thought, he said, “Since that’s the case, prepare for the implantation of the God chips.”

After Chen Chen gave this order, one of the clones who had shown the best growth was taken out of the life incubator and laid out on an operating table.

As for the actual operation, Chen Chen did not have to get his hands dirty. Neither did Hannibal and Li Lei. The assistants in the laboratory were good enough for that.

These assistants were all talents with potential that Chen Chen had recruited from all over the world. At the start, they would not be involved in such highly confidential projects. Chen Chen would only issue the invitation after they had been inspected by him and proved that there were no problems with their capability and temperament.

The moment they agreed to join God’s Forbidden Zone, Chen Chen would implant them with the God chip.

As a result, the people here knew about the existence of the God chip.

Currently, Chen Chen looked at the team of assistants surrounding the operating table, carrying out the work he had once done. He was overcome by a momentary wave of emotion.

More than a year ago, before he came to Namibia, he had personally performed this operation on Samuel and his daughter.

At that point, Chen Chen was still a lone wolf. God’s Forbidden Zone was just a dummy organization too. Samuel was the first person to join God’s Forbidden Zone, only he did not know it.

Today, Samuel was still loyally helping Chen Chen manage the small research institute in Switzerland. Meanwhile, his daughter had started walking again long ago and was living happily just like her peers.

As they had an abundance of manpower, the God chip was successfully implanted in the back of the clone’s neck within half an hour.

This clone was a black male youth who came from the genes of a Gorob villager. At this moment, as the chip was implanted, the black youth on the operating table immediately opened his eyes.

As they had not used any anesthetic, the young black man did not show any symptoms of anesthesia after the operation. First, he sat up in the hospital bed, but when he tried to get out of bed and stand up, he could not do it.

“This must be what happens when he tries to control his body for the first time. After all, this body has never been mobilized, so the muscles aren’t fully stretched out.”

Hannibal explained by one side.

Chen Chen simply walked over and said to the young black man on the bed, “God, can you hear me?”

The young black man nodded.

“Very good. If you can hear what I’m saying, move your left hand.”

Hearing this, the young black man remained seated on the hospital bed and slowly raised his left hand.

“Very good. Now you move your right hand and draw a circle.”

The young black man then raised his right hand and shakily drew a circle.

“Now try moving your legs.” Chen Chen continued.

The young black man immediately shook his lower legs gently.

“Well done.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Although the movements are still very rusty, at least it’s certain that the God chip can control these clones. In that case, all remaining clones should be implanted with the God chip, except for our customers’ clones.”

After some thought, Chen Chen added. “Li Lei, Hannibal, your department should also make preparations. I’m going to move your department to an experimental base that’s more hidden.”

“Another experimental base that’s even more hidden?” The two of them looked at each other in surprise.

Chen Chen nodded. “You’ve seen Resident Evil before, right? There’s an underground lab in the movie called ‘the Hive’.”

“You’re saying that we have an underground lab similar to the Hive?”

Hannibal and Li Lei looked down toward the ground subconsciously.

“That’s right. Our God’s Forbidden Zone has already built an underground laboratory.”

Chen Chen snapped his fingers. “Although it’s not as large as the Hive, it’s more concealed. A few more living facilities and it’s done.”

Next, following Chen Chen’s order, the clone research department took out all of the remaining clones and implanted God chips into them.

Little X only had to perform targeted programming once on the chips of these clones. Then, they were able to continuously train themselves like intelligent AI, improving the strength, physical fitness, and reflexes of the body.

One could imagine — a group of people who knew no feelings, no pain, not even exhaustion, could continuously strengthen themselves and constantly exceed their physical limits. As long as these people were given a few months, they could immediately become the mightiest warriors!

They did not even need to go through a learning process. As long as Little X incorporated various skills into their chips, this group of people would be like members of a holistic special force, without any weakness whatsoever!

Seeing the immense combat potential of these clones, Chen Chen decided to separate these cloned fighters from the Black Security Service and set up a new rapid reaction force instead.

After thinking about it, Chen Chen named this new unit — the “Black Knights”.

After all, this would be a force that could only exist in the dark, never seeing the light of day.

Unlike the Black Security Service which was a combat department out in plain sight, the Black Knights would be Chen Chen’s real trump card…

chapters.title: Chapter 169: Press Conference
chapters.body:
Chapter 169: Press Conference

Chapter 169: Press Conference

1 April 2023. Sunny day.

Harbor of Walvis Bay, Namibia.

At the break of dawn, before the locals had even risen for the morning, they began hearing the loud noises of a gathering crowd outside their windows.

A lively atmosphere quickly took over the coastal town. The gathering of thousands of visitors from afar at the harbor of Walvis Bay had penetrated the tranquility of the bay. They were facing the sea, waiting for the arrival of a grand cruise ship.

This group was comprised of media outlets from all across the world.

Even though Qian Wenhuan had only sent out invitations to approximately 500 media agencies, more than 3000 agencies were present at the scene. These media representatives behaved like they were fueled by pure adrenaline, paying out of their pocket to get all the way out here. When Qian Wenhuan was notified of this, he did not know whether to laugh or cry.

This was the resulting scenery: It was as if all of the media outlets across the world were gathered in the city of Namibia. Among the sea of crowd, one could easily spot internationally-renowned media bodies such as Chinese Press, Associated Press, Reuters, and Agence France-Presse.

Despite being more than two hours until the conference, there were already several thousands of news reporters gathered at the Walvis Bay harbor. The number only continued to increase as time went on.

“Choo–!”

At that moment, the sound of a bellowing whistle came from a distance. Many people turned toward the direction where it came from. They were greeted by the sight of a silhouette of a figure the size of a mountain approaching from the horizon.

“That’s the Blackwatch Cruise? It’s enormous!”

“Oh my God...”

“How is this possible, this cruise is nearly larger than an aircraft carrier...”

Many people present at the scene held their breaths subconsciously upon noting the massive scale of the gigantic cruise ship. Some media had already booted up their cameras and aimed their lenses at the towering figure.

Under the morning sunbeam, a cruise ship resembling a gigantic sea monster surrounded by darkness began approaching.

A media outlet labeled with the AP trademark occupied the very front row of the harbor. Following the signal of a director, a coolly dressed Caucasian lady put on her signature smile and appeared in front of the camera.

“Dear viewers, you’re now watching a broadcast from the Associated Press, my name is Allie, and I will be your host today. It’s the first of April, usually a light-hearted day of celebration, but a particular corporation has set their conference on this date. This company is none other than Blackwatch, the corporation behind the construction of the Eco Science City in Africa.

“Does everybody see the black ship in the distance? It’s the newly launched Blackwatch Cruise, it’s also the largest recorded cruise ship in the Earth Federation. Its body spans more than four hundred meters with a displacement reaching up to 350,000 tons. What’s even more surprising is that the cruise ship will not be opened to the public as it’s a completely private cruise ship. Today, we’ll be attending the conference held by Blackwatch Corporation on the cruise ship...”

Allie, the host of Associated Press recited her script confidently. Somewhere close by the Associated Press was the Agence France-Presse.

“Dear honored viewers, this is the Agence France-Presse report. I’m now standing at Walvis Bay, the coastal city in Namibia. As you can all see, there are flamingos everywhere around here. Matter of fact, this area is a nature reserve owned by the Namibian government, it’s illegal to hunt or kill flamingos here.

“Today, we’ll be attending the conference hosted by Blackwatch. As many of you may have heard, a company has managed to reverse the aging process of mankind and allow the elderly to restore their youth. They managed to accomplish something that has never been done before. Stay tuned with Agence France-Presse to find out if this is all hyped up or even a scam...”

Similar broadcasts were reenacted again and again at the harbor of Walvis Bay. Even nearby residents were drawn in to observe the rare spectacle. The Namibian officials even specifically dispatched a battalion of troops to serve as security forces for this event.

Very soon, the black ship began inching closer. The crowd was able to see the logo on the ship as well as a significant amount of floating ribbons tied by the side of the vessel. It was the decoration for the press conference.

At last, the black cruise ship docked itself by the harbor at the amusement of the crowd numbering in the ten thousand. A group of employees dressed in matching uniforms came down from the cruise ship and began checking entry tickets. The first ones to enter were the media personnel invited by Blacklight Biotechnology. The media bodies who invited themselves were last in line.

Qian Wenhuan set the opera house on the cruise ship as the venue for the conference.

This was an opera house that could accommodate more than two thousand people. The stands were divided into an upper and lower section, allowing it to fit nearly three thousand attendees.

Even with these seating arrangements, it was not able to fully accommodate all attendees. Apart from the invited media members, the rest of them had to stand and watch from the far back without an assigned seat.

The venue was not much different from regular conferences. There was a large platform erected in front of the audience and on the platform, there was a podium. Right behind the podium, there was a 200-inch screen with a size of 3650*2750mm set up. There was nothing else notable apart from that.

Before long, the conference officially commenced.

Qian Wenhuan stepped on the stage with several high-level executives. Since it was just a simple press conference, there was not much by way of introduction or opening remarks, it was largely left for the audience to interpret on their own.

The group gradually took their seats on the stage. Qian Wenhuan took charge of the microphone first and greeted the audience with a smile, “Ladies and gentlemen, please pardon our shortcomings as the conference was set up in a hurry. I’d also like to express my heartfelt thanks to all the journalists who have traveled from afar to attend our company’s press conference. Thank you, everybody!”

They bowed modestly on stage, their gestures were met with ceremonious applause from the audience below.

“Alright, the conference shall officially begin now.”

Qian Wenhuan switched to a solemn expression. “I believe that many of you are aware that we here at Blacklight Biotechnology Ltd are a medical enterprise dedicating our efforts to extending human lifespan and looking out for the best interest of human civilization as we know it. This is the vision of our company, as well as our ultimate end goal!

“And so, we’ve launched a treatment procedure for Alzheimer’s disease, the chronic disease of the century, successfully limiting its effects to its initial stages. The thesis was published in the world’s top scientific journal, Nature.

“In addition, we developed an aging reversal treatment program that can restore people from old age to middle age, effectively extending the human lifespan by thirty years. Similarly, we have published the thesis on this methodology in the world’s pioneering magazine, Cell.”

Qian Wenhuan proclaimed confidently, “With these two accomplishments alone, we at Blacklight Biotechnology proudly claim our place at the very forefront of the biological world. One day, we suddenly had an idea – could we build an effective system chain that effectively progresses from research and development to experimentation, to incorporating the results in the application and service field in the shortest span possible?

“With that, Eco Science City was born!”

This sparked discussions among the audience below as countless reporters began raising their hands fervently.

“There’s no need to rush. Everyone will get a chance at asking questions.”

Qian Wenhuan made a waving gesture and pointed at a reporter from Chinese Press sitting in the front row. “Mrs. Ye Zhiqiu from Chinese Press.”

“Pleasure, President Qian.”

A woman whose poise expressed gracefulness and intelligence got up and spoke politely in Chinese, “Question, what kind of service business is conducted in Eco Science City and is it any different from regular medical facilities?”

“Strictly speaking, there’s no difference.”

Qian Wenhuan considered the question and shook his head before smiling again. “But more strictly speaking, there’s a drastic difference!”

The remark sparked curious looks among the crowd.

“The reason I claimed that there was not much difference is that our Eco Science City also operates on a people-oriented principle. This is our everlasting motto.

“As for the reason I say there’s a drastic difference is because we’re simply faster. Our medical facilities are better equipped, also... Our technology is more advanced!”

At the end of Qian Wenhuan’s sentence, thunderous flashes erupted from below the stage.

Ye Zhiqiu coughed dryly and presented another question in a slightly unnatural manner. “President Qian, I can understand your latter remarks. However, could you elaborate on what you mean by being ‘simply faster’?”

“There’s a very simple explanation for what I mean by being simply faster.”

Qian Wenhuan expressed formally. “As you all know, here at Blacklight Biotechnology, we have our own research center. Plenty of groundbreaking medical advancements have been pioneered by this research center and we subsequently incorporate these technologies into clinical trials in the shortest period. After acquiring international certification, we incorporate it into the application and service field in the shortest time possible, so that our clients may access the latest medical technology available.

“This is what I mean by being ‘faster’!”

Qian Wenhuan’s formally addressed speech was met with thunderous applause from the audience. Ye Zhiqiu from the Chinese Press also nodded contently before sitting down.

“Alright, next up is Miss Allie from Associated Press...”

chapters.title: Chapter 170: Gradually Fermenting
chapters.body:
Chapter 170: Gradually Fermenting

Chapter 170: Gradually Fermenting

Apart from the opening speech, Qian Wenhuan had spent the rest of the conference answering questions presented by the audience. With the constant burst of flashing lights to boot, he felt like he was close to being blinded.

After an hour and a half had passed, Qian Wenhuan exchanged glances with the group of people beside him before finally standing up. “Alright, the press conference concludes here. Feel free to explore the Blackwatch from here on.”

After Qian Wenhuan had finished, he turned around and left.

The assistant beside him followed up to address the crowd. “Alright now, everyone please leave the venue in order. Everyone can either leave directly or head over to the main deck where we have wine, steak, and a buffet prepared. Please feel free to indulge yourselves.

“Still, I’d like to remind everyone to be mindful. Not only are there soldiers posted by the important hubs on the ship, but there’s surveillance everywhere as well. I believe everyone present is of the most cultured members of the Federation and won’t be inclined to do anything rash...

“Should it interest you, please stay with us for a few more days here. In seven days, the Blacklight Grand Hotel will be open to all fellow reporters for free. All you need to do is present your press verification at the reception. Everyone can take a tour around the newly opened city. I’m sure you’ll not be disappointed...”

With that, the press conference had concluded.

The original motive for hosting the press conference was to proclaim the official establishment of the Eco Science City. The combined influence of these media establishments naturally exceeded any amount of buzz Chen Chen could ever generate on his own.

Moreover, several districts built in Eco Science City had also been assigned to employees. Commercial lots in the city were also starting to be rented out.

Various commercial shops, tobacco and alcohol parlors, daily necessities stores, and other stores were popping up, adding a fresh breath of life to the newly constructed city.

The villa section by the sea was assigned to high-profile people like the professors. As for the Sandwich Bay which was positioned farthest, the hundreds of sanatoriums were stationed by large groups of medical professionals. This spot would soon become the most prosperous section of the Eco Science City.

Following the commencement of the conference and all of the present media outlets had published their respective articles, the topic of Eco Science City began trending and sparked a new wave of public opinion. Countless phrases such as “Age-reversal treatment” and “Blacklight Biotechnology” circulated across the Internet. Countless armchair scientists began sprouting up one after another, discussing the feasibility of the various technologies developed by Blacklight Biotechnology.

As for how these things often went, there was a crowd with opposing views. A bunch of experts and professors began coming out of the woods with claims that acts of prolonging the lifespan such as these were the equivalent of ripping souls out of the hands of the Reaper. This would sooner or later lead to chaos and destruction of mankind!

Furthermore, some believed that the use of cloned embryos to extend human life contradicted basic human ethics. They believed that this practice was utterly unacceptable and should be strictly banned.

As you may expect, these opposing forces did about as good as a cockroach could at stopping a moving vehicle. Facing a historical movement, they barely were able to generate any noise against it. For the most part, most people observed silently at a distance to see if this newly established Eco Science City was all bark and no bite or the real deal.

It was the elderly class of wealthy people who were especially intrigued by this development. In a mere few days, direct flights to Namibia became extremely difficult to attain. To get to Namibia, many people went through the effort of taking a flight to neighboring countries of Namibia and then driving to Namibia.

In just several days, tens of thousands of people had arrived at Eco Science City. Some of them stayed at the international hotels of the Eco Science City while others went straight for the Sandwich Bay area to enquire regarding the technology at the sanatorium.

Several thousands of people moved into the sanatorium over several days and began receiving the age-reversal treatment.

Regardless of the surrounding opinions and the stance taken in favor of or against it, Eco Science City gradually became known to more people. As the word-of-mouth effect continued to ferment, Eco Science City was bound to become more and more prosperous.

This time, nobody would be able to stop it.

...

At present, Cheng Cao drove Chen Chen to an office building nearby the Sun District.

After parking the car, the two of them entered an elevator in the office building. When the elevator door closed, they went down instead of up.

Any other outsiders who witnessed this scene would probably fall into a state of panic. After all, most buildings should only have one to two levels of basements and yet, the elevator had gone down beyond ten levels.

When the elevator door opened again with a “Ding”, an underground space of approximately 500 square meters came into the view of the two men.

The underground space was completely vacant save for two tramcars which looked like miniature trains that were positioned by the platform. As Chen Chen and Cheng Cao stepped out of the elevator, the elevator doors shut on their own before returning to the surface.

The platform resembled a metro platform.

Instead of having subway trains, it was just these small tramcars. They were approximately 5 meters long, 3 meters wide, and 2 meters tall. Its body was an all-alloy structure, the front of the car utilized a triangular design with a marked Blacklight company logo at its head.

The track on which the tram traveled was a one-way loop track. Two parallel tracks ran their course down to the end of the platform.

These were the tunnel trams that led to the entrance of the underground laboratory.

Although the entrance to the underground laboratory was located right under an underground parking lot in a certain district, it had long been sealed up with concrete. Alternatively, several underground tunnel trams similar to this one were placed in the area surrounding the Sun District.

When Chen Chen and Cheng Cao boarded the basic tunnel tram, it activated on its own and began accelerating toward the other end of the tunnel.

The tunnel trams were extremely fast. They arrived at their destination within several minutes.

When Chen Chen got out, what appeared before him was a checkpoint station that was nearly identical to the previous platform.

Only then did Chen Chen technically arrive at the underground section of the Sun District.

This was where the entrance to the underground laboratory elevator was located. In the future, Chen Chen would begin posting cloned security guards in this section to tighten the security measures.

The tramcar automatically screeched to a stop upon approaching the end of the line. After the two of them hopped out of the tramcar, it circled and stopped itself on the opposite track. Any personnel exiting the underground laboratory could take this tramcar to return to the previous platform.

Upon the commencement of the underground laboratory construction, the previous lift platform was replaced with a properly enclosed elevator. There were two of them installed as well. It was done this way so that there was no time wasted entering and exiting the underground laboratory.

Upon entering the elevator, Chen Chen immediately noticed that the elevator was far more pleasing to the eye than the state it was in during construction. The interior was a full metallic gray. In addition, to help alleviate boredom, there was music constantly being played in the elevator with the option to choose the music to boot.

Chen Chen simply browsed the music library and discovered that the catalog pretty much consisted of all the artists in Earth Federation. Even songs from bottom tier artists could be found. It could be noted that the person who designed this feature was extremely attentive.

After five minutes, when the elevator was drowned in silence again, the doors suddenly opened.

What was behind the door was no longer the exposed underground space as seen before. It was a gleaming glass corridor more than three meters wide.

chapters.title: Chapter 171: Reverse Engineering
chapters.body:
Chapter 171: Reverse Engineering

Chapter 171: Reverse Engineering

Upon witnessing the scenery before him, Cheng Cao, who was just about to step into the corridor, did a double-take and slowly pulled back his foot that was close to stepping out.

The corridor was around twenty meters long. Whether it was the walls on both sides, the ceiling or the flooring, they were all made of glass.

Just the glass itself was not a big deal on its own. What stood out was that right behind the glass was a bunch of squares. These squares were composed of light blue strips of light.

Countless strips of lights had illuminated the corridor brightly.

At the end of the corridor was a metal gate shut tightly.

When Cheng Cao took in the scenery before him, he felt very exposed...

“Don’t worry.”

Upon noticing Cheng Cao’s hesitation, Chen Chen stepped a foot inside and began explaining, “There’s an AI recognition system installed. While we were taking the elevator entering the underground section, the AI has already identified us.”

“Can these strips of lights also emit lasers like in the movie?”

Cheng Cao could not resist the urge to ask as he walked in and measured his surroundings.

“It’s called an AR ion laser generator. It can emit tens of thousands of degrees of plasma.”

Chen Chen ran his finger across the glass wall as he continued explaining, “The surrounding walls are made of a tempered glass known as ‘fluorescent organic polymer-capped gold’. Apart from having tougher than steel qualities, it also has beam expanding functionalities. It can emit combined lasers in any shape or form, effectively getting rid of any unauthorized presence entering the corridor without leaving an inch of a blind spot.”

“Welcome, Commander Sigma05. Sir Commander, you’re now located in the underground ‘Spire’ laboratory base code A0001.”

An icy, mechanical voice of a girl suddenly echoed across the corridor. Unlike Little X’s voice which contained hints of emotion, its voice was closer to that of a soulless robot.

Chen Chen pointed at the roof and informed Cheng Cao, “This is ‘X-111’, the artificial intelligence of the ‘Spire’, it manages this section.”

This was X-111, a copy of Little X.

Due to issues pertaining to permissions, Little X could not expose itself in lower-tier permission dimensions. Similar to the God chip, Little X had created a slightly inferior artificial intelligence copy of itself to maintain the operations of the underground laboratory.

As the two of them approached the end of the corridor, the steel gate parted on both sides with a deafening sound, revealing another gate that was rising behind the steel gate.

When the second gate opened, another spiral-shaped gate appeared. It was only after all three gates had opened successfully when the two men saw an ascending escalator behind the gates.

As a security measure, the elevator entrance to the Spire laboratory extended 50 meters downward. The Spire was surrounded by tens of meters thick reinforced concrete on all sides.

This meant that even if an invader managed to destroy the laser corridor they had just passed through, they would still have to break through a fifty-meter thick layer of reinforced concrete to break into the Spire laboratory.

After climbing to the top of the escalator and passing through so many safety installments, Chen Chen and Cheng Cao had finally arrived at the underground base.

This was the Spire laboratory sector.

A structure resembling the Mayan Pyramid stood tall in the distance. No matter how you look at it, it was a breathtaking marvel. It was surrounded by many other buildings such as dormitories, training centers, warehouses, canteens, and similar facilities.

There were plenty of decorative plantations that did not require sunlight around the surrounding empty spaces. Chen Chen specifically built a large underground garden to prevent the research staff in the Spire from developing mental illness.

The addition of light everywhere also emulated a sensation of being on the surface.

In the section of the underground base that bordered around the ocean, a hole with a diameter of around 50 meters was dug to serve as an artificial dock.

In the future, this dock would be opened like a tunnel, connecting it with the ocean. With that done, it should be able to house submarines and serve as the secondary entrance to the Spire.

There was hardly a need for concern of seawater intrusion as the tunnels connected to the ocean took on an L-shape. The idea behind it was similar to how when putting a cup upside down into a body of water, the middle space of the cup would not be filled with water due to the atmospheric pressure present.

When the water pressure of the seawater was equal to the atmospheric pressure in the air, it was impossible for the seawater to flow into the underground laboratory.

As for the original long narrow passage leading into the underground laboratory which they initially discovered, Chen Chen was going to have it sealed away for good with concrete.

At the present moment, Chen Chen could see fifty clones implanted with God chips on the training ground of the underground laboratory, drowned in sweat as they conducted their rehabilitation training.

With the God chip governing over them, their physical capacity was utilized to its maximum limit without overexerting them the slightest bit nor sparing any additional preserve. They would continue to grow stronger by the day until they reached the limits of the human body.

The only thing these fighters were missing was the fitting equipment and weapons.

Of course, the weapons referred were no ordinary weapons.

Although Chen Chen had at one point smuggled several batches of arms from Russian arms dealers, how did these weapons compare with the troops of the Earth Federation?

In the future when these black knights embark on their mission and face encounters with various other continents, the excellence of the cloned fighters would not make up for the gap in weaponry. After all, the age where war relied on manpower was long past.

Therefore, Chen Chen had required a great deal of non-standard weapons such as the GS combat suit, PK Armor, and various equipment transcending modern technology. This was the only way to properly arm a world-leading combat force.

However, Chen Chen did not have this much energy in the USB drive.

That was why the next phase of Chen Chen’s plans was to reverse-engineer the items instantiated from the movie.

The GS combat suit was composed of nano-scale carbon fiber and polyethylene fiber. Producing the nano-scale filament alone posed a great challenge, not to mention the far more difficult task of manufacturing liquid armor and electronic spine.

When Chen Chen considered this, his mind wandered back to the material laboratory back then that performed the material analysis for the GS combat suit as well as the three scientists working there.

It would appear that now was the time to recruit a group of scientists specializing in material science. Before that, he needed to have the world’s most advanced material research facility.

While Chen Chen had this in mind, the two of them had arrived at the entrance of the Spire laboratory. He turned to Cheng Cao. “Cao, why don’t you take a break in the breakroom. I’ll call you when I’m leaving.”

Cheng Cao nodded and promptly took his leave.

Once again, Chen Chen entered the laboratory and went to the earliest constructed radiation protection laboratory on the first floor.

Chen Chen took a seat in front of the experiment table and removed the USB drive attached to the wristband.

Previously, he took four months to charge the USB drive with an enormous amount of electricity using the Symphony of the Seas. If this electricity were to be converted to energy, it would be equivalent to a nuclear bomb of 21.5 million tons.

Chen Chen did not use all of this energy to instantiate the crystalline, he only instantiated 33 of them in total. He used two of them to conduct experiments on the mice and absorbed the remaining 31 crystallines.

Calculating by the weight of 4.5 kilogram per piece, Chen Chen would have instantiated a total of 148 kilograms of items. At this time, the USB flash drive could still instantiate 52 kilograms of material.

So, what should he instantiate next?

Chen Chen thought about it for a moment before plugging the USB drive into the computer. He selected the USB drive and turned his attention to a movie in it.

Terminator: Dark Fate.

chapters.title: Chapter 172: T-800
chapters.body:
Chapter 172: T-800

Chapter 172: T-800

After some consideration, Chen Chen did not open Terminator: Dark Fate right away. Instead, he turned on the browser and downloaded the second movie of the Terminator series online.

Terminator 2: Judgement Day.

When the download was completed, Chen Chen moved the movie into the USB drive right away.

The two terminator movies merged into one movie in the USB drive immediately.

Chen Chen attempted to view the movie. The opening of the movie was a war montage of the battle between mankind and Skynet. The soaring robots up above and the machineries on land unleashed strange plasma beams, completely crushing the human rebel armies in the frontlines.

In this cruel and vicious paddle, mankind was hanging on by the edge the entire time.

As the frame switched abruptly, a T-800 that had lost one of its arms and legs as well as combat prowess could be seen laying among the rubbles, still squirming around.

As destiny would have it, this T-800 would be taken out by a resistance soldier the very next second. However, Chen Chen clicked the pause button before the soldier could make his appearance.

Chen Chen began thinking when he looked at the image on the display.

The T-800 robot was the fourth generation Skynet robot preceded by the T-1, T-400, and T-600. It was also the first infiltration-optimized combat robot mass-produced by Skynet.

The robot possessed extremely tough superalloy exoskeletons. It was the smallest of all T-series robots, standing at only 1.9 meters and weighing below 80 kilograms.

Coated with a layer of “living tissue Infiltration outer sheath” which was a layer of living tissue, it became almost indistinguishably identical to the terminator Governor Arnold played. Using these indistinguishable terminators, Skynet had infiltrated the human base camp for the very first time and massacred them all.

Needless to say, the USB drive could not instantiate the “living tissue Infiltration outer sheath”, but there were still many valuable technologies to be obtained from a T-800.

For instance, the fuel cell in the T-800 that allowed it to operate for 120 years, superalloy components, and other parts such as its processors. If Chen Chen could decipher any one of these technologies by way of reverse engineering, it should be greatly beneficial.

On top of that, Chen Chen’s decision to use the T-800 instead of the T-850, T-1000, and T-X was a wise decision.

First of all, the T-800 was quite unlike the T-850. It did not contain a hydrogen fuel cell that could trigger a nuclear-like explosion nor did it engage in self-destructive behavior.

Secondly, the T-800 was not as pesky as liquid metal robots like the T-1000 and T-X. Chen Chen believed that if he were to instantiate the latter two terminators into reality, the underground laboratory would probably be in big trouble.

As you may expect, Chen Chen had considered instantiating other robot variations such as the one in Alita before. However, there was still the underlying problem of Alita not being a complete robot as her brain was that of a human still.

Therefore, it was practically impossible to instantiate Alita.

Apart from that, Chen Chen had also considered the movie I, Robot. The movie contained a robot known as NS-5 which was quite unlike the murder machines in Terminator, they were more aptly classified as service-class robots.

Based on the movie setting, the NS-5 was 1.8 meters tall, constructed with a titanium shell, and consisted of 456 moving parts.

The NS-5 could sustain weight up to 360 kilograms and could withstand over a thousand kilograms of impact. It had 1TB of memory and was able to perform more than 6MB calculations per second, capable of conversing in 80 languages...

Chen Chen planned to instantiate the robot from both of these movies. Perhaps he could build a stronger, more intelligent war robot by combining the strengths of the two respective robots.

The problem was there was not much energy remaining in the USB drive.

Chen Chen decided to just shelf the movie I, Robot as a backup.

Just to be safe, Chen Chen did not instantiate it right away but instead lifted his head and instructed, “Little X, order the Black Knights to get their weapons and gather in my lab.”

Only several minutes after Chen Chen had issued the order, the cloned soldiers who were training on the training grounds immediately put on their bulletproof vests and headed to Chen Chen’s laboratory with their weapons in tow.

Chen Chen took a glance at them and saw the expressionless cloned soldiers identified as Black Knights. They behaved like walking corpses, perhaps because in a sense, that was what they were.

The cloned soldiers did not have a brain as they were uniformly controlled by the God chip. As a result, they wore no expressions on their faces except for a blank look.

Upon noting the arrival of the cloned soldiers, Chen Chen considered for a moment before pointing the experiment recorder at the computer display. He reached for the display with full concentration...

A T-800 weighed 80 kilograms. Since the T-800 Chen Chen was planning to instantiate was missing an arm and a leg with missing scraps in its body, there should not be much issue in the weight aspect.

Chen Chen only wondered, would the size of the computer display affect the instantiation results?

As these questions were flashed across Chen Chen’s mind, he felt a metallic surface that was cold to the touch. When Chen Chen pulled with might, a metal skull riddled with scratches appeared outside the display!

After that, Chen Chen pulled with all his strength. A T-800 in an unseemly state was pulled out of the display!

The very moment the T-800 was brought into reality, Chen Chen leaped backward in case it suddenly decided to attack him.

However, nothing happened in the end.

Upon closer inspection of the unmoving terminator robot, Chen Chen noticed that it was already shut down. It did not react to its surroundings nor was there any light in its eye sockets.

When the robots in Terminator operated, the sockets of their eyes would radiate a scarlet beam.

Chen Chen found this observation very enthralling.

In the movie, despite having one of its legs and arms cut off, it continued to struggle and fumble around. Could this mean that in the period which it was instantiated from the movie, the T-800 shut down due to some form of disruption?

Regardless, Chen Chen did not step forward. Instead, he instructed one of the Black Knight members, “Do you see its scalp? There’s a slot on the right side of its scalp that can be opened. After you open it, hand me the main control chip inside it.”

“Yes.”

It was a Caucasian cloned soldier who had received Chen Chen’s orders. Upon receiving his orders, he knelt without the slightest hesitation and opened the robot’s scalp, removing a ten centimeters long chip.

“Sir Commander, I’ve completed my task.”

The cloned soldier got up and handed the chip to Chen Chen before returning to its ranks.

Chen Chen inspected the chip.

It was a rectangular chip that was ten centimeters long and five centimeters wide. Based on the movie, it should possess extremely high remote computing capabilities.

In the movie Terminator: Genesis, this chip was able to handle dimension-spanning calculations. Although it burnt up after doing it, it was still nothing short of amazing.

chapters.title: Chapter 173: Hydrogen Fuel Cell
chapters.body:
Chapter 173: Hydrogen Fuel Cell

Chapter 173: Hydrogen Fuel Cell

Similar to how Chen Chen had decoded the chip from the movie Upgrade, he first created a port for him to plug the T-800 main control chip inside. Before connecting it to the computer, he poured liquid nitrogen as a coolant to cause it to malfunction before being connected with Little X.

As the chip slowly thawed out bit by bit, before it even had a conscience of what was going on, Little X began to devour it in small chunks until its conscience was wiped away completely.

As seen in the movie, Skynet was lurking everywhere. The chip in these terminators could very well be a part of Skynet. This was something Chen Chen could not afford to overlook.

“I’ve erased the T-800 program from the chip!”

As the chip completely defrosted, Little X’s response came as well. “Compared to the STEM chip, the T-800 chip’s resistance is a drop in the bucket!”

“That’s normal.”

Chen Chen nodded and answered stately, “That’s the plot of the movie as well. By enslaving these T-800, the resistance army was able to rewrite the T-800 coding and cause these robots to betray their masters and fight for the humans. As you can see, Skynet isn’t particularly adept in the security department.”

After unplugging the chip, Chen Chen handed it to a cloned soldier again and had the soldier plug it back into the T-800’s scalp.

The moment the chip was plugged in, a red beam appeared in the T-800’s eye sockets as it rebooted.

They watched as the T-800 which was missing a leg moved slightly and turned its head around to face Chen Chen with its pair of dark red eyes. At the same time, its metallic teeth moved up and down in a pattern as a bass-heavy voice came out:

“Conducting systems engine analysis...

“Left-arm engine out of service, right locomotive leg out of service. Damage to the frictionless bearing on the left leg. Damage to the hydraulic shaft on the neck region. Damage to the detachable monitor module. Damage to the inertia impact damper...

“Overall damage calculations, forty percent. Twenty-three percent battery remaining...

“Self-analysis complete.”

After completing its routine, the T-800 fixed its gleaming red eyes on Chen Chen again. “T-800 terminator reporting to Sir Commander.”

“Little X, have you found any observations worth noting from this robot?” Chen Chen asked.

“Based on the data in the database, there certainly are some notable clues!”

Little X’s prompt response came from the speakers above. “The T-800 uses tantalum alloy armor, commonly found in jet engines, nuclear reactors, and missiles. It’s made of a mixture of rhenium, tungsten, molybdenum, zirconium, and hafnium. Of course, the main component is iron.

“The motivation behind using tantalum alloy for the T-800 is because tantalum alloy can effectively resist the erosion of biological fluids. Apart from hydrofluoric acid and hot sulfuric acid, tantalum is resistant to almost all acidic substances.”

Little X continued explaining, “On top of that, tantalum alloy is also referred to as high-temperature tantalum alloy. It can resist extreme temperatures up to two thousand degrees. Especially considering that toward the end of the Terminator movie, both parties were using plasma weaponry, therefore it’s reasonable to use tantalum alloy armor for its temperature resisting qualities.”

Chen Chen nodded. “What else?”

“There’s the inertial impact damper system in its head which serves to protect the chip, similar to the cerebrospinal fluid surrounding the human brain. A more fundamental feature is the servo drive control system on the T-800. Its limbs are controlled by an axial drive motor and a clavicle trailing chain which allows it to run at extraordinary speed!

“Apart from that, there’s the application of frictionless bearing throughout the T-800’s body. This is a variant of a unique magnetic levitation technique that keeps the joints of the T-800 immune to friction damage over time. As for the interior section of the armor...”

As Little X was explaining, a slot popped out of T-800’s chest, revealing two lighter sized metal strips embedded inside it.

They watched as T-800 used its only remaining right arm to extract one of the metal strips. Little X’s explanation resumed on the speakers all the while. “The T-800’s powering system is supplied by two high-efficiency fuel cells. The fuel the batteries contain is liquid hydrogen!”

“Isn’t it the same thing as the T-850?”

Chen Chen was taken by surprise. He still remembered the scene in Terminator 3 when Arnold used a knife to slice through his body and proceeded to throw out a hydrogen battery.

Due to the damage sustained by the hydrogen battery during a previous battle, Arnold ditched the damaged battery by tossing it out the car window.

Subsequently, the battery exploded with the power of a dozen tons of TNT explosion and the range of the shock wave exceeded 100 meters!

In the ending of the movie, the T-850 also stuffed its final hydrogen battery into its mouth, annihilating the two of them together.

This was the reason Chen Chen had wisely kept his distance from the T-850 and went with the slightly inferior T-800.

After all, how was anyone supposed to keep a self-destructive soldier in line?

Yet, Little X again revealed to him that the T-800 also used a hydrogen fuel cell.

“The T-850 uses a hydrogen nuclear battery which is a different thing.”

Little X quickly followed up. “The hydrogen fuel cell the T-800 uses is a regular fuel cell and isn’t prone to explosions. Hydrogen fuel cells are present in real life as well. They’re inherently a form of power generation device. They’re quite unlike regular batteries that can be discarded after being exhausted or rechargeable batteries that need to be recharged after being expended. As its name suggests, it’s a battery that continues to supply power by adding fuel to it!

“However, the actual application of fuel batteries in real life barely reaches a thermal efficiency rating of 50%. Although it’s better than internal combustion engines that operate at 45% efficiency, it’s nowhere near perfect yet. The thermal efficiency of the T-800 hydrogen battery can reach 95%.”

Chen Chen raised an eyebrow. He was not unaware of the implications of thermal efficiency of up to 95%. If this variant of battery could be mass-produced, it could very well historically revolutionize the energy industry.

“Also, the T-800 has a built-in extractor that can continuously extract the hydrogen in the air to power its hydrogen fuel cell. This is the reason behind the T-800 robot being able to function up to hundreds of years!”

“I see!”

Chen Chen snapped his fingers and reflected on the revelation. “I never expected so many good things to come out of just one T-800. This does make me look forward to establishing my industrial system.”

All this time, Chen Chen had been paving the foundation for his “Mankind Anti-aging Project”. Everything he did, he did for the succession of the project.

Now, the “Mankind Anti-aging Project” was beginning to move on track. Chen Chen was also finding more methods to further attain energy at ever-increasing efficiency. Even the construction of the nuclear power plant was a short way away now.

This was to say that Chen Chen had already achieved the goal he had initially charted. It was time to step into the second phase.

Chen Chen’s initial goal could be defined as the process of seeking energy supply and establishing his position in the world. In that case, his next goal should be further developed to gradually shift his influence on a global scale, extending his reach over all the corners of the world...

Chen Chen was still a long way away from achieving this step.

chapters.title: Chapter 174: Self-Invited
chapters.body:
Chapter 174: Self-Invited

Chapter 174: Self-Invited

In the early hours of one Sunday, while Samuel was still under the sweet spell of sleep, he was suddenly rudely awoken.

It was accompanied by the pestering voice of a young girl. “Dad, Dad, get up already, Dad!”

A frown came upon Samuel’s face. He was about to explode in a fit of rage but halted himself when the words approached his lips. He opened his drowsy eyes and sat up hesitantly. “Annie, let Dad sleep a while longer.”

“No. Did you forget, Dad? Today is the opening day of August’s concert!”

A girl with a pretty face and several spots of freckles on her face chirped about endlessly like a little lark. “Dad, didn’t you promise me last week that you were going to go to August’s concert with me?”

“Yeah, yeah, Dad got it...”

Samuel then sheepishly massaged his head before he put on his socks and shoes.

“Hmm, quickly get up Dad, Aunt Linda already made breakfast!”

Only then did the little girl willingly leave Samuel’s bedroom.

“Sigh...”

Samuel was not sure how to feel when he saw his childlike girl. He always found all of his anger instantly appeased whenever he looked at his daughter’s joyous face full of happiness.
